source.subject: Tailwind Labs — hosted evaluation readiness and quickstart asks
messages:
From: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
To: Sam Park <sam@tailwind.ai>; Elena Ruiz <elena@tailwind.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Tailwind Labs — hosted evaluation readiness and quickstart asks

Hi Sam / Elena — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our side and a small set of asks so we can spin up your hosted pilot quickly: 

Summary of call:
- Primary use: customer support triage + short-form summarization in the app (text generation + embeddings for rerank).
- Expected traffic: ~120k requests/day during peak (typical prompt size ~120 tokens; final response ~40–80 tokens).
- Security: no PHI, but must persist audit logs for 90 days and keep account-level isolation.
- Timeline: pilot next week if we can provision sandbox endpoints and a short-rate window.

Immediate asks for hosted quickstart:
1) Confirm you want Hosted API (fastest path) vs Dedicated (reserved GPUs) or Private VPC. From the call it sounded like Hosted is preferred unless we need tenant isolation for compliance.
2) Can you share a short traffic week profile (CSV) with hourly buckets or an expected concurrency target? That helps determine endpoint count and rate slices.
3) Any IP allowlist or network constraints for callbacks from our sandbox?

I attached our Hosted Quickstart Checklist — it lists the sandbox endpoints we'll give you, suggested rate windows, and the minimal telemetry we collect for billing/observability. If Hosted is fine, we can create the trial account and provision two sandbox endpoints (one gen + one embeddings) and open a 48-hour ramp window for the pilot.

Next steps:
- Sam: confirm deployment preference and paste a simple hourly profile (or even an estimate like "peak 3 RPS, baseline 0.5 RPS").
- Marissa / Jordan: I looped you in for capacity planning and to prepare the quickstart bundle.

Thanks — talk soon,

--
Soojin Lee | Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
soojin@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: Redwood-Hosted-Quickstart-Checklist.pdf (8 pages)

From: Sam Park <sam@tailwind.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@tailwind.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 17:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tailwind Labs — hosted evaluation readiness and quickstart asks

Thanks Soojin — great to meet the team today. A few clarifications inline and the traffic profile attached.

We are comfortable starting with Hosted. The main constraints are: 1) audit logs retained for 90 days, 2) embeddings data stored only for 30 days (we'll be fine with that during pilot), and 3) we need token-level billing estimates before the ramp (so we can brief finance).

Attached: tailwind-rate-profile.csv (hourly buckets for a typical weekday + a weekend overlay). Top-line: peak 350 requests/hour (roughly 1.5 RPS sustained for 10:00–12:00 PT), baseline 40 req/hour overnight.

Network: no allowlist needed for sandbox — but if we go to production we'd prefer VPC for traffic isolation. For pilot, Hosted + sampling is fine.

One more ask: can Redwood provide an expected cost range for the 48-hour ramp window (ballpark $) by model family (e.g., base Llama vs quantized 8-bit variant)? That will help us pick which variant to try.

Thanks,
Sam

--
Sam Park | Head of Platforms
Tailwind Labs
sam@tailwind.ai | +1 (646) 777-4421

Attachment: tailwind-rate-profile.csv

From: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Jun 2026 08:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Tailwind Labs — hosted evaluation readiness and quickstart asks

Marissa / Jordan — forwarding Sam's profile and confirms Hosted is the path for pilot. Key points for provisioning and pre-sales collateral: 

- Traffic: peak ~1.5 RPS sustained (350/hr) — small but steady. Baseline very low.
- Data: audit logs 90 days, embeddings retention 30 days (pilot). No PHI.
- Customer wants cost bands for 48-hour ramp across model variants before we provision.

My suggestion:
- Provision two sandbox endpoints (gen + embed) with rate window: 2 RPS burst cap, 1.5 RPS sustained.
- Use a quantized 8-bit variant for the gen endpoint as default (cheaper, faster); include a second endpoint pinned to a higher-fidelity base model for A/Bing if they want quality comparison.
- Prepare a one-pager cost estimate: conservative, expected, and low scenarios for 48-hour ramp. Include token assumptions (prompt/response avg).

Can you take the lead on the cost sheet (Marissa)? Jordan, can you prepare the endpoint provisioning (sandbox) and update the quickstart bundle with rate window settings and the KV cache note? I will reply to Sam with the schedule and the ask for a 30-minute kickoff to provision tomorrow.

--
Soojin

On Mon, Jun 22, 2026 at 5:04 PM Sam Park <sam@tailwind.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Soojin — great to meet the team today....

From: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
To: Sam Park <sam@tailwind.ai>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@tailwind.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 23 Jun 2026 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tailwind Labs — hosted evaluation readiness and quickstart asks

Sam / Elena — thanks for the quick turn. We got your CSV and I've looped in ops and pricing. Proposed plan:

1) Kickoff / provisioning: 30-min call tomorrow at 11:00 AM PT to create the sandbox endpoints and validate the hourly profile live. Can you confirm?
2) Endpoints we will provision: gen-hosted-quant8 (pinned 8-bit quantized variant), gen-hosted-base (pinned base variant for quality comparison), and embeddings-hosted-default.
3) Rate window: default sustained 1.5 RPS, burst to 2.5 RPS for short spikes (we'll monitor).
4) Cost bands: Marissa will send a short cost spreadsheet by EOD showing 48-hour ramp cost in low/typical/high scenarios using the token averages you gave.

If that works we'll schedule the 30-min provisioning and I'll share the DocuSign quickstart agreement (standard trial T&C, no SLA commitment) so finance can sign-off quickly.

Confirm time and I'll lock the calendar.

Thanks,

--
Soojin Lee | Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
soojin@redwood.com

Attachment: Redwood-Hosted-Quickstart-Checklist.pdf (attached earlier)

source.subject: Vendor onboarding: PO visibility, portal handback SLA & email ingest fallback
messages:
From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the onboarding call this week. Finance asked me to send the example invoice and confirm the preferred intake for Acme moving forward:

- Do you prefer we push invoices into your portal (preferred) or email billing as a fallback?
- If email, what subject token or filename rules help automate PO linking?
- Any constraints on multi-PO invoices (we often combine 2 POs on one PDF).

I've attached a sample invoice (sample_invoice_acme.xlsx) and our ERP export sample. Happy to pilot whichever you recommend.

Best,
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics
[sample_invoice_acme.xlsx]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables: 

1) Portal (preferred):
   - Upload CSV or PDF via supplier portal. Portal will automatically match PO numbers to the ledger when the PO is present in the subject/metadata.
   - We expose an "upload confirmation" (handback) within ~1 hour for successful automated matches; full processing/GL mapping completes within 24 hours.

2) Email fallback:
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO<po-number> (e.g., "INV:PO12345"). For multi-PO, use commas: INV:PO12345,PO12346.
   - Filename convention: invoice_<supplierinvoiceid>.pdf or .csv (invoice_INV12345.pdf).
   - We can accept PDFs (preferred) and machine-readable CSVs. Avoid embedded images-only PDFs (OCR is slower).

3) Multi-PO rules:
   - If you include multiple POs on one invoice, include a short mapping CSV in the email body or as a second attachment with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_total.

I'm attaching our lightweight template (redwood_invoice_template.csv) — it's minimal but aligns with our ledger fields. We'll pilot portal next week; if you want, Monica can run a quick preflight on two sample invoices.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Procurement Partnerships | Redwood Inference
[redwood_invoice_template.csv]

From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Billing Team <billing@acmecorp.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Thanks Aditya — that helps. A couple of clarifying Qs from our side before we spin up the portal pilot: 

- Handback latency: you mention ~1 hour for automated matches — what does the handback payload include (ACK vs full line-item match)?
- If we send a single PDF that contains multiple PO sections, do you need a separate CSV mapping every time? We could also embed a short CSV in the body if the portal is unavailable.

Our AR team uses QuickBooks and prefers PDFs, but we can generate a CSV export for the portal if that speeds automated matching. Also, confirm whether we should put our internal supplier invoice id in both the filename and the PDF header to improve de-duping.

Cheers,
Sara

On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 11:05 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables...

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 11:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Adding a quick operational playbook from ops side to make this pilot low-friction: 

- Portal-first: supplier portal ingestion (CSV preferred) will provide a 1-hour ACK when invoice file lands and a processing status update (MATCHED / PENDING / ERROR). ACK includes: supplier_invoice_id, received_timestamp, matched_po_numbers (if any), and an intake_id we use for troubleshooting.

- Email fallback (only when portal inaccessible):
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai
   - Required: Subject token exactly INV:PO<po#> or INV:PO<#>,<#> for multiple (no spaces around commas).
   - Attachments: include a PDF (final invoice) and optionally a small mapping CSV (filename: invoice_mapping_<supplier_invoice_id>.csv) with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_net_amount.
   - Filename preference: invoice_<supplier_invoice_id>.pdf. We rely on filename + subject token to reduce manual triage.

- SLA & exceptions: ACK within 24 hours for email submissions (we aim for same-day), portal ACK within 1 hour for successful automated ingestion. Complex multi-PO PDFs may require manual intake — add the mapping CSV to avoid that.

I'll have Ops enable a short validation rule that rejects image-only PDFs and flags them for manual processing. Monica — can you run the two-sample preflight with Acme and confirm expected ACK payload?

—Jordan
Senior Vendor Operations | Redwood Inference

From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Billing Team <billing@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Perfect — appreciate the clarity. We'll: 

1) Pilot portal on Wed 24 Jun with two invoices (one single-PO PDF, one multi-PO PDF + mapping CSV).
2) Also send the same files to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO67890,PO67891 as a controlled fallback so you can validate email flow.
3) We'll include supplier_invoice_id in both filename and PDF header.

Attachments to expect from us for the pilot: invoice_INV12345.pdf, invoice_INV12346.pdf, invoice_mapping_INV12346.csv

Icc'ing our AR lead in case you need additional ERP exports: billing@acmecorp.com

Thanks — talk soon.
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics
[invoice_INV12345.pdf]

Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Thanks Aditya — that helps. A couple of clarifying Qs from our side before we spin up the portal pilot: 

- Handback latency: you mention ~1 hour for automated matches — what does the handback payload include (ACK vs full line-item match)?
- If we send a single PDF that contains multiple PO sections, do you need a separate CSV mapping every time? We could also embed a short CSV in the body if the portal is unavailable.

Sara

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 11:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Adding a quick operational playbook from ops side to make this pilot low-friction: 

- Portal-first: supplier portal ingestion (CSV preferred) will provide a 1-hour ACK when invoice file lands and a processing status update (MATCHED / PENDING / ERROR). ACK includes: supplier_invoice_id, received_timestamp, matched_po_numbers (if any), and an intake_id we use for troubleshooting.

- Email fallback (only when portal inaccessible):
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai
   - Required: Subject token exactly INV:PO<po#> or INV:PO<#>,<#> for multiple (no spaces around commas).
   - Attachments: include a PDF (final invoice) and optionally a small mapping CSV (filename: invoice_mapping_<supplier_invoice_id>.csv) with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_net_amount.
   - Filename preference: invoice_<supplier_invoice_id>.pdf. We rely on filename + subject token to reduce manual triage.

- SLA & exceptions: ACK within 24 hours for email submissions (we aim for same-day), portal ACK within 1 hour for successful automated ingestion. Complex multi-PO PDFs may require manual intake — add the mapping CSV to avoid that.

I'll have Ops enable a short validation rule that rejects image-only PDFs and flags them for manual processing. Monica — can you run the two-sample preflight with Acme and confirm expected ACK payload?

—Jordan
Senior Vendor Operations | Redwood Inference

Icc'ing our AR lead in case you need additional ERP exports: billing@acmecorp.com

Thanks — talk soon.
Sara Thomas

Subject: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the onboarding call this week. Finance asked me to send the example invoice and confirm the preferred intake for Acme moving forward:

- Do you prefer we push invoices into your portal (preferred) or email billing as a fallback?
- If email, what subject token or filename rules help automate PO linking?
- Any constraints on multi-PO invoices (we often combine 2 POs on one PDF).

I've attached a sample invoice (sample_invoice_acme.xlsx) and our ERP export sample. Happy to pilot whichever you recommend.

Best,
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics
[sample_invoice_acme.xlsx]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables: 

1) Portal (preferred):
   - Upload CSV or PDF via supplier portal. Portal will automatically match PO numbers to the ledger when the PO is present in the subject/metadata.
   - We expose an "upload confirmation" (handback) within ~1 hour for successful automated matches; full processing/GL mapping completes within 24 hours.

2) Email fallback:
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO<po-number> (e.g., "INV:PO12345"). For multi-PO, use commas: INV:PO12345,PO12346.
   - Filename convention: invoice_<supplierinvoiceid>.pdf or .csv (invoice_INV12345.pdf).
   - We can accept PDFs (preferred) and machine-readable CSVs. Avoid embedded images-only PDFs (OCR is slower).

3) Multi-PO rules:
   - If you include multiple POs on one invoice, include a short mapping CSV in the email body or as a second attachment with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_total.

I'm attaching our lightweight template (redwood_invoice_template.csv) — it's minimal but aligns with our ledger fields. We'll pilot portal next week; if you want, Monica can run a quick preflight on two sample invoices.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Procurement Partnerships | Redwood Inference
[redwood_invoice_template.csv]

Sara

source.subject: Post-call: MediaStream pricing/target $/1M tokens and competitive extract
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Johnson <alex.j@mediastream.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-18T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap: MediaStream pricing / target $/1M tokens

Alex — thanks for taking the time today. Quick recap and the competitive extract you asked for. I captured the rough qualification we discussed and a recommended next-step pricing approach. See bullets and attachments.

Call highlights (quick):
- Monthly token expectation: initial pilot 5M tokens/mo, scaling to 35–45M tokens/mo within 6–9 months (customer estimate).
- Workload: mostly generation (multi-turn chat) with ~30% embeddings for search/rerank. Typical peak latency constraint: p95 <= 450ms for single-shot prompts.
- Procurement/budget range: $6k–$30k/month depending on usage and commitment; procurement indicated they need a $/1M anchor to compare.

Target unit-econ benchmarking (per your ask — $/1M tokens):
- Redwood proposal (pilot): $6.0 — $7.5 / 1M tokens (pilot pricing + limited commitment, includes prefix caching and 2x free trial credit)
- Redwood committed rate (12-mo renewal): $4.5 — $6.0 / 1M tokens (volume tiers, dedicated pools, quantized model options)
- OpenAI (public endpoint, approximate list): ~$14 / 1M tokens (general-purpose; varies by model & context length)
- Anthropic (public endpoint, approximate): ~$12—$14 / 1M tokens
- Self-hosted open-models (infra-only estimate): $3 — $8 / 1M tokens (model-dependent; hidden ops, monitoring, infra overhead not included)

Notes/context on numbers:
- Our $/1M splits assume typical prompt/response lengths from your session (avg 300 tokens per interaction). If you push longer contexts or heavy streaming, effective $/1M will change.
- The "self-hosted" low end requires aggressive quantization + spot GPU mix and assumes mature SRE and model ops — many teams underestimate ops and support costs.
- Redwood competitive position aims to sit meaningfully below the hosted incumbents on unit cost while delivering managed SLAs and less ops burden than self-hosting.

Suggested immediate next steps:
1) I attached a compact comparator (redwood_token_model_comp.xlsx) with the per-1M math and assumptions. Please confirm if we should swap the avg token size assumption.
2) I'll draft a short pilot SOW (attached draft) for a 90-day pilot at the $6.5 / 1M pilot anchor with a 6-week ramp clause and clear KPIs.
3) If procurement wants, we can run a 2-week pilot at no-cost up to 5M tokens to validate latency/quality.

Would you like me to push a formal proposal and SOW this week? If so, I can get Marissa involved for commercial terms and Jordan to scope the Dedicated pool sizing.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
---
Attachments referenced: redwood_token_model_comp.xlsx, MediaStream_SOW-draft.pdf

From: Alex Johnson <alex.j@mediastream.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@mediastream.io
Date: 2026-06-18T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: MediaStream pricing / target $/1M tokens

Ben — appreciate the quick turnaround and the transparent comparator. A few clarifying points from my side:
- Volume band looks right. We expect a conservative ramp: 5M in month 1, 20M by month 4, then 40M+ by month 9 if results hold.
- Budget note: leadership has signaled a $18k/month ceiling for Q3 pilots until they see latency/ROAS from the pilot.
- Procurement asked whether the $6.5 / 1M pilot rate is net of credits or if that includes the "2x free credit" you mentioned. Can you confirm what the invoiced rate would show?

Also, could you add a short line in the SOW about data residency (US-only) and an audit trail for inference logs for 90 days? Procurement requires explicit language on both for our initial legal review.

If you can get the proposal + SOW updated today, we can route to procurement and aim for a call with them Thursday morning.

— Alex
Alex Johnson
Head of ML Products | MediaStream

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Johnson <alex.j@mediastream.io>
Date: 2026-06-18T14:35:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Post-call recap: MediaStream pricing / target $/1M tokens — quick internal ask

Team — forwarding Alex's reply. Procurement wants the $6.5 pilot clarified (net-of-credits vs invoiced). Suggested approach:
- Show pilot commercial: list price $8.5 / 1M with an applied pilot credit that nets to $6.5 effective during the trial. That keeps the committed-rate ceiling intact for negotiation and preserves upside on conversion.
- For contract language: add a 90-day log retention clause (US-only region) and a simple extract for SOC2 scope & audit contacts. Legal will need the exact KMS/HSM wording but we can start with a stub.

Would also propose a conservative committed rate table for the SOW:
- 0–10M: $8.5 / 1M (list)
- 10–50M: $5.5 / 1M (committed tier)
- 50M+: $4.5 / 1M (enterprise commitment)

Marissa — can you draft the commercial terms to reflect the pilot credit structure + an express US-only data residency clause? Jordan — quick check on Dedicated pool sizing: assume baseline q8 quantized variant + 2x burst nodes for peak — does that match the 40M/mo target? If not, flag changes.

I'll incorporate those items into the SOW and send back to Alex and procurement.

Thanks — Ben

From: Alex Johnson <alex.j@mediastream.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, procurement@mediastream.io
Date: 2026-06-18T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: MediaStream pricing / target $/1M tokens

Thanks — that structure sounds fine. Procurement confirmed they want the SOW + a short one-page pricing annex that shows both the list and effective pilot net rate (so our finance team can see the discount mechanics). Could you get that to us by EOD today? If yes, procurement will schedule a short call Thursday 9:00am PT to walk contract items.

Also: one sticky point — leadership asked for a quick comparison vs "self-hosted open model" run by our infra team. They want to see the hidden ops risks and a TCO note. If you could add a single-paragraph TCO note to the Drive comparator that highlights ops/monitoring/latency risk assumptions, that would be helpful.

I'll expect the SOW + pricing annex and updated comparator (drive link) by EOD.

— Alex

- 0–10M: $8.5 / 1M (list)
- 10–50M: $5.5 / 1M (committed tier)
- 50M+: $4.5 / 1M (enterprise commitment)

Marissa — can you draft the commercial terms to reflect the pilot credit structure + an express US-only data residency clause? Jordan — quick check on Dedicated pool sizing: assume baseline q8 quantized variant + 2x burst nodes for peak — does that match the 40M/mo target? If not, flag changes.

I'll incorporate those items into the SOW and send back to Alex and procurement.

Thanks — Ben


From: Alex Johnson <alex.j@mediastream.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, procurement@mediastream.io
Date: 2026-06-18T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap: MediaStream pricing / target $/1M tokens

Thanks — that structure sounds fine. Procurement confirmed they want the SOW + a short one-page pricing annex that shows both the list and effective pilot net rate (so our finance team can see the discount mechanics). Could you get that to us by EOD today? If yes, procurement will schedule a short call Thursday 9:00am PT to walk contract items.

Also: one sticky point — leadership asked for a quick comparison vs \"self-hosted open model\" run by our infra team. They want to see the hidden ops risks and a TCO note. If you could add a single-paragraph TCO note to the Drive comparator that highlights ops/monitoring/latency risk assumptions, that would be helpful.

I'll expect the SOW + pricing annex and updated comparator (drive link) by EOD.

— Alex

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Pamela Nguyen <pamela.nguyen@veridianhealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: AuthZ collection request — Four-eyes audit for Veridian Health

Hi Pamela,

Thanks for the quick sync earlier. Per Veridian's SOC 2 scope we want to assemble a focused access-control packet that your audit team can review for the upcoming "four-eyes" entitlement review. To keep this scoped and reviewable, can you confirm the specific artifacts you prefer first? Common asks are:

- RBAC snapshot for production org (role -> members) as of 2026-06-01
- SCIM delta covering provisioning events for the prior 90 days (CSV/JSONL)
- SAML assertion/examples for SSO flows (sample timestamps + attributes)
- Owners mapping (role owners, approvers) and any high-risk service accounts

We can deliver via encrypted Drive folder (shared link) or SFTP; happy to deliver both. If you can confirm preferred format and whether the team needs signed hashes for chain-of-custody, we will package and deliver by EOD Wednesday (06/03).

Looping Jordan (on-product) and Soojin (security engineering) to coordinate extraction and redaction.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: none in this message
From: Pamela Nguyen <pamela.nguyen@veridianhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Veridian Health Security <security@veridianhealth.com>
Date: Mon, 1 Jun 2026 11:03:24 -0700
Subject: Re: AuthZ collection request — Four-eyes audit for Veridian Health

Ben —

Thanks, this looks good. For the initial review we need the following minimal set in this order of priority:

1) RBAC snapshot (CSV) with role id, role name, member ids (email), assignment timestamp, and a flag for owner/steward.
2) SCIM delta events covering 2026-03-01 to 2026-05-31 (we will accept JSONL).
3) Two SAML assertion samples that include the attributes you actually send (nameid, groups, email, iss, audience, inResponseTo). Redact PII except for the attributes themselves.
4) Owners map with email and management chain for each privileged role.

Two policy clarifications: we will not accept production log extracts without an integrity hash (SHA256) and a short explanation of any redaction. Also please mark any service accounts that can escalate to super-admins.

Preferred delivery: Google Drive link + HubSpot ticket ID. Please attach the HubSpot ticket number in your reply so our intake automation can file and triage.

Can you deliver the first bundle by EOD Wednesday (06/03)? If not, indicate the ETA.

Regards,
Pamela Nguyen
Director, Security Assurance — Veridian Health
security@veridianhealth.com

Hashing: We will publish SHA256 for each top-level file and include a signed timestamp (GPG) of the manifest. If Veridian needs HSM-signed artifacts let me know — otherwise GPG-signed manifest + SHA256 is standard.

source.subject: Route decision: billing responsibility and co-sell routing for Marketplace vs Direct
messages:
From: Maya Shah <maya.shah@procureops.com>
To: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naveen Gupta <naveen.gupta@cloudpartner.co>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Billing routing question — invoice owner / marketplace path

Hi Sofia,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — ProcureOps is excited about Redwood's latency profile. Our procurement team has two competing requests and I wanted to get your recommendation: they prefer to buy via AWS Marketplace private offer so the PO and invoice flow through Marketplace, which simplifies our internal AP controls. Our finance team also needs confirmation on co-sell crediting for our reseller partner (CloudPartner).

Specifically:
- If we purchase via AWS Private Offer, who is the seller-of-record on the invoice (Redwood or AWS)?
- Will our reseller partner (CloudPartner) be eligible for co-sell credits if we purchase through Marketplace?
- If we go direct (sign Redwood MSA + purchase invoice from Redwood), how does that affect co-sell attribution and timing for credits?

I attached our vendor RFP and a short spreadsheet with our expected monthly consumption. Could you advise recommended routing (Marketplace private offer vs direct MSA) given our needs and whether there are billing or compliance tradeoffs we should flag?

Appreciate any guidance and next steps.

Best,
Maya Shah
Sr. Procurement Manager
ProcureOps

Attachments: vendor_rfp_questions.pdf (PDF, 240KB)
From: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Shah <maya.shah@procureops.com>
Cc: Naveen Gupta <naveen.gupta@cloudpartner.co>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Billing routing question — invoice owner / marketplace path

Hi Maya — thanks for the clear questions and the RFP. Quick answers up front, and I can pull in our channel and finance leads if you’d like a short call:

- Seller-of-record: On AWS Private Offers the invoice is issued through the Marketplace flow — typically the buyer sees "AWS Marketplace" on some invoice fields but the charge is remitted to Redwood (seller) under the private offer contract. There are nuances depending on whether we act as seller-of-record or use a reseller model.
- Co-sell credits: Co-sell attribution depends on Marketplace listing setup and partner registration. CloudPartner can get credit if they are registered as the partner on the private offer or via a joint-sell agreement, but timing and paperwork (partner registration + evidence) matter.
- Direct MSA: Buying direct (Redwood invoice under our MSA) is the most straightforward for attribution to Redwood and for any custom contractual terms; partner incentives are handled via separate partner invoicing/crediting rather than Marketplace attribution.

Given your AP preference for PO+Marketplace invoice, the pragmatic recommendation is: try AWS Private Offer with seller-configuration that preserves Redwood as the economic seller while enabling CloudPartner registration for co-sell crediting. This keeps PO/invoice flow in Marketplace but preserves the partner/program eligibility.

If that sounds useful, I’ll loop in our channel and finance leads and share a short one-pager with the pros/cons and timing for onboarding CloudPartner. I’ve attached a pricing matrix so you can see SKU/term mappings.

Next steps?
- Confirm whether ProcureOps needs Redwood explicitly listed as seller-of-record on the top-line invoice field, or if Marketplace flow is acceptable.

title: Route decision: billing responsibility and co-sell routing for Marketplace vs Direct
preview:
From: Maya Shah <maya.shah@procureops.com>
To: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naveen Gupta <naveen.gupta@cloudpartner.co>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Billing routing question — invoice owner / marketplace path

Hi Sofia,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — ProcureOps is excited about Redwood's latency profile. Our procurement team has two competing requests and I wanted to get your recommendation: they prefer to buy via AWS Marketplace private offer so the PO and invoice flow through Marketplace, which simplifies our internal AP controls. Our finance team also needs confirmation on co-sell crediting for our reseller partner (CloudPartner).

Specifically:
- If we purchase via AWS Private Offer, who is the seller-of-record on the invoice (Redwood or AWS)?
- Will our reseller partner (CloudPartner) be eligible for co-sell credits if we purchase through Marketplace?
- If we go direct (sign Redwood MSA + purchase invoice from Redwood), how does that affect co-sell attribution and timing for credits?

I attached our vendor RFP and a short spreadsheet with our expected monthly consumption. Could you advise recommended routing (Marketplace private offer vs direct MSA) given our needs and whether there are billing or compliance tradeoffs we should flag?

Appreciate any guidance and next steps.

Best,
Maya Shah
Sr. Procurement Manager
ProcureOps

Attachments: vendor_rfp_questions.pdf (PDF, 240KB)


From: Sofia Ivanova <sofia.ivanova@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Shah <maya.shah@procureops.com>
Cc: Naveen Gupta <naveen.gupta@cloudpartner.co>
Date: Tue, 2 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Billing routing question — invoice owner / marketplace path

Hi Maya — thanks for the clear questions and the RFP. Quick answers up front, and I can pull in our channel and finance leads if you’d like a short call:

- Seller-of-record: On AWS Private Offers the invoice is issued through the Marketplace flow — typically the buyer sees "AWS Marketplace" on some invoice fields but the charge is remitted to Redwood (seller) under the private offer contract. There are nuances depending on whether we act as seller-of-record or use a reseller model.
- Co-sell credits: Co-sell attribution depends on Marketplace listing setup and partner registration. CloudPartner can get credit if they are registered as the partner on the private offer or via a joint-sell agreement, but timing and paperwork (partner registration + evidence) matter.
- Direct MSA: Buying direct (Redwood invoice under our MSA) is the most straightforward for attribution to Redwood and for any custom contractual terms; partner incentives are handled via separate partner invoicing/crediting rather than Marketplace attribution.

Given your AP preference for PO+Marketplace invoice, the pragmatic recommendation is: try AWS Private Offer with seller-configuration that preserves Redwood as the economic seller while enabling CloudPartner registration for co-sell crediting. This keeps PO/invoice flow in Marketplace but preserves the partner/program eligibility.

If that sounds useful, I’ll loop in our channel and finance leads and share a short one-pager with the pros/cons and timing for onboarding CloudPartner. I’ve attached a pricing matrix so you can see SKU/term mappings.

Next steps?
- Confirm whether ProcureOps needs Redwood explicitly listed as seller-of-record on the top-line invoice field, or if Marketplace flow is acceptable.
- If acceptable, I’ll run internal checks on partner registration and send a short checklist for CloudPartner.

Warm regards,
Sofia Ivanova
Commercial Lead, North America
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_pricing_matrix.xlsx (Excel, 42KB)

source.subject: Execution plan: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing & legal routing
messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: NovaMed Legal <legal@novamed.com>
Date: Thu, 28 May 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — next steps and timeline

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Legal at NovaMed reviewed the MSA + order form and flagged that we need an explicit transfer addendum attached to the DPA (SCCs referenced) before procurement will sign.

Can Redwood share a proposed sequencing for: (1) integrating the transfer addendum into the order form, (2) alignment with the MSA exhibits, and (3) identifying which clauses we should expect to see redlined (SCC insertion, processor/controller roles, and KMS/localization language)?

We're hoping to keep this to a single round of redlines to avoid procurement delays — target signature by the end of June if possible. I've attached our internal checklist (not the full legal comments) and CC'd our legal mailbox.

Thanks,
Elena
Director, IT Procurement
NovaMed Health
[Attachment: NovaMed_DPA_Checklist.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 28 May 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — next steps and timeline

Elena — thanks, got it.

Quick proposal for sequencing: 
1) Redwood to provide a single-transfer-addendum draft (SCCs + role table) — ETA 1 business day.
2) NovaMed legal to provide consolidated redlines into that single document — ETA 2 business days.
3) Redwood legal reviews and pushes redline into Order Form/Exhibits; produce a tracked Order Form PDF for procurement — ETA 1 business day.
4) Final DocuSign packet (Order Form + MSA signature block + DPA addendum) — ETA 1 business day after signoff.

I'll attach a baseline addendum and loop in Ben (contracts lead) and Laura (legal) for detailed redline guidance. If that timeline works, we'll set up a short call tomorrow to run through corner cases (KMS, EU export restrictions, sub-processor flow).

Connor
VP, Solutions — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: DPA_Transfer_Addendum_v1.docx (docx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 28 May 2026 14:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — proposed redlines

Elena / team — tagging in a few redline priorities from our side so NovaMed legal can consider them in the single round: 

- SCCs: we'll insert the latest EU Commission module and include a migration clause for future SCC updates (we'll request a mutual update clause rather than requiring re-signature for administrative SCC updates).
- Roles: provide a 2-column role table (Controller/Processor actions) — we ask NovaMed to confirm any controller-level obligations that would require us to assume additional liability.
- KMS / Key separation: propose optional clause where customer manages their KMS via Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) for EU-hosted private deployments; for Hosted we propose encrypted-at-rest with customer-managed KMS as an add-on.
- Localization: propose geographic routing commitments in Order Form exhibit A for "EU-only processing" (if required) and fallback routing policy.
- Evidence: we will provide SOC2 + encryption attestations; NovaMed to list any additional audit scoping in response.

Attached: quick inline annotations (high level) for the addendum. I'll be on the 15:30 call if you want to walk through.

Ben Carter
Sr. Contracts Manager — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: DPA_Addendum_annotated_notes.pdf (pdf)]

From: NovaMed Legal <legal@novamed.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Date: Fri, 29 May 2026 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — NovaMed redline questions

Hi Ben / Connor,

Thanks for the draft and notes. A few clarifying questions before we do a consolidated redline: 
1) Will Redwood accept the mutual-update approach for SCCs (i.e., administrative SCC updates pushed by reference) or require full re-execution? We're fine with reference updates but need a notice period.
2) For BYOK, please confirm the subset of features that would be unavailable if we actively manage keys (e.g., model fine-tuning snapshots, shared caching).
3) Regarding liability: our procurement team will push for a cap carveout if Redwood is processing strictly as a processor for health data. Can you confirm any standard carveouts you typically accept for processor-level breaches?

We'll consolidate redlines and return by Monday. If you can confirm Q1 and Q2 by EOD today, it will help our team triage the redlines.

Regards,
NovaMed Legal
[Signature omitted]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: NovaMed Legal <legal@novamed.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — answers + schedule

Good morning — quick answers to keep your procurement sprint moving: 

1) SCC updates: Redwood accepts SCC updates by reference for administrative updates with a 30-day notice period and the right for either party to request re-execution for substantive legal changes. We'll document notice mechanics in Section 6 of the addendum.

2) BYOK impacts: BYOK will restrict our ability to provide shared KV caching and certain cross-customer tooling (we will call these out in Exhibit B). Core runtime and inference features remain available; we'll provide a small matrix that lists which optional features are limited under BYOK.

3) Liability carveouts: Redwood's standard approach is a limited carveout for processor-only incidents tied to gross negligence or willful misconduct; we can consider a negotiated cap for pure processor processing of health data in this deal. Provide specific language or target cap and we'll evaluate.

Schedule: please send one consolidated redline by EOD Monday 2026-06-01; Ben and I will turn around internal comments within 24 hours and Jordan will prepare the DocuSign packet once legal signs off.

Laura Bennett
Head of Legal Operations — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: OrderForm_MSAV2_redlines.pdf (pdf)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>, NovaMed Legal <legal@novamed.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — next ops steps (DEAL-5821)

Elena / Legal team — once you send the consolidated redline we'll do the following in parallel to keep to your end-of-June target: 
- Ben/Laura: finalize redline review within 24 hours.
- Me: build the DocuSign packet (Order Form + MSA signature block + DPA addendum) and create a HubSpot deal activity (DEAL-5821).
- Ops: prepare a one-page feature-impact matrix (BYOK vs Hosted) and the SOC2 evidence bundle.

Can we confirm a 11:00am UTC quick alignment tomorrow (2026-06-02) for 20 minutes? I'll send a calendar invite pending your confirmation.

Thanks — Jordan
Account Executive — Redwood Inference

VP, Solutions — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: DPA_Transfer_Addendum_v1.docx (docx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 28 May 2026 14:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — proposed redlines

Elena / team — tagging in a few redline priorities from our side so NovaMed legal can consider them in the single round: 

- SCCs: we'll insert the latest EU Commission module and include a migration clause for future SCC updates (we'll request a mutual update clause rather than requiring re-signature for administrative SCC updates).
- Roles: provide a 2-column role table (Controller/Processor actions) — we ask NovaMed to confirm any controller-level obligations that would require us to assume additional liability.
- KMS / Key separation: propose optional clause where customer manages their KMS via Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) for EU-hosted private deployments; for Hosted we propose encrypted-at-rest with customer-managed KMS as an add-on.
- Localization: propose geographic routing commitments in Order Form exhibit A for "EU-only processing" (if required) and fallback routing policy.
- Evidence: we will provide SOC2 + encryption attestations; NovaMed to list any additional audit scoping in response.

Attached: quick inline annotations (high level) for the addendum. I'll be on the 15:30 call if you want to walk through.

Ben Carter
Sr. Contracts Manager — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: DPA_Addendum_annotated_notes.pdf (pdf)]

From: NovaMed Legal <legal@novamed.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@novamed.com>
Date: Fri, 29 May 2026 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — NovaMed redline questions

Hi Ben / Connor,

Thanks for the draft and notes. A few clarifying questions before we do a consolidated redline: 
1) Will Redwood accept the mutual-update approach for SCCs (i.e., administrative SCC updates pushed by reference) or require full re-execution? We're fine with reference updates but need a notice period.
2) For BYOK, please confirm the subset of features that would be unavailable if we actively manage keys (e.g., model fine-tuning snapshots, shared caching).
3) Regarding liability: our procurement team will push for a cap carveout if Redwood is processing strictly as a processor for health data. Can you confirm any standard carveouts you typically accept for processor-level breaches?

We'll consolidate redlines and return by Monday. If you can confirm Q1 and Q2 by EOD today, it will help our team triage the redlines.

Regards,
NovaMed Legal
[Signature omitted]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: NovaMed Legal <legal@novamed.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA transfer-addendum sequencing — answers + schedule

Good morning — quick answers to keep your procurement sprint moving: 

1) SCC updates: Redwood accepts SCC updates by reference for administrative updates with a 30-day notice period and the right for either party to request re-execution for substantive legal changes. We'll document notice mechanics in Section 6 of the addendum.

2) BYOK impacts: BYOK will restrict our ability to provide shared KV caching and certain cross-customer tooling (we will call these out in Exhibit B). Core runtime and inference features remain available; we'll provide a small matrix that lists which optional features are limited under BYOK.

3) Liability carveouts: Redwood's standard approach is a limited carveout for processor-only incidents tied to gross negligence or willful misconduct; we can consider a negotiated cap for pure processor processing of health data in this deal. Provide specific language or target cap and we'll evaluate.

Schedule: please send one consolidated redline by EOD Monday 2026-06-01; Ben and I will turn around internal comments within 24 hours and Jordan will prepare the DocuSign packet once legal signs off.

Laura Bennett
Head of Legal Operations — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: OrderForm_MSAV2_redlines.pdf (pdf)]

source.subject: EdgeEmbed POC: SDK auth, routing & 429/retry approach
messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-28T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Hi Amit —

Thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so both teams are aligned before we draft the SOW: 

What we captured from the call:
- POC goals: validate embeddings + reranker pipeline for semantic search at 50k queries/day with p95 latency < 250ms for retrieval path.
- Primary constraints: strict customer data residency (US), want per-route cost controls and predictable throttling during peak jobs.
- Integration touchpoints: SDK embed + server-side batching for bulk ingestion, realtime reranker for top-K candidate rescoring, and a lightweight tool-calling path for structured entity enrichment.

Key technical asks we promised to follow up on:
1) Auth options: we outlined API key + mTLS and mentioned support for token rotation via our platform KMS. Attached is a short "auth-options-summary.pdf" we think will help procurement.
2) Routing: recommend naming two logical routes — embeddings (gpu-opt) and reranker (cpu/gpu-mix); we can provide model aliases and automatic routing policy (cost-first vs latency-first).
3) 429 / retry behavior: propose an idempotency + request-id pattern for safe retries, and default backoff policy (expo + jitter) with respect to Retry-After headers. We included a sample header suggestion in "sample-request-ids.txt".

Proposed immediate next steps:
- Your team: confirm which auth option you prefer (API key server-to-server vs short-lived JWT) and share any procurement requirements.
- Our team: prepare POC SOW draft and a short integration checklist (SDK snippets for ingestion + client-side request ID examples).
- Kickoff: tentatively 2026-06-07 for a 1-hour tech kickoff (engineering + infra).

I've attached an initial POC scope (edgeembed-poc-scope.xlsx). Jordan and Marissa: please add anything I missed on the infra/SLAs.

Thanks,
Camila
Account Exec | Redwood Inference
From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@edgeembed.com>
Date: 2026-05-28T15:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Camila — thanks, useful summary. A couple quick clarify items from our infra team: 

- On auth: we prefer server-to-server short-lived JWTs issued by our identity provider. We need token rotation docs and rotation window expectations. Can Redwood integrate with our IDP (OIDC) or must we proxy through your KMS?

- On routing: reranker will run as an autoscaled service in our VPC; we need a deterministic way to pin reranker traffic to dedicated capacity during the POC. Is that something Redwood Dedicated can handle or do we need to add a header to route to a model alias?

- On 429s: we want a clear SLA for 429s vs throttling (is 429 only rate limit or also quota enforcement?). Also want guidance on idempotency for reranker calls (these are low QPS but must not duplicate charges/side effects).

We can do the kickoff on 6/7; please send a calendar invite.

—Amit
Sr. Engineering Manager, EdgeEmbed
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-28T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Amit —

Answers inline below, and attaching the short "auth-options-summary.pdf" for your IDP/infosec: 

- IDP / OIDC: Redwood supports OIDC for token exchange in Private deployments (we can federate to your IDP and issue short-lived platform tokens). For Hosted/Dedicated, we currently support server-to-server short-lived JWTs via our token-exchange endpoint or API keys with rotation via KMS. Which deployment mode are you evaluating for the POC? If Private/VPC, federating to your IDP is straightforward — we'll share the config doc.

- Routing to Dedicated capacity: Yes — you can pin by model-alias header (x-redwood-model-alias) or by route tag. For POC we recommend model-alias because it's explicit and works for both Hosted and Dedicated. We'll include SDK examples.

- 429 semantics: 429s are returned for rate limiting and momentary overload. Quota enforcement returns 403 with quota-identifier in the body. For idempotency: reranker calls should supply a request-id header (x-redwood-request-id) and an idempotency-key when any call could be retried and might change state. For pure scoring calls (no side effect) request-id is sufficient. See attached sample-request-ids.txt for header examples.

Next: I'll schedule the kickoff and Jordan will follow up with a short implementation checklist (SDK snippets, preferred headers, and sample backoff config).

Camila
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-29T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Hi Amit — quick technical follow-up before the kickoff: 

Implementation guidance (short bullets):
- SDK setup: we will provide language SDKs (Python/Go/Node) with a client constructor that accepts either short-lived JWTs (OIDC flow) or server API keys. Example: Client(auth=OIDC, id_token="...").
- Headers we recommend: x-redwood-model-alias, x-redwood-request-id, x-redwood-idempotency-key (optional for rerankers with side effects), and x-client-time (epoch-ms) for latency reconciliation.
- Retry policy: client default is exponential backoff with decorrelated jitter starting at 200ms, max backoff 8s, and max attempts 5. Honour Retry-After when present.
- 429 handling: treat 429 as transient — retry with backoff. If you receive repeated 429 + Retry-After > 30s, fall back to your low-cost routing (e.g., reroute to smaller model alias) or queue the request.
- Observability: include request-id in logs; Redwood will surface token-level latency/cost breakdowns — include the request-id so we can trace across both sides.

Attached: sample-request-ids.txt with header examples and the POC scope workbook Camila attached. Marcus: can you confirm we can reserve a small Dedicated pool for the reranker on the proposed dates?

Jordan
Principal Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@edgeembed.com>
Date: 2026-05-29T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Thanks Camila/Jordan — that all sounds good. We prefer OIDC federation in our VPC for the POC.

Action items on our side:
- Priya will collect procurement requirements and share the contract redline window by 6/2.
- Engineering will add the request-id header to our client and test the backoff policy you suggested.

Please send the kickoff invite for 6/7 and the POC SOW when ready. Looking forward to it.

Amit

1) Auth options: we outlined API key + mTLS and mentioned support for token rotation via our platform KMS. Attached is a short "auth-options-summary.pdf" we think will help procurement.
2) Routing: recommend naming two logical routes — embeddings (gpu-opt) and reranker (cpu/gpu-mix); we can provide model aliases and automatic routing policy (cost-first vs latency-first).
3) 429 / retry behavior: propose an idempotency + request-id pattern for safe retries, and default backoff policy (expo + jitter) with respect to Retry-After headers. We included a sample header suggestion in "sample-request-ids.txt".

Proposed immediate next steps:
- Your team: confirm which auth option you prefer (API key server-to-server vs short-lived JWT) and share any procurement requirements.
- Our team: prepare POC SOW draft and a short integration checklist (SDK snippets for ingestion + client-side request ID examples).
- Kickoff: tentatively 2026-06-07 for a 1-hour tech kickoff (engineering + infra).

I've attached an initial POC scope (edgeembed-poc-scope.xlsx). Jordan and Marissa: please add anything I missed on the infra/SLAs.

Thanks,
Camila
Account Exec | Redwood Inference


From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@edgeembed.com>
Date: 2026-05-28T15:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Camila — thanks, useful summary. A couple quick clarify items from our infra team: 

- On auth: we prefer server-to-server short-lived JWTs issued by our identity provider. We need token rotation docs and rotation window expectations. Can Redwood integrate with our IDP (OIDC) or must we proxy through your KMS?

- On routing: reranker will run as an autoscaled service in our VPC; we need a deterministic way to pin reranker traffic to dedicated capacity during the POC. Is that something Redwood Dedicated can handle or do we need to add a header to route to a model alias?

- On 429s: we want a clear SLA for 429s vs throttling (is 429 only rate limit or also quota enforcement?). Also want guidance on idempotency for reranker calls (these are low QPS but must not duplicate charges/side effects).

We can do the kickoff on 6/7; please send a calendar invite.

—Amit
Sr. Engineering Manager, EdgeEmbed


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-28T17:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Amit —

Answers inline below, and attaching the short "auth-options-summary.pdf" for your IDP/infosec: 

- IDP / OIDC: Redwood supports OIDC for token exchange in Private deployments (we can federate to your IDP and issue short-lived platform tokens). For Hosted/Dedicated, we currently support server-to-server short-lived JWTs via our token-exchange endpoint or API keys with rotation via KMS. Which deployment mode are you evaluating for the POC? If Private/VPC, federating to your IDP is straightforward — we'll share the config doc.

- Routing to Dedicated capacity: Yes — you can pin by model-alias header (x-redwood-model-alias) or by route tag. For POC we recommend model-alias because it's explicit and works for both Hosted and Dedicated. We'll include SDK examples.

- 429 semantics: 429s are returned for rate limiting and momentary overload. Quota enforcement returns 403 with quota-identifier in the body. For idempotency: reranker calls should supply a request-id header (x-redwood-request-id) and an idempotency-key when any call could be retried and might change state. For pure scoring calls (no side effect) request-id is sufficient. See attached sample-request-ids.txt for header examples.

Next: I'll schedule the kickoff and Jordan will follow up with a short implementation checklist (SDK snippets, preferred headers, and sample backoff config).

Camila


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-05-29T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Hi Amit — quick technical follow-up before the kickoff: 

Implementation guidance (short bullets):
- SDK setup: we will provide language SDKs (Python/Go/Node) with a client constructor that accepts either short-lived JWTs (OIDC flow) or server API keys. Example: Client(auth=OIDC, id_token="...").
- Headers we recommend: x-redwood-model-alias, x-redwood-request-id, x-redwood-idempotency-key (optional for rerankers with side effects), and x-client-time (epoch-ms) for latency reconciliation.
- Retry policy: client default is exponential backoff with decorrelated jitter starting at 200ms, max backoff 8s, and max attempts 5. Honour Retry-After when present.
- 429 handling: treat 429 as transient — retry with backoff. If you receive repeated 429 + Retry-After > 30s, fall back to your low-cost routing (e.g., reroute to smaller model alias) or queue the request.
- Observability: include request-id in logs; Redwood will surface token-level latency/cost breakdowns — include the request-id so we can trace across both sides.

Attached: sample-request-ids.txt with header examples and the POC scope workbook Camila attached. Marcus: can you confirm we can reserve a small Dedicated pool for the reranker on the proposed dates?

Jordan
Principal Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference


From: Amit Shah <amit.shah@edgeembed.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@edgeembed.com>
Date: 2026-05-29T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed POC scope (auth, routing, 429 strategy)

Thanks Camila/Jordan — that all sounds good. We prefer OIDC federation in our VPC for the POC.

Action items on our side:
- Priya will collect procurement requirements and share the contract redline window by 6/2.
- Engineering will add the request-id header to our client and test the backoff policy you suggested.

Please send the kickoff invite for 6/7 and the POC SOW when ready. Looking forward to it.

Amit

From: Amit Shah <amit@hypercart.io>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-05-23T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: POC load run - request for additional credits

Hi Connor,

Thanks again for the help getting the HyperCart integration into preprod. We finished the warmup today and want to run a full overnight throughput sweep (12 hours, peak concurrency ~1,000 concurrent requests) to validate tail latencies and cost per token. Our existing trial allocation will be exhausted by the time we run the soak.

Can Redwood extend the trial window or apply a short-term credit top-up specifically for this run? Ideally we need ~150k token-equivalent credits for the 12-hour soak starting night of May 24 (US Pacific).

Attached is the short test plan and the expected request matrix. If you need an NDA amendment or updated MSA note for the extended run, let me know.

Appreciate a quick turnaround — schedule is tight.

Thanks,
Amit Shah
Head of Platform
HyperCart Inc.
[Attachment: poc-test-plan.pdf (pdf, 1.2MB)]
[Attachment: load-matrix.csv (csv, 24KB)]

To: Amit Shah <amit@hypercart.io>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-05-23T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC load run - request for additional credits

Amit —

Thanks for the heads up and for sending the plan. This sounds reasonable for a controlled overnight soak; the run will give us the tail-latency signals we need to validate our batching and cache settings for your workload.

Looping in Jordan and Marissa to approve a short-term credit allowance and confirm the timing. Jordan — is a temporary 150k token-equivalent voucher OK here (timebox: 24–48h, single-run, non-renewing)?

Also can you confirm the exact start time (PDT) and whether you want streaming enabled for the test endpoints? If you want us to provision a dedicated canary on a 2-GPU pool for the run we can look at that as an alternative to a straight credit top-up.

Connor
Connor O'Brien | Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
T: +1 (415) 555-0123
[redwood.inference]

Date: 2026-05-24T09:24:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC load run - request for additional credits

Connor / Marissa —

Approved: we can provision a one-off promotional credit for HyperCart. Approve 150,000 token-equivalent credits tied to deal HC-2026-1123, valid for 36 hours starting at the requested run window. Please mark as "promo: poc-accelerant" in the billing note so Finance can track.

Finance — can you create the voucher code and attach the HubSpot deal link to the record? Will also need a short expiration TTL and a manual flag so it cannot be auto-renewed.

Marissa — once Finance confirms, Connor please coordinate the start time with Amit and we can flip a low-latency canary route if they choose.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake | Head of Sales, Pre-Sales Solutions | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: approval-note.txt (text, 1KB) — internal approval summary]

>
> Also can you confirm the exact start time (PDT) and whether you want streaming enabled for the test endpoints? If you want us to provision a dedicated canary on a 2-GPU pool for the run we can look at that as an alternative to a straight credit top-up.
>
> Connor
> Connor O'Brien | Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-05-25T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC load run - request for additional credits

Jordan / Connor —

Thanks — this is great news. We confirm the run for May 24 23:00 PDT start (overnight window), streaming disabled (we want raw token latency for the first pass). We accept the 36-hour TTL and the single-use voucher. Please share the voucher code and any special instructions (rate limits, recommended concurrency caps, model variant to pin) and we'll kick off the tests.

Also copying our ops alias in case there are any real-time questions on the run: ops@hypercart.io.

Best,

source.subject: POC success criteria & readout scheduling — Claragen
messages:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Park <sophie.park@claragen.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 22 May 2026 14:10:00 +0000
Subject: POC success criteria & readout scheduling — Claragen

Thanks for the call earlier, Sophie — good to meet the Claragen team. Per our discussion, putting a short written recap and proposed next steps so we have a clear trial runway.

TL;DR: attached is a one-page success criteria spreadsheet and a draft SOW. Proposed POC: 6 weeks (2-week pilot + 4-week evaluation), goal is an exec readout week of June 28 for a go/no-go decision.

Proposed success criteria (draft):
- Throughput: end-to-end inference latency < 250ms p95 on 512-token baseline (test harness to be provided)
- Cost: token unit cost budget <= $0.0016 per generated token (we'll provide breakdown)
- Accuracy: top-1 intent match >= 92% vs your human-labeled set (10k sample)
- Integration: end-to-end demo using your staging dataset and our streaming API with authentication
- Security: SOC2 controls confirmation and KMS proof-of-concept for key rotation

Owners (proposed):
- Redwood trial lead: Dylan Brooks (trial orchestration + runtime config)
- Claragen product lead: Sophie Park (datasets, label set, success judgments)
- Contract owner: Jordan Blake (Redwood commercial/contracting)

Immediate asks:
1) Sophie: confirm the success criteria above or flag edits by EOD Monday 5/25
2) Claragen security contact (cc Tom if possible) to confirm SOC2 evidence cadence
3) Jordan/Marissa: can you tentatively block an exec readout window the week of 2026-06-28? Looking for a 30–45 minute slot with Claragen execs + Redwood VP (Marissa).

Attachments: Claragen_POC_Success_Criteria_v1.xlsx (one-tab), Claragen_SOW_draft_20260522.pdf (draft commercial terms)

Thanks — happy to jump on a short alignment call if needed.

—
Dylan Brooks
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
D: +1 415-555-0143 | dylan.brooks@redwood.ai

From: Sophie Park <sophie.park@claragen.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Wu <tom.wu@claragen.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 25 May 2026 09:15:00 -0400
Subject: Re: POC success criteria & readout scheduling — Claragen

Thanks Dylan — appreciate the recap and the clear owners. Quick responses inline: 

- Success criteria: overall looks great. Two small edits: (1) for latency let's call out our canonical payload (avg 450 tokens) and ask for p95 < 300ms on that payload; (2) for accuracy let's change threshold to >= 90% (we need a bit more headroom for sampling).
- Security: looping Tom (our security lead) — Tom, can you confirm which SOC2 artifacts you need and if we need a short security workshop?
- Timeline: the 6-week window is fine, but Claragen prefers the exec readout in the week of 2026-07-05 (slightly later) — internal calendar constraints. If that's OK for Redwood, we'll target that.

Attachments: I've uploaded a short Claragen eval rubric (internal) to the shared drive — link below. Also attached our internal stakeholder list.

Shared link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/claragen-poc-eval

If you can update the success criteria sheet with our two edits and resend, I'll confirm owners on our side and get Tom's SOC2 note.

Best,
Sophie
Sophie Park
Director of Product | Claragen

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 27 May 2026 17:30:00 +0000
Subject: Fwd: POC success criteria & readout scheduling — Claragen

Marissa / Jordan — forwarding Sophie (Claragen)'s reply. Key changes: latency target move to p95 < 300ms on 450-token payload, accuracy threshold to >= 90%, exec readout week requested 2026-07-05 (Claragen constraint).

Can we do the following: 
- Marissa: can you tentatively block a 30–45 min exec slot the week of 2026-07-05 (ideally 07/07 or 07/08) for a readout + decision? We'd ask for VP-level attendance from Redwood (you) and Claragen execs.
- Jordan: once internal slot is confirmed, I'll work with Sophie to align on final attendees and we'll push the SOW draft into DocuSign for signature on go decision. Can you prepare a concise contract path (two options: fast-track 12-mo commit or standard 36-mo with discounts) so we can present at readout?

I've updated the attached success criteria with Sophie's edits (attached as v1.1). Planning to begin trial instrumentation week of 2026-06-01 if Claragen confirms.

Quoted for context:
> On Mon, 25 May 2026 09:15 -0400 Sophie Park <sophie.park@claragen.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Dylan — appreciate the recap and the clear owners. Quick responses inline:
> > - Success criteria: overall looks great...

Attachments: Claragen_POC_Success_Criteria_v1.1.xlsx

—
Dylan

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sophie Park <sophie.park@claragen.com>
Date: Sun, 24 May 2026 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC success criteria & readout scheduling — Claragen

Thanks for the clear threading, Dylan — this works. I'm available the week of 2026-07-05 and will block 45 minutes on 2026-07-07 at 16:00 PT (please confirm with Claragen). Jordan: I'll need your recommended commercial options in brief (one-pager) by 2026-06-21 so legal can prelim-review terms ahead of the readout.

On the SOC2 front, Tom (cc'd) should provide a short artifact list; if Claragen needs a short workshop we'll run it during trial week 2. Sophie — thanks for the drive link.

Next steps checklist (Redwood action owners):
- Dylan: finalize trial harness plan and test payload by 2026-05-29
- Jordan: draft commercial one-pager (fast-track vs standard) by 2026-06-21
- Marissa: reserve exec calendar (done, pending confirmation)
- Sophie/Claragen: confirm internal stakeholders and Tom's SOC2 artifact needs by 2026-05-29

I'll hold the 07/07 16:00 PT slot for now. Dylan — please confirm final attendees and send calendar invite with dial-in + agenda once Claragen confirms.

Best,
Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Success | Redwood Inference

TL;DR: attached is a one-page success criteria spreadsheet and a draft SOW. Proposed POC: 6 weeks (2-week pilot + 4-week evaluation), goal is an exec readout week of June 28 for a go/no-go decision.

Proposed success criteria (draft):
- Throughput: end-to-end inference latency < 250ms p95 on 512-token baseline (test harness to be provided)
- Cost: token unit cost budget <= $0.0016 per generated token (we'll provide breakdown)
- Accuracy: top-1 intent match >= 92% vs your human-labeled set (10k sample)
- Integration: end-to-end demo using your staging dataset and our streaming API with authentication
- Security: SOC2 controls confirmation and KMS proof-of-concept for key rotation

Owners (proposed):
- Redwood trial lead: Dylan Brooks (trial orchestration + runtime config)
- Claragen product lead: Sophie Park (datasets, label set, success judgments)
- Contract owner: Jordan Blake (Redwood commercial/contracting)

Immediate asks:
1) Sophie: confirm the success criteria above or flag edits by EOD Monday 5/25
2) Claragen security contact (cc Tom if possible) to confirm SOC2 evidence cadence
3) Jordan/Marissa: can you tentatively block an exec readout window the week of 2026-06-28? Looking for a 30–45 minute slot with Claragen execs + Redwood VP (Marissa).

Attachments: Claragen_POC_Success_Criteria_v1.xlsx (one-tab), Claragen_SOW_draft_20260522.pdf (draft commercial terms)

—
Dylan Brooks

source.subject: Hosted API pricing follow-up — monthly token volume & peak TPS
messages:
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Erin Walsh <erin@siriushealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Recap + asks: hosted API usage & peak TPS for pricing sketch

Erin — thanks again for taking the time earlier today. Quick recap and a couple of concrete asks so we can put together a hosted pricing sketch that maps to what you described on the call.

Call recap (high level):
- Primary product: patient triage and clinical summarization (API calls are a mix of short prompts + longer summarization).
- Current pilot volume: small (hundreds of requests/day). Planned ramp in Q3 with a fuller rollout in Q4.
- Key drivers for cost: monthly token volume, 99th percentile TPS during peak windows, and % of payloads that require long-context generation (>2k tokens).

Asks (will let Jordan/Marissa fold into the pricing sketch):
1) Can you confirm target monthly token range (best estimate for month 1 after rollout and expected steady-state)? Prefer a low/typical/high bracket.
2) For peak sizing — what is the expected sustained TPS during high-traffic windows vs absolute short bursts? If you have a 99th percentile TPS or observed peak during load tests, that helps.
3) Rough breakdown: % of requests that are embeddings/semantic search vs text generation (summaries).
4) Any hard budget target in $/1M tokens (or a ballpark $ per month) that procurement will push for?

Attached: I re-attached the CSV you shared on the call (Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv) — can you confirm it’s the latest export? Also we’ll drop a first-pass hosted estimate (PDF) once we have the items above.

Next steps:
- Erin: confirm numbers above by end of day Wednesday (5/20) if possible.
- Jordan: draft hosted estimate + commit scenarios (we’ll propose a couple of slabs).
- Marissa: prepare onboarding/CS checklist and expected time-to-live production for a hosted setup.

Thanks — David

--
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
david.kim@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0199
From: Erin Walsh <erin@siriushealth.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@siriushealth.com
Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 09:14:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Recap + asks: hosted API usage & peak TPS for pricing sketch

David — thanks for the clear recap. Confirming numbers below (these are internal estimates; will loop procurement on formal docs):

Monthly token volume (estimates):
- Typical month (post-rollout steady-state): ~3.4 million tokens/month
- High month (marketing-driven spikes / new feature rollout): up to ~8.5 million tokens/month
- Low month (pilot/limited): ~700k tokens/month

Peak sizing:
- Sustained high-traffic windows (10–20 minute windows during business hours): ~45 TPS sustained
- Short burst peaks (under 1 minute) we’ve seen up to ~250 TPS in our internal sim — those are rare but important for us

Workload mix:
- ~65% generation (patient summaries, longer context responses)
- ~30% embeddings/semantic search
- ~5% other (reranking, small utilities)

Budget target (procurement guidance):
- Benchmark target: $85–$110 per 1M tokens for hosted inference (they'll push lower if commitment/term). If an SLA-backed commit is required, procurement will expect a discounted band.

Attachments: the CSV attached to the call is the latest traffic export (Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv). I'll also share a short procurement intake doc to procurement@siriushealth.com this afternoon.

Happy to jump on a follow-up to walk through how the bursts map to concurrency with your team.

—Erin
Erin Walsh
Head of Product, Sirius Health
erin@siriushealth.com
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Recap + asks: hosted API usage & peak TPS for pricing sketch

Forwarding Erin's confirm with my quick analysis / asks.

Quoted message below for context.

Quick takeaways:
- Steady-state ~3.4M / month, spike headroom to 8.5M — we should prepare a 3-tier hosted pricing: Pilot (<=1M), Growth (~3–4M), Burst (>=8M) with clear per-1M pricing and burst TPS allowances.
- Peak bursts up to 250 TPS mean we need to propose burst capacity (paid ephemeral burst or automatic fallback to a cheaper small-context variant).
- Procurement target $85–$110 / 1M: recommend we lead with $99/1M for Growth slab (with a 10% discount for 12-month commit) and present a higher price for on-demand (no commit) and a lower per-1M for a credit/commit band at 5M+.

Action items:
- Jordan: can you sketch three hosted bands with those reference points and include an explicit burst surcharge (e.g., additional $/peak-TPS-hour beyond X TPS)? Aim for a first draft by EOD Thursday (5/21).
- Marissa: start a short onboarding timeline (how long to get them to production on hosted API, security checklist items they’ll need for SOC2 evidence) — they explicitly care about SOC2 and procurement will want standard evidence.

Also attaching a draft estimate PDF (Redwood-pricing-estimate-v1.pdf) — it's a placeholder until Jordan's numbers land.

Thanks — David

--
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Erin Walsh <erin@siriushealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, procurement@siriushealth.com
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Recap + asks: hosted API usage & peak TPS for pricing sketch — next steps

Erin / procurement — thanks for the quick numbers. We’ll incorporate the buckets and burst profile into a short hosted proposal. Proposed next steps: 

1) Internal: Jordan will share a first-pass hosted pricing slab (Growth / Burst / Pilot) by EOD today (Thu 5/21).
2) Joint review: Schedule a 60-minute pricing review next Tue 5/26 at 10:00am PT — will walk through per-1M assumptions, burst handling, and a commit vs on-demand comparison. Please confirm if that works.
3) Procurement: if you can share any required contract templates or procurement intake (DocuSign / SOW process) before the review, that will speed things up.

Attachments included: Redwood-pricing-estimate-v1.pdf (placeholder). Also confirming we have Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv as the canonical sample.

If the 10:00am PT slot doesn't work, propose 3:00pm PT same day — happy to be flexible.

Thanks — looking forward to getting a concrete number aligned.

David
--
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0199
Attachment stubs: Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv (CSV, 74 KB); Redwood-pricing-estimate-v1.pdf (PDF, 210 KB)
