- Low month (pilot/limited): ~700k tokens/month

Peak sizing:
- Sustained high-traffic windows (10–20 minute windows during business hours): ~45 TPS sustained
- Short burst peaks (under 1 minute) we’ve seen up to ~250 TPS in our internal sim — those are rare but important for us

Workload mix:
- ~65% generation (patient summaries, longer context responses)
- ~30% embeddings/semantic search
- ~5% other (reranking, small utilities)

Budget target (procurement guidance):
- Benchmark target: $85–$110 per 1M tokens for hosted inference (they'll push lower if commitment/term). If an SLA-backed commit is required, procurement will expect a discounted band.

Attachments: the CSV attached to the call is the latest traffic export (Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv). I'll also share a short procurement intake doc to procurement@siriushealth.com this afternoon.

Happy to jump on a follow-up to walk through how the bursts map to concurrency with your team.

—Erin
Erin Walsh
Head of Product, Sirius Health
erin@siriushealth.com


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 08:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Recap + asks: hosted API usage & peak TPS for pricing sketch

Forwarding Erin's confirm with my quick analysis / asks.

Quoted message below for context.

Quick takeaways:
- Steady-state ~3.4M / month, spike headroom to 8.5M — we should prepare a 3-tier hosted pricing: Pilot (<=1M), Growth (~3–4M), Burst (>=8M) with clear per-1M pricing and burst TPS allowances.
- Peak bursts up to 250 TPS mean we need to propose burst capacity (paid ephemeral burst or automatic fallback to a cheaper small-context variant).
- Procurement target $85–$110 / 1M: recommend we lead with $99/1M for Growth slab (with a 10% discount for 12-month commit) and present a higher price for on-demand (no commit) and a lower per-1M for a credit/commit band at 5M+.

Action items:
- Jordan: can you sketch three hosted bands with those reference points and include an explicit burst surcharge (e.g., additional $/peak-TPS-hour beyond X TPS)? Aim for a first draft by EOD Thursday (5/21).
- Marissa: start a short onboarding timeline (how long to get them to production on hosted API, security checklist items they’ll need for SOC2 evidence) — they explicitly care about SOC2 and procurement will want standard evidence.

Also attaching a draft estimate PDF (Redwood-pricing-estimate-v1.pdf) — it's a placeholder until Jordan's numbers land.

Thanks — David

--
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Erin Walsh <erin@siriushealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, procurement@siriushealth.com
Date: Thu, 21 May 2026 10:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Recap + asks: hosted API usage & peak TPS for pricing sketch — next steps

Erin / procurement — thanks for the quick numbers. We’ll incorporate the buckets and burst profile into a short hosted proposal. Proposed next steps: 

1) Internal: Jordan will share a first-pass hosted pricing slab (Growth / Burst / Pilot) by EOD today (Thu 5/21).
2) Joint review: Schedule a 60-minute pricing review next Tue 5/26 at 10:00am PT — will walk through per-1M assumptions, burst handling, and a commit vs on-demand comparison. Please confirm if that works.
3) Procurement: if you can share any required contract templates or procurement intake (DocuSign / SOW process) before the review, that will speed things up.

Attachments included: Redwood-pricing-estimate-v1.pdf (placeholder). Also confirming we have Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv as the canonical sample.

If the 10:00am PT slot doesn't work, propose 3:00pm PT same day — happy to be flexible.

Thanks — looking forward to getting a concrete number aligned.

David
--
David Kim
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0199
Attachment stubs: Sirius_traffic_snapshot.csv (CSV, 74 KB); Redwood-pricing-estimate-v1.pdf (PDF, 210 KB)

From: Emily Reyes <emily@northgate.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@northgate.com
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood onboarding: DPA, SOC2, subprocessor list + PO flow

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the walkthrough earlier. Procurement has a short intake checklist we need to complete before we can issue a PO. Can you send:

- The DPA (or draft if you prefer DocuSign) we should sign
- SOC 2 report (either a PDF or a link to the sealed report)
- Current subprocessor inventory (CSV is fine)

Also, a couple of process questions so we can route internally:
- Do you accept our standard PO template (attached)?
- Billing contact and invoicing email for net-30 terms
- Any one-time onboarding or setup fees we should list on the PO

We'd like to keep this moving next week. Looping in procurement group on CC.

Thanks,
Emily Reyes
Senior Procurement Manager
Northgate Logistics

(attachment: northgate_po_template.docx)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Emily Reyes <emily@northgate.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 17 May 2026 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood onboarding: DPA, SOC2, subprocessor list + PO flow

Hi Emily — great to meet earlier. Thanks for the checklist; please see answers below and attached artifacts.

1) DPA: I attached a Redwood draft DPA (Redwood_DPA_Northgate_draft.pdf). We can send via DocuSign once terms are agreed. DocuSign link: https://app.docusign.com/ndg-dpa-sign

2) SOC 2: Link to our most recent SOC 2 Type II (2025): https://redwood-inference.s3.amazonaws.com/soc2/2025-report.pdf (PDF attached as a hosted link; summary also in the linked doc).

3) Subprocessors: current inventory attached as subprocessors_202605.csv. Includes hosting, logging, and monitoring vendors. We keep this updated monthly and can provide attestations for any listed vendor on request.

PO + invoicing:
- We accept your PO template; attached is a copy we reviewed.
- Billing contact: Finance Ops — invoices@redwood.com (include Deal ID: NDG-8421 on the invoice).
- Standard terms: net-30 by default for enterprise deals; early payment/discounts subject to negotiation. No mandatory one-time onboarding fee for standard hosted API. If you require Dedicated or Private deployment, there may be an activation charge — we can flag that now if you expect to use Dedicated capacity.

I've CC'd Laura (Finance Ops lead) to confirm invoice routing and Jordan from customer success to coordinate onboarding kickoff and any configuration work. Attached stubs: Redwood_DPA_Northgate_draft.pdf, subprocessors_202605.csv. Also included a small text file with the SOC2 hosted link name (Redwood_SOC2-2025-link.txt).

Next steps:
- If terms on the DPA look OK, we can send the DocuSign envelope; once signed, we'll accept PO and start the onboarding sprint.
- Please confirm invoicing email for AP and whether Northgate requires any additional billing identifiers.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

(attachments: Redwood_DPA_Northgate_draft.pdf, subprocessors_202605.csv, Redwood_SOC2-2025-link.txt)

From: procurement@northgate.com
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Emily Reyes <emily@northgate.com>, AP Team <ap@northgate.com>
Date: Mon, 18 May 2026 08:37:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood onboarding: DPA, SOC2, subprocessor list + PO flow

Ben/Laura,

Thanks for the quick reply and the attachments — procurement reviewed the DPA and SOC2 link. A few items before we can issue the PO: 

- We need Redwood to acknowledge a data retention clause specific to logistics telemetry (we'll paste our short paragraph into the PO if you cannot accept in the DPA).
- Please confirm whether any subprocessors process EU-origin data and whether you have SCCs in place (Northgate will require SCCs if so).
- Invoices should go to ap@northgate.com and reference our internal cost center CC-7091.

We are fine with net-30 and the standard PO flow. We'll plan to issue the PO once we have: signed DPA (DocuSign), a one-line confirmation on SCCs (or the subprocessors CSV shows location), and the billing contact confirmation.

Emily
(on behalf of Northgate Procurement)

To: procurement@northgate.com, Emily Reyes <emily@northgate.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 19 May 2026 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood onboarding: DPA, SOC2, subprocessor list + PO flow

Hi Emily / Northgate procurement team —

Thanks — quick clarifications and proposed next steps: 

- Data retention clause: we reviewed your paragraph and can accept the logistics telemetry retention limits proposed; we'll incorporate the clause into the DPA and put a redline in the DocuSign envelope for your review.
- SCCs / EU data: none of the listed subprocessors process EU-origin data for Northgate under this engagement (this is a hosted US-region integration). I'll have our legal ops confirm in writing and append SCCs if that changes — for now we can provide the affirmative one-liner you asked for.
- Invoice routing: confirmed — invoices@redwood.com is our external-facing billing address, but per your request we will put ap@northgate.com as recipient and include cost center CC-7091 on the invoice. Please instruct AP to expect a PDF invoice after PO acceptance.

Next steps (proposed timeline):
1) I'll update the DPA with the retention clause and send via DocuSign by EOD Tue (DocuSign link will be the same: https://app.docusign.com/ndg-dpa-sign).
2) Once signed, Northgate issues PO to: invoices@redwood.com (please CC ap@northgate.com in your PO email).
3) Laura (cc'd) will confirm invoice format and whether you need a specific W-9 / tax form.
4) Jordan will schedule a 30-minute onboarding kickoff for Wed to review API keys, allowed origins, and support SLAs.

If anything changes on your side, ping me — happy to escalate to legal or our privacy team if procurement wants a quick call.

Best regards,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0123

(attachments: Northgate_PO_Template.pdf)

Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 25 Apr 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Procurement & Legal review: DPA redlines + review meeting

Hi Aisha / Legal team —

Thanks for confirming the procurement review this week. Attached is the DPA draft with the changes we discussed (Redwood-DPA-draft-v2.pdf) and a short agenda for the call. Goals for the 30-minute review: confirm the outstanding redline items, align on logging/audit retention ask, and agree on next steps to get to signature.

Proposed time: Tue 2026-04-27 10:00–10:30 AM PT. I added a calendar invite — let me know if we need another slot.

Attachments: [Attachment: Redwood-DPA-draft-v2.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB)], [Attachment: Meeting-Agenda.pdf (PDF, 120KB)]

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Redwood Inference — developer-first inference platform

Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Procurement & Legal review: DPA redlines + review meeting

Aisha —

We were on for 10:00 and I waited in the meeting room; I don't see anyone joined. Wanted to check that the invite still works on your side and whether we should reschedule. I have Laura (legal) and Jordan (sales lead) on as backup in case any redline clarifications are needed.

If something came up, no problem — can you share best availability for the rest of this week or early next week? If it's easier, we can also do a quick async call where Laura flags the one or two critical clauses and we follow up with a DocuSign flow.

Ben

source.subject: PharosRx security follow-up: SOC 2, DDQ, and trust portal access
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Harper Liu <harper@pharosrx.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Apr 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: PharosRx security follow-up: SOC 2, DDQ, and trust portal access

Hi Harper —

Thanks again for taking the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment on the security/compliance items you called out:

- PharosRx asks: SOC 2 Type II report, bridge letter/interim attestation, and a vendor DDQ (we heard the DDQ will be authored on your side).
- Data residency / HIPAA: Redwood supports US and EU residency; we are HIPAA-ready and can sign a BAA.
- Evidence exchange: we use a Trust Portal (secure link) for artifacts, plus S3/SFTP options for customers with procurement constraints.

My asks:
1) Can you share the DDQ document or portal invite when it’s ready? (I added Jordan to help with technical answers.)
2) Would Pharos prefer the SOC 2 Type II PDF + standard bridge letter, or do you need an expanded attestation for specific controls?
3) Timeline — what’s your deadline for receiving the report/attestation?

I attached a short post-call deck with the compliance touchpoints. Jordan and I will coordinate with our security team to get you the SOC2 package and a bridge letter by the turnaround you need.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0129
Redwood Inference

Attachments: postcall-deck.pdf (slide deck, 6 pages)
From: Harper Liu <harper@pharosrx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: security@pharosrx.com
Date: Wed, 15 Apr 2026 11:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: PharosRx security follow-up: SOC 2, DDQ, and trust portal access

Thanks, Ben — great recap. A few items from our side:

- I’ll upload the vendor DDQ into our security portal today; you should receive an invite from security@pharosrx.com.
- For the SOC 2: we need the latest Type II report (end date within the last 12 months) plus a bridge letter if the report doesn’t cover the current quarter.
- HIPAA: we will need confirmation that Redwood will sign a BAA (we can share our standard BAA).
- Can you confirm whether log retention and audit logs are available in the portal you mentioned? We typically ask for 12 months of retention and access to audit trails for admin changes.

Also, we prefer DocuSign for the BAA and any procurement paperwork. Can you send links or attach the SOC 2 when available?

Harper Liu
Director, Information Security
PharosRx
harper@pharosrx.com | (617) 555-2233

Subject: Re: PharosRx security follow-up: SOC 2, DDQ, and trust portal access

Harper — quick note to confirm next steps and to loop in our security lead, Marissa.

- Jordan: can you prepare answers to the technical DDQ items once Harper’s portal invite arrives?
- Marissa: short summary below of what Pharos needs (SOC2 Type II + bridge letter, BAA, audit log retention) — can your team provide the report and bridge letter and a Trust Portal upload?

Harper: we can provide the SOC2 Type II PDF (Redwood's most recent report) and a one-page bridge letter. For audit logs and retention, we store admin/audit logs for 12 months by default; we’ll confirm the exact retention window for specific control types. We can put the artifacts into our Trust Portal and/or provide a time-limited S3 bucket link — whichever works for procurement.

If you send the portal invite/DDQ, Jordan will take point on the questionnaire and Marissa will provide the artifacts. Ben will handle the procurement/BAA routing.

Thanks —
Ben

>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Harper Liu <harper@pharosrx.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2026 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood SOC 2 package and bridge letter — PharosRx

Hi Harper —

Thanks for the notes. I’m Marissa from Redwood’s Trust & Compliance team — I’ll coordinate artifacts and the secure handoff.

What I’m attaching / providing now:
- Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2025.pdf (full Type II report, cover period: 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31)
- Redwood_Bridge_Letter_2026-03.docx (interim bridge letter covering controls through Q1 2026)
- Secure Trust Portal invite: https://trust.redwood.com/portal/PharosRx-access-request (one-click access; will expire in 7 days)

Audit/log retention: we retain admin/audit logs for 12 months and can provide S3 exports on a limited-time basis if you need raw access for forensics.

BAA: we are willing to sign your BAA. Ben — feel free to route DocuSign and I’ll review the legal language if needed.

If you want a short walkthrough inside the Trust Portal, Jordan can join and demo artifact locations. Next steps from our side: I’ll upload the two artifacts and grant the Pharos security group access once you accept the portal invite.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Director, Trust & Compliance | Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachments: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_2025.pdf (PDF), Redwood_Bridge_Letter_2026-03.docx (DOCX)
From: Harper Liu <harper@pharosrx.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: security@pharosrx.com
Date: Fri, 17 Apr 2026 15:04:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Redwood SOC 2 package and bridge letter — PharosRx

Marissa — thanks for the quick turnaround and the portal link. I accepted the invite and can see the uploaded artifacts. A couple quick items:

1) The report and bridge letter look good at a high level; I’ll share with our procurement/legal for the final review.
2) Please route the BAA via DocuSign to harper@pharosrx.com and security@pharosrx.com — we prefer a signed BAA before any production data exchange.
3) We may have a short follow-up on control A.3.2 around key rotation frequency; Jordan, I’ll ping you in the DDQ once I see the technical details.

Appreciate the speed — I’ll keep you posted after procurement review.

Best,
Harper

source.subject: Renewal: onboarding acceleration + staggered PO for capacity uplift
messages:
From: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2026 14:02:00 -0400
Subject: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Hi Aditya / team,

Quick context — Globex's renewal is coming up (contract ends 2026-06-30). Procurement asked me to see if we can accelerate vendor re-onboarding so we can finalize a staggered PO (initial commit + top-up tranche) before the end of May. Two asks from our side: 1) confirm process for "vendor re-certification" in your portal (we need to upload SOC2 artifacts and new W-9), and 2) provide a short mutual action plan so legal/finance can sign off on the split PO approach.

We'd like:
- a checklist for what PR/Finance needs to approve the staggered PO
- expected turnaround time if we submit docs this week
- single-person contact who can shepherd the vendor onboarding steps

I can loop internal finance (cc'd) once we have the checklist. Appreciate a crisp plan — Globex is tying seat increase (from 200k to 350k tokens/month equivalent) to the renewal.

Thanks,
Lori Chen
Procurement Lead, Globex Corp

--
Attachment: procurement_request_form.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Apr 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Lori — thanks for the clear ask. High-level proposal below and attaching a one-page mutual action plan to make procurement/finance signoff straightforward.

Summary of path:
1) Vendor re-onboarding in Redwood Procurement Portal (link below) — requires SOC2 evidence index, updated PO/POB (purchase order/billing contact), and updated tax docs. Portal submission typically validates-in 48-72 hours for basic docs; evidence review for SOC2 artifacts can add 3-5 business days.
2) Staggered PO approach — we can accept an initial PO for 60% of committed seats and a top-up PO for the remainder to be issued within 90 days of renewal start. Legal has pre-approved this pattern for customers of your size (we'll attach the SOW addendum).
3) Single point liaison — Jordan will own the portal handoff and will be the escalation for any evidence gaps. Marissa will oversee finance/legal coordination for the PO split. Evelyn will track SOC2 evidence items and confirm the evidence index.

Immediate next steps I suggest (mutual action plan):
- Today: Lori to confirm procurement email and attach the initial PO draft + W-9 (if available).
- By 14 Apr: Jordan will create vendor profile in portal and send portal invite to Globex procurement.
- By 16 Apr: Globex uploads SOC2 evidence index + any requested artifacts.
- By 20 Apr: Redwood validates the evidence index and sends SOW addendum (redlines allowed via DocuSign).
- By 28 Apr: Finance signoff on initial PO (60%) and DocuSign complete.

Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN_Globex.xlsx; I'll also include the SOW redlines after legal finalizes.

If this timeline works, Jordan will start the portal invite tomorrow morning.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Sr. Account Exec — Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN_Globex.xlsx

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Apr 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Hi Lori — I'll own the portal steps. Quick notes on the onboarding path and a couple of friction points we've seen: 

Portal steps (short):
- Create vendor profile -> assign procurement user -> upload tax docs -> submit evidence index -> vendor attestation -> finance PO attach.

Common friction and mitigations:
- SOC2 evidence scanned as a single PDF can cause field-mapping delays; please use the evidence index CSV in the attachment so we can map to our portal fields.
- If procurement can't upload the full SOC2 report due to NDAs, a signed summary + control mapping is sufficient for the onboarding gate; we'll list what we need in the evidence index.

DocuSign flow: our legal prefers the SOW addendum executed electronically. I'll prepare a DocuSign envelope and share the link as soon as legal finishes redlines.

Portal invite will come from onboarding@redwood.com — keep an eye on spam folders and whitelist that domain. I plan to send the invite later today (14 Apr).

Jordan
Implementation Lead, Customer Onboarding
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: SOC2_evidence_index.csv

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Apr 2026 16:20:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Lori — looping in Finance (Evelyn) explicitly to confirm PO split approvals and billing schedule. Evelyn, see brief below and doc links (HubSpot deal GLX-089). We are ok to proceed with the initial 60% PO + top-up if the billing/recognition cadence works for Globex. Please confirm EOM billing cadence, and whether you need the vendor invoice to include both POs or the initial only.

Also, legal asked me to flag a single minor redline on the SOW addendum (term change clause): they want an explicit rollback clause if usage exceeds the 90-day ramp by >30% so we can auto-trigger a capacity reprice review. I'll attach our redline PDF.

If everything is green, Aditya — please push the DocuSign envelope today so Lori can sign and procurement can upload docs.

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Director, Commercial Operations
Redwood Inference

--
Attachments: SOW_Globex_v3_redlines.pdf
Forwarded-From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
On Mon, 13 Apr 2026 at 09:18, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com> wrote:
> [mutual action plan attached]
>
> --
> Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN_Globex.xlsx

From: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>

Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2026 14:02:00 -0400
Subject: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Hi Aditya / team,

Quick context — Globex's renewal is coming up (contract ends 2026-06-30). Procurement asked me to see if we can accelerate vendor re-onboarding so we can finalize a staggered PO (initial commit + top-up tranche) before the end of May. Two asks from our side: 1) confirm process for "vendor re-certification" in your portal (we need to upload SOC2 artifacts and new W-9), and 2) provide a short mutual action plan so legal/finance can sign off on the split PO approach.

We'd like:
- a checklist for what PR/Finance needs to approve the staggered PO
- expected turnaround time if we submit docs this week
- single-person contact who can shepherd the vendor onboarding steps

I can loop internal finance (cc'd) once we have the checklist. Appreciate a crisp plan — Globex is tying seat increase (from 200k to 350k tokens/month equivalent) to the renewal.

Thanks,
Lori Chen
Procurement Lead, Globex Corp

--
Attachment: procurement_request_form.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Lori Chen <lori.chen@globex.com>

Subject: Re: Renewal kickoff & onboarding acceleration request

Lori — thanks for the clear ask. High-level proposal below and attaching a one-page mutual action plan to make procurement/finance signoff straightforward.

Summary of path:
1) Vendor re-onboarding in Redwood Procurement Portal (link below) — requires SOC2 evidence index, updated PO/POB (purchase order/billing contact), and updated tax docs. Portal submission typically validates-in 48-72 hours for basic docs; evidence review for SOC2 artifacts can add 3-5 business days.
2) Staggered PO approach — we can accept an initial PO for 60% of committed seats and a top-up PO for the remainder to be issued within 90 days of renewal start. Legal has pre-approved this pattern for customers of your size (we'll attach the SOW addendum).
3) Single point liaison — Jordan will own the portal handoff and will be the escalation for any evidence gaps. Marissa will oversee finance/legal coordination for the PO split. Evelyn will track SOC2 evidence items and confirm the evidence index.

Immediate next steps I suggest (mutual action plan):
- Today: Lori to confirm procurement email and attach the initial PO draft + W-9 (if available).
- By 14 Apr: Jordan will create vendor profile in portal and send portal invite to Globex procurement.
- By 16 Apr: Globex uploads SOC2 evidence index + any requested artifacts.
- By 20 Apr: Redwood validates the evidence index and sends SOW addendum (redlines allowed via DocuSign).
- By 28 Apr: Finance signoff on initial PO (60%) and DocuSign complete.

Attachments: MUTUAL_ACTION_PLAN_Globex.xlsx; I'll also include the SOW redlines after legal finalizes.

If this timeline works, Jordan will start the portal invite tomorrow morning.

Best,
Aditya Rao

Redwood Inference

source.subject: Pilot scope & incremental top-up option for Rivermark trial
messages:
From: Ava Simons <ava@rivermark.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 5 Apr 2026 09:07:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot scope & top-up option

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for last week's demo — the team liked the latency numbers and the routing suggestion for multi-model fallbacks. We're ready to run the 4-week timeboxed pilot, but a couple of asks before we lock the start date: 

- Can we add a one-week "top-up" option that we can trigger mid-pilot if we run into higher-than-expected volumes? We'd prefer a short automatic extension window rather than negotiating a contract amendment.
- If we use the top-up, can Redwood offer a promotional rate for that incremental week (we're a Series A with limited runway)?

Attached our SOW draft and a quick traffic estimate. Can you outline how the billing would work if we exercise the top-up? Also, we may need a short note on data handling for compliance.

Appreciate it — would like to start by Apr 12 if possible.

Best,
Ava
Ava Simons
Head of Integrations, Rivermark.ai
ava@rivermark.ai

Attachments: Rivermark-SOW-v1.pdf (application/pdf), pilot-usage-estimate.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Ava Simons <ava@rivermark.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2026 10:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot scope & incremental top-up option

Hi Ava — thanks for the clear notes and the SOW. Short answers up front: yes on a one-week top-up, and we can offer a discounted incremental rate if we get a commitment signal to convert to an annual contract within 30 days after the pilot.

How we typically structure it: the 4-week timebox is a fixed pilot fee that covers baseline provisioning and support. The top-up is treated as a separate one-week add-on with metered charges; for this situation I can propose a 20% discount on the top-up week if Rivermark signs a 12-month commitment (we'd put that in a simple amendment). If you don't convert, the top-up would be billed at standard metered rate.

I'll loop in Marissa (sales lead) to confirm we can approve the 20% concession and Jordan from contracting if we need an amendment template. Also tagging our compliance note: data residency and retention will follow the Private deployment standard in the SOW if required — we can add a one-paragraph addendum.

Proposed next steps:
1) You confirm start date (Apr 12) and opt-in language for the top-up (simple one-line trigger email to a dedicated alias).
2) I put together a short amendment with the 20% top-up rate conditional on a 12-month commitment.
3) We schedule a kickoff and compliance checklist call.

If that works I can get the amendment drafted today.

Cheers,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

---
On Sun, Apr 5, 2026 at 09:07 AM Ava Simons <ava@rivermark.ai> wrote:
> Hi Avery,
> 
> Thanks again for last week's demo — the team liked the latency numbers and the routing suggestion for multi-model fallbacks. We're ready to run the 4-week timeboxed pilot, but a couple of asks before we lock the start date: 
> 
> - Can we add a one-week "top-up" option...
>

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 5 Apr 2026 11:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot scope & incremental top-up option (approval needed)

Avery — quick internal. I read the ask and 20% on the top-up is acceptable if we get the conversion signal within 30 days post-pilot. A couple of operational notes:

- Make the top-up trigger a one-line opt-in email from an authorized Rivermark contact so CS can scale machines without delay.
- Add a clause that the top-up consumes reserved capacity only if capacity exists; otherwise fallback to hosted pool with documented latency SLO differences.

Jordan, please prep a one-page amendment template that references the pilot SOW and the conditional 20% concession. No need for legal redlines right now — keep it ops-focused. Once Avery sends the draft amendment to Ava, I'll sign off and we can route for e-sign.

Marissa
Director, Sales Operations
Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.com

> From: Ava Simons <ava@rivermark.ai>
> Date: Sun, Apr 5, 2026 at 9:07 AM
> Subject: Pilot scope & incremental top-up option
> To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
> 
> (original message)

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.com>
To: Ava Simons <ava@rivermark.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 6 Apr 2026 15:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot scope & incremental top-up option

Hi Ava — quick update and the attached amendment draft. Thanks for confirming Apr 12 as the start. We've added: 

- One-week timebox top-up, exercisable by a one-line opt-in email from an authorized Rivermark contact to pilots@redwood.com.
- Conditional promotional rate: top-up billed at 20% off list if Rivermark signs a 12-month host + support commitment within 30 days after pilot completion.
- Operational note: top-up will use Dedicated pool capacity where available; if we cannot provision dedicated nodes, the top-up will run on hosted capacity with a documented SLO caveat (included in the amendment).

Ava — attaching the short amendment (Rivermark-pilot-amendment-v1.pdf) and a quick one-page compliance note. If you approve, reply to this thread with a one-line "Rivermark opts in to top-up terms" and we'll lock the start date and schedule the kickoff.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments: Rivermark-pilot-amendment-v1.pdf (application/pdf), pilot-compliance-note.txt (text/plain)

> > Hi Ava — thanks for the clear notes and the SOW. Short answers up front: yes on a one-week top-up...

source.messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Li <samantha.li@atlaspayments.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-29T10:12:00-07:00

messages:
From: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Interested in a quick product walkthrough

Hi Ava,

Signed up for a trial this morning and landed on the self-serve page — curious to see how Redwood handles our inference patterns (high throughput, short-context reranks). I grabbed an available 30m slot on your calendar for today at 10am PT.

If that slot doesn’t work, happy to move. Goal is a quick look at:
- typical latency/throughput for batch scoring
- deployment options (dedicated vs private)
- pricing guardrails for spikes

Thanks,
Samir
Product Lead, Orchard BI
(408) 555-0199

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 30 Mar 2026 09:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Interested in a quick product walkthrough

Hi Samir — welcome, and thanks for signing up. I confirmed the 10:00am PT slot and sent a calendar invite (attached).

Agenda (30m):
- 10m: Orchard BI use case & current constraints
- 10m: Demo of Redwood Dedicated and quick perf estimates
- 10m: Next steps (trial runway, PoC guardrails)

Quick logistics:
- Join: https://meet.redwood.ai/ava-demo (passcode on invite)
- If anything changes, reply to this thread or reschedule via the calendar link.

Attaching a one-pager with how we measure latency/cost so you can skim ahead. Jordan (AE) is cc’d and will handle contract/pricing questions if we get into terms.

Looking forward to it — see you shortly.

Best,
Ava Chen
SDR, Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0101
(attachment: demo-invite.ics)
(attachment: redwood-onboarding-one-pager.pdf)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 31 Mar 2026 10:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Missed — quick check in

Samir — we missed you on the demo a few minutes ago. No problem if something came up. I can: 
- reschedule for another 30m this week; or
- send a 15m technical sync with Jordan for perf/pricing detail only.

Proposed times (PT):
- Wed 4/1 10:00am
- Thu 4/2 11:30am
- Fri 4/3 9:30am

Or paste times that work and I’ll lock it in. If you prefer self-serve, here’s my public booking link: https://cal.redwood.ai/j/ava-30min

If this is no longer a priority, quick reply with “not now” and I’ll close the loop.

Thanks — Ava

From: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2026 08:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Missed — quick check in

Hey Ava — sorry, got pulled into an all-hands. Can we do Thu 4/2 at 11:30am PT? Prefer to do the full 30 if Jordan can join. Also — could you share any short perf benchmarks for short-context reranking ahead of the call?

Thanks,
Samir

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Missed — quick check in

Hi Samir — Jordan here (AE). 11:30am PT on Thu 4/2 works — I’ll join and bring perf slides. 

Quick answers for the rerank case (high level):
- Typical p50 token latency (128-token KV cache path): ~12–18ms on a single A100 for batch=1; improves with batching.
- For throughput-heavy scoring, we recommend Dedicated + prefix caching; customers often move to quantized weights for cost savings.

I’ll include a 1-slide perf summary and an example config for batching so you have something to share with the infra team. See you Thursday.

— Jordan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
(415) 555-0123

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Bcc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2026 10:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Quick follow — materials and link for today

Samir — thanks for confirming. I attached the invite for 11:30am PT (demo link + passcode) and Jordan’s one-slide perf summary is linked here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cPERF (view-only).

If anything changes last minute, reply to this thread and I’ll send an updated invite. Otherwise, we’ll use the agenda from the original note and keep the demo tightly scoped to your reranking needs.

See you at 11:30 — Ava
(attachment: demo-invite.ics)

From: Samir Patel <samir@orchardbi.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2026 11:55:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Quick follow — materials and link for today

Post-demo note — great overview, Jordan. A couple of follow-ups from our infra team:
- Could you share an example autoscaling policy for Dedicated capacity?
- Any guidance on retention settings for KV caches (we have bursty windows overnight)?

Also looping in our Eng manager (Anika Rao) — anika@orchardbi.com — to coordinate PoC timelines.

Thanks,
Samir

source.subject: Throughput & Latency certification — collab and next steps
source.raw_thread:
From: Samuel Rhodes <sam.rhodes@fastcart.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Mar 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: FastCart benchmarking kickoff — targets and first-run request

Hannah —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Attached is our initial onboarding plan and sample payload set. High level targets for certification: P95 latency <= 200ms, P99 <= 400ms for 512-token requests; sustained throughput target 120 req/s with 15% headroom for short bursts up to 240 req/s.

We would like Redwood to run an acceptance benchmark with these targets against the model variant you recommend for production. Can you confirm:
- recommended model variant and quantization profile for cost/latency tradeoffs
- expected GPU profile you will run this on (type + count)
- what telemetry you can export (metrics, traces, token-level timings)

I've attached the payload sample (sample-payloads.zip) and a short notes doc. We'd like a first run next week (any morning PST).

Regards,
Sam Rhodes
Head of Engineering, FastCart
---
[Attachment: sample-payloads.zip (application/zip)]

---

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Samuel Rhodes <sam.rhodes@fastcart.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Mar 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FastCart benchmarking kickoff — targets and first-run request

Sam —

Thanks — this looks good and reasonable as a starting point. Proposed plan (quick summary):

1) Acceptance criteria (draft):
   - P95 latency <= 200ms (512 tokens)
   - P99 latency <= 400ms (512 tokens)
   - Sustained throughput 120 req/s for 30 minutes with no SLA errors
   - Error rate < 0.1% (5xx/timeout)

2) Test environment and config:
   - Model variant: redwood/gpt-like-13b-8bit (recommended for balanced latency/cost)
   - GPU footprint: 4x A40-equivalent GPUs in Dedicated onboarding pool (we will provision)
   - Quantization: 8-bit with KV-cache warmup enabled
   - Request mix: sample payloads you attached + stepdown load pattern to capture tail behavior

3) Telemetry and artifacts we will share:
   - Token-level latency CSV (p50/p95/p99), per-host CPU/GPU utilization, request traces (trace ids), and aggregated charts
   - Run artifacts: run-steps.md, raw load CSVs, fastcart-load-results.csv (post-run)

4) Schedule options (pick one):
   - Tue Mar 17 09:00–11:00 PST (preferred)
   - Wed Mar 18 13:00–15:00 PST

Action items for you: confirm preferred slot and provide any additional edge-case prompts you want included. We'll prepare the handoff checklist and instrument the run.

Attaching a handoff checklist we use for onboarding.

Thanks,
Hannah Schmitt
Senior Manager, Customer Success
Redwood Inference
---
[Attachment: handoff-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

---

From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Mar 2026 11:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: run configuration and telemetry checklist for FastCart

Team — quick internal note before we lock schedule with FastCart:

- We'll allocate the Dedicated onboarding pool (4x A40-equivalent). I have capacity available Tue morning.
- Jordan: please pre-bake a model variant with 8-bit quant and test kernel selection for 512-token sequence. Also add a KV-cache warmup step (first 2 minutes of low qps).
- Ben: keep the customer updated on any cost implications of the Dedicated run and confirm PO if needed for multi-day runs.
- I'll enable extended telemetry retention for the run (48h) so we can share traces and raw CSVs.

Checklist items to capture during the run: start/end timestamps, per-host GPU mem utilization, scheduler queue length, observed token generation time, and error traces. Add these to run-steps.md and attach to the post-run bundle.

Monica
Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Samuel Rhodes <sam.rhodes@fastcart.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Mar 2026 13:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FastCart benchmarking kickoff — run steps and telemetry link

Sam — looping you in with the short run plan and telemetry link. We will:

1) Warmup phase (5 min, 10 req/s) to populate KV cache and blow away cold-starts.
2) Ramp to steady-state (20 min) at 120 req/s with synthetic pacing matching your payload distribution.
3) Burst cycles (4x 30s) to 240 req/s to validate headroom.
4) Cooldown and artifact collection (10 min).

I'll attach run-steps.md with the exact curl commands and the instrumentation toggles. Telemetry export will land at the SharePoint link below after the run. Please confirm you are good with Tue 09:00 PST.

Links: https://redwood-inference.sharepoint.com/teams/onboard/FastCart/telemetry-2026

Jordan Blake
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
---
[Attachment: run-steps.md (text/markdown)]

---

From: Samuel Rhodes <sam.rhodes@fastcart.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Mar 2026 13:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FastCart benchmarking kickoff — first-run results attached

Jordan / Hannah —

We ran the first verification pass this morning and attached the raw CSV. Summary: steady-state throughput held at ~118 req/s (close to target) but we saw a P99 spike up to 540ms around minute 12 (burst window). P95 remained ~210ms. Error rate was 0.06%.

Steps we took: repeated the run twice, cleared client-side caches, re-sent identical payloads. The spike seems correlated with a scheduler queue starve event (see trace ids in CSV). Can you advise on likely causes and recommended mitigations? We are blocked from signing off until the P99 is under 400ms.

Attached: fastcart-load-results.csv

Sam
---
[Attachment: fastcart-load-results.csv (text/csv)]

---

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Samuel Rhodes <sam.rhodes@fastcart.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Mar 2026 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FastCart benchmarking kickoff — remediation plan and next run

Sam — thank you for the run artifacts and the detailed CSV. Short update and proposed next steps: 

Root-cause hypothesis (what we saw):
- Trace correlation shows a brief scheduler stall on host-2 during your burst window which created a queue backlog; that produced tokenization serialization and increased tail latency for a subset of requests.

Immediate mitigations we will execute before the next run:
1) Apply KV-cache pre-warm across all nodes for 3 minutes prior to ramp to avoid cold fills.
2) Increase per-host worker threads by 10% to reduce scheduling backlog during burst windows.
3) Add a short adaptive-batching window (50ms) to smooth microbursts while keeping p95 tight.

Proposed follow-up run: Thu Mar 19 09:00–10:30 PST (we've reserved capacity). We'll instrument additional traces and provide a pre-run smoke-check.

Action items:
- Sam: confirm Thu 09:00 PST works for FastCart and whether you want us to include the additional edge-case prompts (we can flip them on).
- Jordan: please prepare the updated run bundle and the pre-run smoke-check script.
- Ben: please confirm whether we should proceed under the current onboarding PO or convert to a short invoice for the Dedicated run window.

We will share the post-run artifacts to the SharePoint link and a short executive summary with the P95/P99 tables.

Thanks — we are on this.

Hannah Schmitt
Senior Manager, Customer Success
Redwood Inference
---

[End of thread]

messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Ellen Park <ellen@novusretail.com>, Carlos Ruiz <c.ruiz@novusretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-03-04T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up & draft pilot plan (post-demo)

Hi Ellen / Carlos —

Thanks again for the demo time today. Good conversation — I captured the key outcomes and a short draft pilot plan below so we can move to a concrete kickoff next week.

Quick summary from my notes:
- Goal: validate Redwood inference for recommendation reranking and customer search relevance for the holiday catalog
- Primary success metrics you called out: relevance uplift vs current baseline (precision@10), API latency (p95), and total inference cost per 1k queries
- Stakeholders: Ellen (product), Carlos (ML/ops), Procurement TBD

Draft pilot (high level):
- Scope: integrate Redwood API for a single classification/reranking endpoint, run production-like load on a 3-week trial window
- Timeline: Kickoff workshop (week 0), 2 full weeks of running evaluation + weekly check-ins, final readout in week 3
- Proposed kickoff window: Mar 9–13 — I suggest Mar 11 at 10:00 AM PT for the initial workshop
- Weekly cadence: 30-minute check-ins (status, blockers, metrics)
- Success criteria (proposal):
  1) +5% precision@10 on the test prompt set compared to baseline
  2) API p95 latency <= 300ms for the test distribution
  3) Cost projection aligns with your $/1k target (we'll produce a burn estimate)

Next steps I need from you to finalize schedule:
1) Confirm the success criteria above (add/remove items)
2) Confirm stakeholders who should be at kickoff (prod/ops/procurement)
3) Any non-functional constraints (data residency, firewall, VPN) we should know about

I attached a lightweight POC template (Novus_POC_Template.xlsx) with milestones and owner slots — please fill the owner column where you can.

If that plan looks roughly right I’ll loop in our eng leads (Marissa and Jordan) to assign owners and lock dates.

Thanks —
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: Novus_POC_Template.xlsx (spreadsheet, 1 sheet)

To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Carlos Ruiz <c.ruiz@novusretail.com>
Date: 2026-03-05T09:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up & draft pilot plan (post-demo)

Thanks Alex — this is really helpful and aligned with our thinking. A few quick notes and confirmations from our side:

Success criteria (confirmed / clarifications):
- Business: target +5% precision@10 on the test set (we'll provide the labeled eval set)
- Performance: p95 latency target to start at 350ms (we can tighten once we see data) — is that reasonable on your end?
- Cost: we need a projected burn estimate for a 2k qps equivalent during peak, broken out by model and instance type

Stakeholders for kickoff:
- Ellen Park (product)
- Carlos Ruiz (ML/ops)
- Lydia Chen (security) — she needs to attend the kickoff to sign off on data handling
- Procurement: I will loop in Kara after we finalize the scope

Constraints / notes:
- Data residency: all data during the pilot must remain in us-west region — no cross-region backups without approval
- We will not provide PII in the eval set; use synthetic PII placeholders if needed

Kickoff: Mar 11 at 10:00 AM PT works for Carlos and me. If that’s also valid for your team we can lock it.

Ellen
Ellen Park
Senior Product Manager, Search & Recommendations
Novus Retail
ellen@novusretail.com | +1 212-555-0987

source.subject: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners
source.body:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

We need an exec decision this week so we can finalize copy, update the console/checkout flows, and give Sales a clean story.

Context (TL;DR): Q1 experiments + customer feedback show our current plan boundaries are confusing (included usage vs overage; billed vs raw tokens; caching/retries). Meter hardening is in-flight; we must keep analyses segmented pre/post meter fixes and avoid anything that could look like retroactive billing changes.

Decisions requested (please reply with A/B/C choices + any hard “no’s”):

1) Free tier + overage behavior (self-serve)
A. Keep free tier as-is, but clarify billed tokens + caching/retries; tighten overage copy; no pricing changes.
B. Reduce free tier included tokens by ~30% and introduce hard cap on high-cost models unless card-on-file; keep overage rates.
C. Keep free tier, but introduce “soft cap” (throttle + upgrade prompts) and require card-on-file for streaming on premium models.

2) Packaging structure (self-serve + sales assist bridge)
A. Current 3 tiers, adjust included usage amounts + overage. (Smallest change)
B. 2 tiers + usage-based overage, add a “Scale” add-on for Dedicated priority routing. (Moderate change)
C. Introduce model-class pricing bands (Standard vs Premium models), simplify tiers. (Biggest change; more code + comms)

3) Discount/credit guardrails
A. Tighten approvals only (no product change): enforce Finance approvals in process; do not alter UI.
B. Add product-level constraints: credit expiry visible in console + prevent stacking discounts beyond threshold.

4) Timing
A. Rollout beginning Mar 4 (first business week after meter v2 shadow compare week 2 completes)
B. Rollout beginning Mar 18 (gives more time for Legal language + SDK/doc updates; slower GTM)

Risks / constraints to factor:
- Meter changes: shadow billing compare is showing deltas for a subset of streaming + retry paths. We can’t interpret “pricing” conversion changes without the pre/post segmentation.
- Billing integrity: no retroactive invoice changes without Finance + Legal sign-off.
- Legal: usage definition language must match what the usage API + invoices present (billed tokens, cache attribution).
- Sales: we need a narrative that is stable for at least 2 quarters, otherwise we’ll keep getting procurement redlines.

Proposed owners (can adjust):
- Product: Nikhil + Chris (final spec + experiment readout + cohort caveats)
- Eng: Ava to staff (control plane + plan entitlements), Logan/Nadia (meter semantics), Cole (billing hooks)
- Finance: Laura/Emily (RevRec + invoice preview process), Rishi (margin model)
- Legal: Michael/Sofia (usage definitions + dispute window copy)
- GTM: Jordan + Soojin (enablement FAQ, objection handling)

Attachments:
- Q1_2026_Pricing_Packaging_Options_v4.pdf
- 2026Q1_Experiment_Readout_Draft_2026-02-17.pptx
- GM_Model_Assumptions_PostMeterFix_2026-02-18.xlsx

If we can align by EOD Thu, we can hit copy approval (PM-1865) and Sales enablement (PM-1860) next week.

Evelyn

—
Evelyn Hart
Chief Product Officer | Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

My takes from Finance/RevRec:

1) Free tier/overage: B or C are both OK *if* we are clear that we’re not changing historical billing and we don’t introduce any meter-definition ambiguity. I slightly prefer C (soft cap + card-on-file for premium streaming) because it reduces margin bleed without creating “we took away free tokens” backlash.

2) Packaging: A or B. Please avoid C right now. Model-class pricing bands + meter changes in the same window is asking for disputes.

3) Guardrails: B (product-level constraints). Process-only guardrails keep failing in edge cases, and we keep cleaning up exceptions.

4) Timing: A is fine IF the shadow compare is stable and we have an invoice preview process (Finance needs 3 business days). If not, push to B.

Also: we need a firm policy on late-arriving usage events vs month-end close. Emily is drafting a checklist but it needs an exec “yes, this is the freeze window”.

Laura

—
Laura Bennett
CFO | Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 11:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

GTM lens:

- Strong preference for Packaging B. “2 tiers + Scale add-on” is easiest for AEs and avoids model-by-model pricing debates during procurement.
- For free tier: C. Requiring card-on-file for streaming on premium models is defendable and is already a common market pattern.
- Guardrails: B. But we need a clear exception path for enterprise (e.g., strategic deals) with documented approvals.
- Timing: A only if we can get the updated FAQ + legal usage definition into a single link we can send customers. Right now the story is fragmented.

Also: please don’t change names of units in the UI unless we’re 100% aligned on “billed tokens” vs “raw tokens”. We’ve got an active thread from Sales on how to explain it.

Jordan

—
Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer | Redwood Inference


From: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 12:07 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

Legal POV:

- Please avoid Packaging C for this quarter. If we do model-class bands, we must update order forms + MSA usage definitions, and we’ll trigger redlines on “premium model classification” and change control.

- For free tier: C is workable, but the copy must be careful: “card-on-file required to continue service” can read like a unilateral pricing change. Phrase as eligibility / anti-abuse + “to enable streaming on Premium models.”

- Guardrails: B is good and helps enforce what we say contractually (credits expiry; stacking limits). We need a standard clause for credit expiration + dispute windows that matches the actual invoice behavior.

- Timing: if we roll Mar 4, I need final customer-facing definitions no later than Feb 25 to review. Sofia can help turn around faster if the underlying definitions are stable.

Also, we should explicitly document that retries/fallback behavior is not double-billed (post fix) and that the Usage API is the canonical customer export for billed units.

Michael

—
Michael Grant
General Counsel | Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 2:22 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

Eng feasibility notes:

- Packaging A is very low risk.
- Packaging B is doable in the Mar 4 window if we freeze scope to: entitlement checks, plan display, and billing plan IDs. Anything that requires “new unit of measure” or rewriting historical usage exports is out.
- Packaging C touches too many surfaces (routing policy exposure, model catalog labeling, and pricing table selection) and would create a long tail of edge cases.

Meter caveat: we can support the product-level guardrails (credit stacking constraints, expiry surfacing) without touching the core usage meter logic.

However, the streaming retry fix and region dimension fix are still in rollout. I’m comfortable with Mar 4 only if we:
1) keep shadow billing compare running through end of Feb, and
2) explicitly annotate dashboards for the meter-change window so Finance/Product don’t mix cohorts.

If we pick Mar 18, we can reduce the risk of “pricing change” being blamed for meter behavior.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP Engineering | Redwood Inference


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 19, 2026 at 9:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

Thanks all. I’m going to propose the following decision set unless there are hard objections by noon:

- Free tier: C (soft cap + require card-on-file to enable streaming on Premium models)
- Packaging: B (2 tiers + “Scale” add-on)
- Guardrails: B (product-level constraints + visible credit expiry)
- Timing: Mar 18 rollout (B)

Rationale: aligns Sales story + reduces meter-change overlap risk. We’ll still ship docs/FAQ sooner to reduce current confusion.

Open items / owners:
1) Product spec (Nikhil/Chris): finalize tier definitions, included usage, overage behaviors, and exact UI language for “billed tokens” by Feb 23.
2) Legal (Michael/Sofia): review customer-facing wording + clause snippets by Feb 25.
3) Finance (Laura/Emily): confirm month-end usage freeze window + invoice preview timeline; publish checklist by Feb 26.
4) Eng (Ava): commit to Mar 18 release scope; confirm no changes require historical usage export changes.
5) GTM (Jordan): Sales enablement FAQ + objection handling; draft by Feb 27 for review.

Rishi: please ensure the margin model sheet includes a sensitivity note for “soft cap reduces premium streaming usage” so we’re not surprised.

If anyone believes Mar 4 is still safe + better, reply with what would need to be true (explicit gates).

Evelyn


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>

title: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners
preview:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

We need an exec decision this week so we can finalize copy, update the console/checkout flows, and give Sales a clean story.

Context (TL;DR): Q1 experiments + customer feedback show our current plan boundaries are confusing (included usage vs overage; billed vs raw tokens; caching/retries). Meter hardening is in-flight; we must keep analyses segmented pre/post meter fixes and avoid anything that could look like retroactive billing changes.

Decisions requested (please reply with A/B/C choices + any hard “no’s”):

1) Free tier + overage behavior (self-serve)
A. Keep free tier as-is, but clarify billed tokens + caching/retries; tighten overage copy; no pricing changes.
B. Reduce free tier included tokens by ~30% and introduce hard cap on high-cost models unless card-on-file; keep overage rates.
C. Keep free tier, but introduce “soft cap” (throttle + upgrade prompts) and require card-on-file for streaming on premium models.

2) Packaging structure (self-serve + sales assist bridge)
A. Current 3 tiers, adjust included usage amounts + overage. (Smallest change)
B. 2 tiers + usage-based overage, add a “Scale” add-on for Dedicated priority routing. (Moderate change)
C. Introduce model-class pricing bands (Standard vs Premium models), simplify tiers. (Biggest change; more code + comms)

3) Discount/credit guardrails
A. Tighten approvals only (no product change): enforce Finance approvals in process; do not alter UI.
B. Add product-level constraints: credit expiry visible in console + prevent stacking discounts beyond threshold.

4) Timing
A. Rollout beginning Mar 4 (first business week after meter v2 shadow compare week 2 completes)
B. Rollout beginning Mar 18 (gives more time for Legal language + SDK/doc updates; slower GTM)

Risks / constraints to factor:
- Meter changes: shadow billing compare is showing deltas for a subset of streaming + retry paths. We can’t interpret “pricing” conversion changes without the pre/post segmentation.
- Billing integrity: no retroactive invoice changes without Finance + Legal sign-off.
- Legal: usage definition language must match what the usage API + invoices present (billed tokens, cache attribution).
- Sales: we need a narrative that is stable for at least 2 quarters, otherwise we’ll keep getting procurement redlines.

Proposed owners (can adjust):
- Product: Nikhil + Chris (final spec + experiment readout + cohort caveats)
- Eng: Ava to staff (control plane + plan entitlements), Logan/Nadia (meter semantics), Cole (billing hooks)
- Finance: Laura/Emily (RevRec + invoice preview process), Rishi (margin model)
- Legal: Michael/Sofia (usage definitions + dispute window copy)
- GTM: Jordan + Soojin (enablement FAQ, objection handling)

Attachments:
- Q1_2026_Pricing_Packaging_Options_v4.pdf
- 2026Q1_Experiment_Readout_Draft_2026-02-17.pptx
- GM_Model_Assumptions_PostMeterFix_2026-02-18.xlsx

If we can align by EOD Thu, we can hit copy approval (PM-1865) and Sales enablement (PM-1860) next week.

Evelyn

—
Evelyn Hart
Chief Product Officer | Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

My takes from Finance/RevRec:

1) Free tier/overage: B or C are both OK *if* we are clear that we’re not changing historical billing and we don’t introduce any meter-definition ambiguity. I slightly prefer C (soft cap + card-on-file for premium streaming) because it reduces margin bleed without creating “we took away free tokens” backlash.

2) Packaging: A or B. Please avoid C right now. Model-class pricing bands + meter changes in the same window is asking for disputes.

3) Guardrails: B (product-level constraints). Process-only guardrails keep failing in edge cases, and we keep cleaning up exceptions.

4) Timing: A is fine IF the shadow compare is stable and we have an invoice preview process (Finance needs 3 business days). If not, push to B.

Also: we need a firm policy on late-arriving usage events vs month-end close. Emily is drafting a checklist but it needs an exec “yes, this is the freeze window”.

Laura

—
Laura Bennett
CFO | Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 11:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

GTM lens:

- Strong preference for Packaging B. “2 tiers + Scale add-on” is easiest for AEs and avoids model-by-model pricing debates during procurement.
- For free tier: C. Requiring card-on-file for streaming on premium models is defendable and is already a common market pattern.
- Guardrails: B. But we need a clear exception path for enterprise (e.g., strategic deals) with documented approvals.
- Timing: A only if we can get the updated FAQ + legal usage definition into a single link we can send customers. Right now the story is fragmented.

Also: please don’t change names of units in the UI unless we’re 100% aligned on “billed tokens” vs “raw tokens”. We’ve got an active thread from Sales on how to explain it.

Jordan

—
Jordan Blake
Chief Revenue Officer | Redwood Inference


From: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 12:07 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

Legal POV:

- Please avoid Packaging C for this quarter. If we do model-class bands, we must update order forms + MSA usage definitions, and we’ll trigger redlines on “premium model classification” and change control.

- For free tier: C is workable, but the copy must be careful: “card-on-file required to continue service” can read like a unilateral pricing change. Phrase as eligibility / anti-abuse + “to enable streaming on Premium models.”

- Guardrails: B is good and helps enforce what we say contractually (credits expiry; stacking limits). We need a standard clause for credit expiration + dispute windows that matches the actual invoice behavior.

- Timing: if we roll Mar 4, I need final customer-facing definitions no later than Feb 25 to review. Sofia can help turn around faster if the underlying definitions are stable.

Also, we should explicitly document that retries/fallback behavior is not double-billed (post fix) and that the Usage API is the canonical customer export for billed units.

Michael

—
Michael Grant
General Counsel | Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Stone <emily_stone@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil_sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Chris Osei <chris_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 18, 2026 at 2:22 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec decision needed: Q1 pricing/packaging changes (Hosted API) + rollout owners

Eng feasibility notes:

- Packaging A is very low risk.
- Packaging B is doable in the Mar 4 window if we freeze scope to: entitlement checks, plan display, and billing plan IDs. Anything that requires “new unit of measure” or rewriting historical usage exports is out.
- Packaging C touches too many surfaces (routing policy exposure, model catalog labeling, and pricing table selection) and would create a long tail of edge cases.

Meter caveat: we can support the product-level guardrails (credit stacking constraints, expiry surfacing) without touching the core usage meter logic.

However, the streaming retry fix and region dimension fix are still in rollout. I’m comfortable with Mar 4 only if we:
1) keep shadow billing compare running through end of Feb, and
2) explicitly annotate dashboards for the meter-change window so Finance/Product don’t mix cohorts.

If we pick Mar 18, we can reduce the risk of “pricing change” being blamed for meter behavior.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP Engineering | Redwood Inference

- Free tier: C (soft cap + require card-on-file to enable streaming on Premium models)
- Packaging: B (2 tiers + “Scale” add-on)
- Guardrails: B (product-level constraints + visible credit expiry)
- Timing: Mar 18 rollout (B)

Rationale: aligns Sales story + reduces meter-change overlap risk. We’ll still ship docs/FAQ sooner to reduce current confusion.

Open items / owners:
1) Product spec (Nikhil/Chris): finalize tier definitions, included usage, overage behaviors, and exact UI language for “billed tokens” by Feb 23.
2) Legal (Michael/Sofia): review customer-facing wording + clause snippets by Feb 25.
3) Finance (Laura/Emily): confirm month-end usage freeze window + invoice preview timeline; publish checklist by Feb 26.
4) Eng (Ava): commit to Mar 18 release scope; confirm no changes require historical usage export changes.
5) GTM (Jordan): Sales enablement FAQ + objection handling; draft by Feb 27 for review.

Rishi: please ensure the margin model sheet includes a sensitivity note for “soft cap reduces premium streaming usage” so we’re not surprised.

If anyone believes Mar 4 is still safe + better, reply with what would need to be true (explicit gates).

Evelyn

source.subject: Fwd: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)
source.body:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 7:15 AM
Subject: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Root cause (RRB-17): when CRD migration partially applies, the rollback cleanup hook exits non-zero before clearing `installer-rollback-lock`. The controller treats rollback as failed and schedules another rollback attempt, which sees the lock and repeats. Net effect: "stuck rollback" / infinite rollback retries.

Patch plan:
- In rollback cleanup: clear lock in a `defer`-style best-effort step even if migration revert fails.
- Add lock TTL (annotation with timestamp) and refuse to retry rollback if lock older than N minutes; surface actionable status.
- Add metric `installer.rollback.loop_detected` + alert when >3 retries in 10m.

Workaround (current): delete CM `installer-rollback-lock` and restart installer-controller.

ETA: PR today, release in 2.9.1 if we can get a quick validation run.

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 8:03 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

+1. Please link the PR in INT-330 once up. Also can we update the upgrade runbook today with the safe command sequence + explicit warnings?

---

From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 8:37 AM
Subject: Re: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

I can add loop detection + the metric/alert while you do the cleanup/TTL logic. If 2.9.1 is tight, we can land the metric behind a feature flag.

---

source.subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back
source.body:
From: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 9:43 AM
Subject: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Hi Ava,

We attempted the 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0 upgrade last night. Its been "rolling back" for hours and never completes. The UI now says "Rollback in progress" indefinitely.

Can you advise? We can share logs.

Nina

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:01 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Thanks Nina  were looking. Can you attach the installer controller logs around the rollback start?

In the meantime: are you seeing any mention of a "rollback lock" or repeated rollback retries?

Ava

---

From: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:22 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Attached installer-controller-logs.txt. There are repeated lines like: "scheduling rollback retry" and it never clears.

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 10:48 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

This matches the stuck rollback / rollback loop we saw (likely RRB-17). Workaround: delete CM `installer-rollback-lock` in the redwood-private namespace, then restart installer-controller. Id recommend we share steps with guardrails + ask them to confirm name/namespace before deleting.

---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@acme-example.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 12, 2026 at 11:08 AM
Subject: Re: Urgent: Redwood Private upgrade keeps rolling back

Nina  we have a workaround that has unblocked this in our lab. If youre comfortable running a couple k8s commands, please do the following (and confirm youre in the *redwood-private* namespace):

1) `kubectl -n redwood-private delete cm installer-rollback-lock`
2) `kubectl -n redwood-private rollout restart deploy/installer-controller`

This should allow the rollback to complete. Please reply with the outcome + any remaining errors.

We are also tracking a patch to prevent the rollback loop from happening again.

Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
