source.subject: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms
related_links: Jira: INT-771 (marketplace docs URL redirect request)
source.body:
From: Elliot Price <elliot_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms

Hey Alex — we’re at the point where AWS + GCP partner portals want “final” customer-facing URLs (security overview + onboarding steps) and they’re validating links during review.

Two asks where we need Eng help (docs site / routing):

1) Redirects / stability
- We have older / legacy URLs floating around from the previous listing and from sales collateral.
- Can we get 301 redirects in place so there are no 404s during partner review?

Top legacy routes I’ve seen in the wild:
- /aws-marketplace
- /gcp-marketplace
- /marketplace
- /docs/marketplace
- /security

2) Navigation/IA (minor)
- We need the marketplace onboarding pages discoverable under “Getting started” (or at least accessible without guessing the URL).
- Also need canonical links (no trailing slash variance / avoid duplicate paths).

Proposed final URLs for submission forms:
- AWS Marketplace onboarding: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws/onboarding
- GCP Marketplace onboarding: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp/onboarding
- Marketplace security overview: https://docs.redwoodinference.com/security/marketplace
- Marketplace FAQ (procurement/billing/support): https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/faq

We already have content drafts in-flight (PR-3421/3422), but we need confirmation on the final URLs + redirect implementation timing.

If you can, please spin up/attach to an INT ticket for Eng. Partner submission target is early next week, so we’d ideally have redirects live by Monday.

Attaching a quick URL list + redirect map.

Thanks,
Elliot
—
Elliot Price
Docs Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachment: marketplace-docs-final-urls-2026-02-05.txt
Attachment: redirect-map-marketplace-legacy-to-new-2026-02-05.csv


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elliot Price <elliot_price@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 9:37 AM PST
Subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms

Yep — agree we should treat these as “hard” URLs for partner validation.

Two quick clarifications:
1) Do we want /marketplace/aws and /marketplace/gcp to be simple landing pages (with “Subscribe” + “How it works” short version), or just keep /onboarding as the entry?
2) For /security/marketplace, are we comfortable having that fully public, or do we need a “public summary” page that links out to gated evidence? (I know Security had opinions here.)

Action on my side:
- I’ll file INT-771 with the redirect list + nav request and ask for a docs-site engineer to implement 301s + ensure canonical routing.
- We can keep the content PRs separate; redirects/nav should be low risk.

If you confirm (1) and (2), I’ll bake them into the ticket.

Alex


From: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwoodinference.com>, Elliot Price <elliot_price@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 10:06 AM PST
Subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms

On (1): yes, we should have landing pages.
- https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws
- https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp

These can be lightweight and point to:
- onboarding
- FAQ
- (where relevant) security overview

On (2): we should publish a “marketplace security overview” publicly with careful language, and keep evidence links gated / “available on request.” That matches what AWS reviewers have been asking for + what Aisha/Olga have been aligned on.

Also +1 on redirecting /marketplace -> an index page (not a 404 or product marketing site). Partner reviewers seem to stay inside docs links.

Soojin


From: Elliot Price <elliot_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 10:22 AM PST
Subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms

Confirming:

(1) Landing pages — yes.
- /marketplace/aws
- /marketplace/gcp
- /marketplace (index)

(2) Security overview public — yes.
- /security/marketplace should be the “public summary” page.
- It can include: shared responsibility, data handling basics, encryption-at-rest/in-transit high level, audit log feature overview, retention defaults + “configurable,” and “evidence available on request.”
- No direct links to SOC 2 report, pen test, etc.

One more nuance: AWS form field is extremely picky. They asked for a “Security documentation URL” and then the reviewer clicked it live on the call.

Forwarding the relevant snippet below (from the AWS listing review sync notes):

---
On Tue, Jan 14, 2026 at 2:18 PM, aws-marketplace@amazon.com wrote:
> Please provide a customer-accessible Security Documentation URL that describes:
> - encryption key management options
> - audit logging availability
> - data retention/deletion
> - support and escalation paths
> The reviewer will validate the link during the submission review.
---

So the redirects matter a lot.

Thanks for filing INT-771.

Elliot


From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elliot Price <elliot_price@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 12:04 PM PST
Subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms

Filed and linked: INT-771

Ticket summary includes:
- Create marketplace index + AWS/GCP landing routes in docs
- Implement 301 redirects for legacy routes in your CSV
- Canonicalization: enforce non-trailing slash or consistent trailing slash (whichever the site currently uses), plus a single canonical per page
- Nav update: add “Cloud Marketplace” under Getting started

Proposed redirect behavior (high level):
- /aws-marketplace -> /marketplace/aws
- /gcp-marketplace -> /marketplace/gcp
- /docs/marketplace -> /marketplace
- /marketplace/aws/onboarding and /marketplace/gcp/onboarding are the final “how it works” pages

I asked for an ETA by EOD tomorrow; will update once Eng confirms.

Alex


From: Elliot Price <elliot_price@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Feb 5, 2026 at 3:47 PM PST
Subject: Re: Docs site changes needed for Marketplace (AWS/GCP): redirects + final URLs for submission forms

Perfect.

One tiny change request before Eng starts (to reduce churn in partner forms):
- Please make sure the URLs we submit are EXACTLY these (no later renames):
  - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws
  - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/aws/onboarding
  - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp
  - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/gcp/onboarding
  - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/marketplace/faq
  - https://docs.redwoodinference.com/security/marketplace

Also request: /marketplace should not redirect to marketing site. It needs to stay in docs.

Once we have redirect + nav live, I’ll update the AWS/GCP submission drafts and ping Partnerships that links are stable.

Elliot

source.subject: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up
messages:
From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 2 Feb 2026 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Hi Alex — thanks again for the call earlier today. Quick recap + a couple of follow-ups so we can size pricing and guardrails correctly before we draft an MPA.

Recap (from our discovery):
- Primary use: real-time summarization + suggestion engine in user flows.
- Current MAU estimate: ~120k (you mentioned seasonal spike windows).
- Average tokens per user session: ~700–900 (you estimate ~800).
- Peak burst behavior: 4–6x baseline for short windows during product announcements (~10–20 minutes).

Key asks for pricing/quotas calibration:
1) Confirm expected steady-state monthly token volume (order of magnitude is fine) — you mentioned ~100M tokens/month as a rough ballpark?
2) Budget range for inference spend (monthly) so we can map committed vs. on-demand pricing.
3) Tolerance for throttling vs. willingness to accept overage charges for short bursts.
4) Any procurement constraints for auto-scaling/short-term quota increases (PO, pre-authorized limit, or need for manual approval).

Preliminary thinking from our side: target unit economics and burst policy options we'll propose:
- Target list: $X per 1M tokens (we'll pick a number once you confirm budget/committed volume).
- Short-term burst allowance (auto-burst) vs. strict quota + overage.
- Overage pricing: typically a multiplier over committed unit price (commonly 1.25–1.75x depending on how pre-authorized the burst is).

Attached is a short modeling workbook we discussed (burst-modeling-v2.xlsx) — Karthik will follow up with a more concrete sketch of the guardrail options. If you can reply with the three items above (steady-state tokens, budget range, and approval model for temporary quota increases), we'll draft a recommended plan and an initial price target.

Next step: If it works for you, I can put together an MPA draft with a 90-day intro committed package and an explicit burst policy for your review.

Thanks,
Stephanie Nguyen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0123

[Attachment: burst-modeling-v2.xlsx]

From: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
Cc: FinApp Procurement <procurement@finapp.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Feb 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Thanks, Stephanie — appreciate the clear recap. Answers inline below.

1) Steady-state monthly tokens: we expect ~90–140M tokens/month in phase 1 (varying by campaign). Best guess: 110M.
2) Budget range: pilot budget is conservative — $10k–$30k/month. If results justify, we can expand to $60k–$120k/month after Q2. Need a clear ROI story to hit the higher band.
3) Approval model: procurement requires a PO for committed spend. For short-term quota increases, we can approve pre-authorized bursts up to a defined cap (finance needs dollar cap defined up front). Anything above that needs manual procurement approval (24–48h).

On bursts: typical pattern is 4–5x baseline for 10–15 minutes, sometimes sustained for up to 30 minutes during major campaigns. We need to avoid hard throttling in the UI — end-users see errors and support tickets spike.

Questions:
- How will overage billing work? Immediate per-token billing at higher unit? Or do you throttle until we sign a short-term increase?
- Any ability to set a soft-limit that alerts + allows a one-time auto-increase within a month?

I'll route procurement contact details if you need formal requirements for PO and invoices.

— Alex
Alex Morgan
Head of Product, FinApp
alex@finapp.com
+1 (646) 555-0199

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 3 Feb 2026 14:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Hi Alex — quick technical note on how Redwood handles burst/overage and a suggested pragmatic policy given your usage pattern.

How rate limits and burst windows work (brief):
- Each customer has a monthly committed token quota (tokens/month).
- We run a token-bucket style rate limiter at per-VM and global routing level to protect latency SLOs; this allows short bursts within a configured burst window.
- We track both steady-state consumption and rolling-interval peak consumption (1-min and 15-min windows).

Suggested policy for FinApp given 110M baseline + 4–5x short peaks:
1) Commit: 100M tokens/month (reserved capacity) — priced at a committed unit rate.
2) Auto-burst allowance: allow bursts up to 2x baseline for up to 30 minutes/h without immediate throttle; beyond that, consume against an auto-increase bucket (pre-authorized) up to +50% of committed (i.e., up to 150M) for short windows (48 hours) if finance allows.
3) Overage: Any consumption above pre-authorized bucket will be billed at 1.5x the committed unit price (simple one-tier overage). We can also offer a 2-tier model (1.25x for soft overage within 10% of cap, 1.5x beyond).
4) Alerts & controls: webhook alerts at 70/85/95% of committed quota + automated Slack/email alerts for bursts.

Example unit economics (illustrative):
- Committed unit price: $950 per 1M tokens (~$0.00095/token).
- Overage unit price (1.5x): $1,425 per 1M tokens.
- If you prefer a lower overage multiplier, we can structure a ramp (trial period with softer multipliers).

Process for short-term quota increases: send a signed MPA addendum or use our self-serve quota increase form (for customers with pre-authorized finance scope). The addendum can include an explicit dollar cap that procurement can sign off on — this is usually fastest for customers who want predictable billing.

I've attached a short ROA that models impact for 110M baseline with 2x and 4x bursts. Hope it helps for procurement conversations.

— Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.inference
[Attachment: ROA_Q1_burst_analysis.pdf]

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>, FinApp Procurement <procurement@finapp.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up — proposed MAP and next steps

Alex / Procurement — following up with a concrete mutual action plan and the MPA draft attached. High level proposal (for discussion):

- Pilot committed quota: 100M tokens/month (90-day pilot).
- Target committed unit price: $950 per 1M tokens (~$0.00095/token).
- Auto-burst policy: short auto-burst up to 2x baseline for up to 30 minutes per hour without throttle.
- Pre-authorized short-term increase: allows consumption up to 150M tokens (i.e., +50%) for up to 48 hours with automatic billing at the overage rate. Finance must confirm a pre-authorized dollar cap for this window.
- Overage terms: consumption beyond the pre-authorized bucket billed at $1,425 per 1M tokens (1.5x).
- Alerts & guardrails: webhooks at 70/85/95% and a hard-guard at 200% of committed with prior notice (to avoid a production outage).

Attached: MPA draft (MPA-draft-FinApp-2026-02-05.pdf) and Karthik’s ROA file. If procurement is comfortable with the pre-authorized cap approach, we can issue an addendum for signature and enable the self-serve quota increase for the campaign window.

Proposed next steps:
1) Procurement confirms whether FinApp can pre-authorize a dollar cap for short-term increases and provides PO terms.
2) Alex confirms committed quota target (100M is our recommendation based on your input).
3) We circulate MPA addendum for signature and turn on the auto-burst + alerts.

If that looks good, I’ll loop in our contracts team and draft the addendum. Happy to jump on a 20-min call with procurement this week to walk through the invoice/PO flow.

Best,
Stephanie Nguyen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0123

[Attachment: MPA-draft-FinApp-2026-02-05.pdf]

2) Alex confirms committed quota target (100M is our recommendation based on your input).
3) We circulate MPA addendum for signature and turn on the auto-burst + alerts.

If that looks good, I’ll loop in our contracts team and draft the addendum. Happy to jump on a 20-min call with procurement this week to walk through the invoice/PO flow.

Best,
Stephanie Nguyen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0123

text:
From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 2 Feb 2026 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Hi Alex — thanks again for the call earlier today. Quick recap + a couple of follow-ups so we can size pricing and guardrails correctly before we draft an MPA.

Recap (from our discovery):
- Primary use: real-time summarization + suggestion engine in user flows.
- Current MAU estimate: ~120k (you mentioned seasonal spike windows).
- Average tokens per user session: ~700–900 (you estimate ~800).
- Peak burst behavior: 4–6x baseline for short windows during product announcements (~10–20 minutes).

Key asks for pricing/quotas calibration:
1) Confirm expected steady-state monthly token volume (order of magnitude is fine) — you mentioned ~100M tokens/month as a rough ballpark?
2) Budget range for inference spend (monthly) so we can map committed vs. on-demand pricing.
3) Tolerance for throttling vs. willingness to accept overage charges for short bursts.
4) Any procurement constraints for auto-scaling/short-term quota increases (PO, pre-authorized limit, or need for manual approval).

Preliminary thinking from our side: target unit economics and burst policy options we'll propose:
- Target list: $X per 1M tokens (we'll pick a number once you confirm budget/committed volume).
- Short-term burst allowance (auto-burst) vs. strict quota + overage.
- Overage pricing: typically a multiplier over committed unit price (commonly 1.25–1.75x depending on how pre-authorized the burst is).

Attached is a short modeling workbook we discussed (burst-modeling-v2.xlsx) — Karthik will follow up with a more concrete sketch of the guardrail options. If you can reply with the three items above (steady-state tokens, budget range, and approval model for temporary quota increases), we'll draft a recommended plan and an initial price target.

Next step: If it works for you, I can put together an MPA draft with a 90-day intro committed package and an explicit burst policy for your review.

Thanks,
Stephanie Nguyen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0123

[Attachment: burst-modeling-v2.xlsx]

From: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
Cc: FinApp Procurement <procurement@finapp.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Feb 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Thanks, Stephanie — appreciate the clear recap. Answers inline below.

1) Steady-state monthly tokens: we expect ~90–140M tokens/month in phase 1 (varying by campaign). Best guess: 110M.
2) Budget range: pilot budget is conservative — $10k–$30k/month. If results justify, we can expand to $60k–$120k/month after Q2. Need a clear ROI story to hit the higher band.
3) Approval model: procurement requires a PO for committed spend. For short-term quota increases, we can approve pre-authorized bursts up to a defined cap (finance needs dollar cap defined up front). Anything above that needs manual procurement approval (24–48h).

On bursts: typical pattern is 4–5x baseline for 10–15 minutes, sometimes sustained for up to 30 minutes during major campaigns. We need to avoid hard throttling in the UI — end-users see errors and support tickets spike.

Questions:
- How will overage billing work? Immediate per-token billing at higher unit? Or do you throttle until we sign a short-term increase?
- Any ability to set a soft-limit that alerts + allows a one-time auto-increase within a month?

I'll route procurement contact details if you need formal requirements for PO and invoices.

— Alex
Alex Morgan
Head of Product, FinApp
alex@finapp.com
+1 (646) 555-0199

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 3 Feb 2026 14:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up

Hi Alex — quick technical note on how Redwood handles burst/overage and a suggested pragmatic policy given your usage pattern.

How rate limits and burst windows work (brief):
- Each customer has a monthly committed token quota (tokens/month).
- We run a token-bucket style rate limiter at per-VM and global routing level to protect latency SLOs; this allows short bursts within a configured burst window.
- We track both steady-state consumption and rolling-interval peak consumption (1-min and 15-min windows).

Suggested policy for FinApp given 110M baseline + 4–5x short peaks:
1) Commit: 100M tokens/month (reserved capacity) — priced at a committed unit rate.
2) Auto-burst allowance: allow bursts up to 2x baseline for up to 30 minutes/h without immediate throttle; beyond that, consume against an auto-increase bucket (pre-authorized) up to +50% of committed (i.e., up to 150M) for short windows (48 hours) if finance allows.
3) Overage: Any consumption above pre-authorized bucket will be billed at 1.5x the committed unit price (simple one-tier overage). We can also offer a 2-tier model (1.25x for soft overage within 10% of cap, 1.5x beyond).
4) Alerts & controls: webhook alerts at 70/85/95% of committed quota + automated Slack/email alerts for bursts.

Example unit economics (illustrative):
- Committed unit price: $950 per 1M tokens (~$0.00095/token).
- Overage unit price (1.5x): $1,425 per 1M tokens.
- If you prefer a lower overage multiplier, we can structure a ramp (trial period with softer multipliers).

Process for short-term quota increases: send a signed MPA addendum or use our self-serve quota increase form (for customers with pre-authorized finance scope). The addendum can include an explicit dollar cap that procurement can sign off on — this is usually fastest for customers who want predictable billing.

I've attached a short ROA that models impact for 110M baseline with 2x and 4x bursts. Hope it helps for procurement conversations.

— Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.inference
[Attachment: ROA_Q1_burst_analysis.pdf]

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.inference>
To: Alex Morgan <alex@finapp.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.inference>, FinApp Procurement <procurement@finapp.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Token economics & burst/overage follow-up — proposed MAP and next steps

Alex / Procurement — following up with a concrete mutual action plan and the MPA draft attached. High level proposal (for discussion):

- Pilot committed quota: 100M tokens/month (90-day pilot).
- Target committed unit price: $950 per 1M tokens (~$0.00095/token).
- Auto-burst policy: short auto-burst up to 2x baseline for up to 30 minutes per hour without throttle.
- Pre-authorized short-term increase: allows consumption up to 150M tokens (i.e., +50%) for up to 48 hours with automatic billing at the overage rate. Finance must confirm a pre-authorized dollar cap for this window.
- Overage terms: consumption beyond the pre-authorized bucket billed at $1,425 per 1M tokens (1.5x).
- Alerts & guardrails: webhooks at 70/85/95% and a hard-guard at 200% of committed with prior notice (to avoid a production outage).

Attached: MPA draft (MPA-draft-FinApp-2026-02-05.pdf) and Karthik’s ROA file. If procurement is comfortable with the pre-authorized cap approach, we can issue an addendum for signature and enable the self-serve quota increase for the campaign window.

Proposed next steps:
1) Procurement confirms whether FinApp can pre-authorize a dollar cap for short-term increases and provides PO terms.
2) Alex confirms committed quota target (100M is our recommendation based on your input).
3) We circulate MPA addendum for signature and turn on the auto-burst + alerts.

If that looks good, I’ll loop in our contracts team and draft the addendum. Happy to jump on a 20-min call with procurement this week to walk through the invoice/PO flow.

Best,
Stephanie Nguyen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.inference
+1 (415) 555-0123

source.subject: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4
source.body:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Sharing Week 3 performance summary + where we need explicit decisions to either (A) iterate or (B) expand budgets/coverage going into Week 4.

Readouts / dashboards
- Week 3 readout (retargeting): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Q9yX-week3-readout-retargeting
- Week 2 readout (console nudges): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1m8Kp-week2-readout-console-nudges
- Week 1 readout (email A/B): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a4V0-week1-readout-email-ab
- Activation funnel dashboard (source of truth): https://console.redwoodinference.com/analytics/dashboards/activation-funnel?range=last_14_days

1) Retargeting (Week 3): what happened
Launch scope
- Platforms: LinkedIn + Google Display (limited), both using UTMs per PM-670.
- Audiences: (i) site visitors (docs + pricing), (ii) signup-no-activation, (iii) activated-but-no-return.
- Guardrails: enterprise opp suppression list applied weekly (Jordan/Isabelle workflow); excluded known regulated domains (fintech/healthcare) per data handling note.

Spend + efficiency (first 5 days live)
- Total spend: $7.8k (cap was $10k for Week 3).
- Blended CPC: $3.41 (LinkedIn higher as expected; GDN lower).
- Landing page CTR (from ad click to “Create API key” page view): 18% (good; suggests landing alignment is decent).

Downstream impact (directional; attribution caveats)
- We are NOT taking “activation lift” as causal yet due to short window + view-through attribution noise.
- That said, among users who hit UTM-tagged landing pages, we see higher completion of “first successful request” within 7 days vs baseline cohorts.
  - Signup-no-activation audience: +3.2pp absolute on “first request within 7d” (small n, directional)
  - Activated-but-no-return audience: +2.1pp on “returned + made a request within 7d” (directional)

Creative learnings
- “Latency + reliability” headline variant is outperforming “lowest cost” in CTR and in downstream “API key created” events.
- The “developer-first / quickstart in 5 min” copy is doing well on GDN but needs better landing page match (right now it drops people on generic docs).

2) Email + Console: any deltas worth noting
Email onboarding v2 (continuing)
- Winner subject line from Week 1 is still holding: open rate stable; click-to-console stable.
- Support signal: we are still getting intermittent “didn’t receive onboarding email” tickets (SUP-24871). Ops is monitoring; deliverability ramp remains conservative.

Console nudges (Week 3 status)
- CTA completion remains strongest on “Create API key” + “Run first request” nudges.
- We saw one regression last week where a nudge state confused the API key flow (now fixed per support + Marissa/Dylan follow-up). If we’re expanding nudges to more cohorts, I want to confirm we’re comfortable with current QA/rollback.

3) Decisions needed (this week)
A) Retargeting — expand or iterate?
Option 1 (iterate, keep spend flat): Hold cap at $10k/week, focus on landing page refinement + creative iteration; maintain current audience set; keep holdout as-is.
Option 2 (expand): Raise cap to $20k/week and add lookalikes for “first request within 7d” converters.
- If we expand, I need explicit sign-off from Camila + Sales (Jordan/Stephanie) that suppression list process is sufficient and we’re comfortable with higher impression volume.

B) Audience rules — do we tighten exclusions further?
- Ask: should we add an additional hard exclusion for any domain present in the enterprise pipeline in last 90 days (not just active opps)? That will reduce reach but also reduce any risk of stepping on active enterprise motions.

C) Console nudges — graduate any to always-on in Week 4?
- Recommendation: graduate “Create API key” + “First request snippet” nudge to always-on for new self-serve signups (still keeping dismissal + frequency caps).
- Ask: Marissa/Paula — OK to proceed, or do we want to keep behind flag until the final consolidated analysis?

If we can resolve A/B/C by EOD Wed, we can make Week 4 a clean consolidation + final recommendation rather than trying to do last-minute changes.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Growth / Lifecycle Experiments
Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Thanks Ben.

Two questions:
1) For the +3.2pp in signup-no-activation, what’s the cohort size and what’s our current holdout design (true holdout vs platform-level holdout)?
2) If we go to $20k/week, what’s the expected incremental activated users and what’s the “stop” threshold if CPA drifts?

On nudges: I’m supportive of graduating API key + first request if we have rollback + we’re confident we’re not increasing support volume.

Camila


---

From: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Adding detail on holdout/attribution.

- Current setup is cohort-level holdout for lifecycle experiments (feature flag assignment) but *retargeting* can only be treated as quasi-experimental right now.
- We do have an internal “no-paid-touch” cohort for signup-no-activation based on eligibility rules, but it’s not a clean randomization because ad platforms have their own delivery constraints.

Re: cohort sizes (last 5 days):
- UTM-tagged landing page visitors from ads: ~1,140 sessions
- Matched to user_id (logged-in or signup within 24h): 312 users
- Of those, in signup-no-activation segment at time of first impression: 188 users

I can add a quick appendix to the Week 3 doc with the raw counts + caveats so it’s explicit.

Also: I recommend we set a guardrail on blended CAC/CPA using “first successful request” as the proxy (vs just signup). We can stop/roll back if CPA > 1.5x baseline for 3 consecutive days OR support tickets spike above a threshold.

Tyler
Marketing Ops & Attribution


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

From Sales side:

- I’m OK increasing spend *if* we keep the active opp suppression (updated weekly) and add a second check for “late-stage procurement” accounts (we can provide a list from HubSpot stage = Security Review / Legal / Procurement).
- I’m not in favor of blanket 90-day exclusion across the full enterprise pipeline. That will over-exclude and we’ll lose a ton of mid-market reach where PLG is actually useful.

Proposal:
- Exclude: active opps + late-stage procurement/security accounts + named sensitive list (Finchbank/Quantico-type regulated)
- Don’t exclude: early-stage exploratory enterprise unless there’s an active deal owner flagged.

If we expand, please keep the “Request a demo” CTA off retargeting for now. We should keep it developer-first (quickstart / API key) and let handoff happen via in-product intent signals.

Jordan


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:36 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

On nudges:

- I’m comfortable graduating “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for *new Hosted API self-serve signups*.
- Conditions:
  1) keep feature flag for immediate rollback,
  2) keep frequency cap + dismissal persistence,
  3) we should exclude users who already generated >100k tokens (don’t keep nagging them),
  4) ship with a short QA checklist for the edge cases we saw last week (SUP-24905 style).

Paula — can you confirm the event properties we need don’t change when we graduate to always-on? I don’t want to create a measurement discontinuity right before Week 4 analysis.

Marissa


---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Super helpful — thank you.

Proposed decision set (unless strong objections by tomorrow noon):

A) Retargeting
- Stay in “iterate” mode through end of Week 4 (cap stays $10k/week).
- Focus: landing page alignment for the developer-first creative + tighten the signup-no-activation audience definition (reduce wasted impressions).
- We’ll come out of Week 4 with a clearer expansion recommendation + expected CPA range.

B) Exclusions
- Adopt Jordan’s proposal: active opps + late-stage procurement/security stage exclusions + named sensitive list.
- We will not do a blanket 90-day exclusion.

C) Console nudges
- Graduate “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for brand new self-serve signups, with rollback + caps + >100k token exclusion as Marissa noted.
- Paula: we’ll keep the existing event taxonomy unchanged; if we need anything new, we’ll add it as an additional property (not renaming events) to avoid breaking Week 4 reporting.

Tyler: please add the appendix with raw counts + caveats to the Week 3 doc (even if it’s ugly) so Camila’s Q1 is fully answered.

Camila: re your Q2 (incremental activated users / stop threshold) — for now, given we’re holding spend flat, we’ll keep monitoring blended CPA using “first successful request” proxy. I’ll propose a concrete stop threshold in the Week 4 consolidated readout where we can normalize across channels (email + nudge + paid).

Soojin: if you have 30 mins tomorrow, I’d like your quick review on the top 2 winning ad headlines so we don’t drift from the hosted API onboarding messaging guidelines.

Ben

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 12:18 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

From Sales side:

- I’m OK increasing spend *if* we keep the active opp suppression (updated weekly) and add a second check for “late-stage procurement” accounts (we can provide a list from HubSpot stage = Security Review / Legal / Procurement).
- I’m not in favor of blanket 90-day exclusion across the full enterprise pipeline. That will over-exclude and we’ll lose a ton of mid-market reach where PLG is actually useful.

Proposal:
- Exclude: active opps + late-stage procurement/security accounts + named sensitive list (Finchbank/Quantico-type regulated)
- Don’t exclude: early-stage exploratory enterprise unless there’s an active deal owner flagged.

If we expand, please keep the “Request a demo” CTA off retargeting for now. We should keep it developer-first (quickstart / API key) and let handoff happen via in-product intent signals.

Jordan


---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 1:36 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

On nudges:

- I’m comfortable graduating “Create API key” + “Run first request” to always-on for *new Hosted API self-serve signups*.
- Conditions:
  1) keep feature flag for immediate rollback,
  2) keep frequency cap + dismissal persistence,
  3) we should exclude users who already generated >100k tokens (don’t keep nagging them),
  4) ship with a short QA checklist for the edge cases we saw last week (SUP-24905 style).

Paula — can you confirm the event properties we need don’t change when we graduate to always-on? I don’t want to create a measurement discontinuity right before Week 4 analysis.

Marissa

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 3:47 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Super helpful — thank you.

Proposed decision set (unless strong objections by tomorrow noon):

A) Retargeting
- Stay in “iterate” mode through end of Week 4 (cap stays $10k/week).
- Focus: landing page alignment for the developer-first creative + tighten the signup-no-activation audience definition (reduce wasted impressions).
- We’ll come out of Week 4 with a clearer expansion recommendation + expected CPA range.

B) Exclusions
- Adopt Jordan’s proposal: active opps + late-stage procurement/security stage exclusions + named sensitive list.
- We will not do a blanket 90-day exclusion.

C) Console nudges

text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Sharing Week 3 performance summary + where we need explicit decisions to either (A) iterate or (B) expand budgets/coverage going into Week 4.

Readouts / dashboards
- Week 3 readout (retargeting): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Q9yX-week3-readout-retargeting
- Week 2 readout (console nudges): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1m8Kp-week2-readout-console-nudges
- Week 1 readout (email A/B): https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a4V0-week1-readout-email-ab
- Activation funnel dashboard (source of truth): https://console.redwoodinference.com/analytics/dashboards/activation-funnel?range=last_14_days

1) Retargeting (Week 3): what happened
Launch scope
- Platforms: LinkedIn + Google Display (limited), both using UTMs per PM-670.
- Audiences: (i) site visitors (docs + pricing), (ii) signup-no-activation, (iii) activated-but-no-return.
- Guardrails: enterprise opp suppression list applied weekly (Jordan/Isabelle workflow); excluded known regulated domains (fintech/healthcare) per data handling note.

Spend + efficiency (first 5 days live)
- Total spend: $7.8k (cap was $10k for Week 3).
- Blended CPC: $3.41 (LinkedIn higher as expected; GDN lower).
- Landing page CTR (from ad click to “Create API key” page view): 18% (good; suggests landing alignment is decent).

Downstream impact (directional; attribution caveats)
- We are NOT taking “activation lift” as causal yet due to short window + view-through attribution noise.
- That said, among users who hit UTM-tagged landing pages, we see higher completion of “first successful request” within 7 days vs baseline cohorts.
  - Signup-no-activation audience: +3.2pp absolute on “first request within 7d” (small n, directional)
  - Activated-but-no-return audience: +2.1pp on “returned + made a request within 7d” (directional)

Creative learnings
- “Latency + reliability” headline variant is outperforming “lowest cost” in CTR and in downstream “API key created” events.
- The “developer-first / quickstart in 5 min” copy is doing well on GDN but needs better landing page match (right now it drops people on generic docs).

2) Email + Console: any deltas worth noting
Email onboarding v2 (continuing)
- Winner subject line from Week 1 is still holding: open rate stable; click-to-console stable.
- Support signal: we are still getting intermittent “didn’t receive onboarding email” tickets (SUP-24871). Ops is monitoring; deliverability ramp remains conservative.

Console nudges (Week 3 status)
- CTA completion remains strongest on “Create API key” + “Run first request” nudges.
- We saw one regression last week where a nudge state confused the API key flow (now fixed per support + Marissa/Dylan follow-up). If we’re expanding nudges to more cohorts, I want to confirm we’re comfortable with current QA/rollback.

3) Decisions needed (this week)
A) Retargeting — expand or iterate?
Option 1 (iterate, keep spend flat): Hold cap at $10k/week, focus on landing page refinement + creative iteration; maintain current audience set; keep holdout as-is.
Option 2 (expand): Raise cap to $20k/week and add lookalikes for “first request within 7d” converters.
- If we expand, I need explicit sign-off from Camila + Sales (Jordan/Stephanie) that suppression list process is sufficient and we’re comfortable with higher impression volume.

B) Audience rules — do we tighten exclusions further?
- Ask: should we add an additional hard exclusion for any domain present in the enterprise pipeline in last 90 days (not just active opps)? That will reduce reach but also reduce any risk of stepping on active enterprise motions.

C) Console nudges — graduate any to always-on in Week 4?
- Recommendation: graduate “Create API key” + “First request snippet” nudge to always-on for new self-serve signups (still keeping dismissal + frequency caps).
- Ask: Marissa/Paula — OK to proceed, or do we want to keep behind flag until the final consolidated analysis?

If we can resolve A/B/C by EOD Wed, we can make Week 4 a clean consolidation + final recommendation rather than trying to do last-minute changes.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Growth / Lifecycle Experiments
Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Thanks Ben.

Two questions:
1) For the +3.2pp in signup-no-activation, what’s the cohort size and what’s our current holdout design (true holdout vs platform-level holdout)?
2) If we go to $20k/week, what’s the expected incremental activated users and what’s the “stop” threshold if CPA drifts?

On nudges: I’m supportive of graduating API key + first request if we have rollback + we’re confident we’re not increasing support volume.

Camila


---

From: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Paula Mendes <paula.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Feb 3, 2026 at 11:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 3 readout: retargeting live + early activation impact — decisions needed for Week 4

Adding detail on holdout/attribution.

- Current setup is cohort-level holdout for lifecycle experiments (feature flag assignment) but *retargeting* can only be treated as quasi-experimental right now.
- We do have an internal “no-paid-touch” cohort for signup-no-activation based on eligibility rules, but it’s not a clean randomization because ad platforms have their own delivery constraints.

Re: cohort sizes (last 5 days):
- UTM-tagged landing page visitors from ads: ~1,140 sessions
- Matched to user_id (logged-in or signup within 24h): 312 users
- Of those, in signup-no-activation segment at time of first impression: 188 users

I can add a quick appendix to the Week 3 doc with the raw counts + caveats so it’s explicit.

Also: I recommend we set a guardrail on blended CAC/CPA using “first successful request” as the proxy (vs just signup). We can stop/roll back if CPA > 1.5x baseline for 3 consecutive days OR support tickets spike above a threshold.

Tyler
Marketing Ops & Attribution

source.subject: Launch brief: Lifecycle growth experiments (onboarding email v2 + console nudges)
source.messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Launch brief: Lifecycle growth experiments (onboarding email v2 + console nudges)

Team — consolidating launch details for the lifecycle growth experiments so we’re aligned on what’s going out, when, and how we’ll measure + escalate.

TL;DR
- Email onboarding v2: continuing A/B and expanding send ramp to broader new-signup cohort starting Wed.
- Console nudges: first experiment goes live behind feature flag to a targeted cohort (with holdout) starting Thu.
- Dashboards: activation funnel + TTFT are the source of truth for decisioning; we’ll use the same cohort defs across email + console.

What’s launching
1) Onboarding email v2 (A/B)
- Audience: new Hosted API signups (self-serve) with standard suppression rules (unsubscribed, bounces, internal domains).
- Segments (initial):
  A) New signup → no API key within 6h
  B) API key created → no first request within 24h
  C) First successful request → nudge to “first 100k tokens” milestone
- Variants: per PM-659; Soojin-approved copy only (no new copy changes during ramp).
- Guardrails:
  - Complaint rate: stop/hold if >0.08% rolling 24h
  - Hard bounce rate: stop/hold if >1.0% rolling 24h
  - Unsubscribe rate: investigate if >1.5% on any send
  - Support spikes: watch SUP-24871 tags (“didn’t receive onboarding email”)

2) Console onboarding nudges (experiment 1)
- Placement: Console home + API Keys page (only when user is eligible for the next step).
- Targeting: feature-flag cohort assignment with holdout group.
- Initial nudges we’re shipping:
  - “Create an API key” (if none exists)
  - “Make your first request” (if key exists, no request)
  - “Enable streaming” (after first request; only if SDK supports it)
- Dismissal: should persist for 14d (or until completion).
- Guardrails:
  - Nudge must never block key creation or navigation (see SUP-24905 learnings).

Timing / rollout plan
- Wed 1/28
  - Email ramp expansion: increase from limited cohort → 50% of eligible new signups (with holdout per PM-659).
  - Confirm deliverability monitoring cadence (Ben to own).
- Thu 1/29
  - Console nudge experiment: enable to 25% of eligible console sessions (with 10% holdout), expand to 50% if no UX issues in first 6h.
  - QA sign-off: Marissa to confirm checklist + backout.
- Mon 2/2
  - Consolidate Week 2 readout and decide “keep/iterate/kill” for first console nudge set.
- Retargeting
  - Not launching this week. Audience + UTMs locked this week; launch window remains Week 3 pending final exclusions from Sales.

Measurement (source of truth)
- Primary activation: % of new signups completing
  1) First successful request within 7d
  2) First 100k tokens within 7d
- Secondary:
  - Time to first token (TTFT)
  - Step completion rates (API key creation, first request)
  - D7 active usage

Dashboards
- Activation funnel (canonical): https://looker.redwoodinference.com/dashboards/activation-funnel
- TTFT + latency breakdown: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/hosted-api-onboarding/activation-and-ttft
- If anything looks off: please flag in-thread and tag Ben + Marissa; also open/attach to INT-3310 if it’s a data discrepancy.

Escalation paths
- Deliverability / sending config: Ben (first), then loop me + (per ENG-10518) for compliance hooks.
- Console UX regressions / nudge odd behavior: Marissa (first), then console eng on-call; file a SUP if it impacts customers.
- Eventing / funnel numbers not matching: open INT-3310 + tag Ben; we should assume instrumentation before interpretation.
- Sales exclusions / retargeting audience questions: Jordan (final say on “do not target” list).

Comms
- Please keep any “launch” announcements internal until we’ve validated metrics are stable for 24h.
- If you need to share externally (customers asking), use the approved phrasing: “We’re improving onboarding guidance in console and via optional lifecycle email; you can manage preferences any time.”

Attached: LifecycleGrowth_LaunchBrief_2026-01-27.pdf (same content, for forwarding to wider internal stakeholders if needed)

Camila
—
Camila Reyes
VP Growth (Exec Sponsor) | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 10:06 AM PT
Subject: Re: Launch brief: Lifecycle growth experiments (onboarding email v2 + console nudges)

Aligned.

For Wed ramp expansion:
- We’ll go to 50% of eligible new signups at 10:00am PT (post-morning reputation check), then reassess at ~4:00pm PT.
- Monitoring: I’ll watch complaint/bounce in SendGrid + the deliverability dashboard every 2 hours during business hours for first 48h.
- If we hit guardrails, we’ll:
  1) pause the newest segment first (usually “no first request within 24h”),
  2) then pause all onboarding sends if needed,
  3) and post in #marketing + #support with impact + ETA.

Also: we’re tagging SUP-24871 with “Lifecycle-Onboarding” so Support can bucket these.

-Ben

On Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Team — consolidating launch details for the lifecycle growth experiments so we’re aligned on what’s going out, when, and how we’ll measure + escalate.
> ...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 11:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Launch brief: Lifecycle growth experiments (onboarding email v2 + console nudges)

Confirmed on console side.

Rollout checklist for Thu 1/29:
- 8:30–9:30am PT: final QA pass in staging + prod smoke
  - verify nudge eligibility rules
  - verify “dismiss” persistence
  - verify CTA routes do not 404 and do not override existing banners
  - verify API key creation flow is unblocked end-to-end (explicitly checking prior SUP-24905 repro)
- 10:00am PT: enable to 25% of eligible sessions + 10% holdout (flag: lifecycle_growth_nudges_v1)
- 4:00pm PT: if no issues + nudge CTR is within expected band, expand to 50%

Backout plan:
- flip flag off (immediate)
- if needed, revert the UI component (Dylan said fastest path is flag-off; revert only if we see side effects)

I’ll post the “go/no-go” in #product and #eng-platform Thursday morning.

Marissa

On Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 2) Console onboarding nudges (experiment 1)
> - Placement: Console home + API Keys page ...


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Launch brief: Lifecycle growth experiments (onboarding email v2 + console nudges)

Thanks — the only add from Sales:

- For retargeting, we need the exclusion list enforced before any spend goes live (active opps + enterprise domains + “sensitive/regulated” accounts). I’ll send the first list by EOD Wed and propose a weekly cadence.
- Please make sure we’re not sending lifecycle emails to any accounts already in an active security review / procurement loop unless they explicitly opted into marketing (we’ve had confusion here in the past).

Otherwise good to proceed with email + console as scoped.

Jordan

On Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Retargeting
>  - Not launching this week. Audience + UTMs locked this week; launch window remains Week 3 pending final exclusions from Sales.

text:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Launch brief: Lifecycle growth experiments (onboarding email v2 + console nudges)

Team — consolidating launch details for the lifecycle growth experiments so we’re aligned on what’s going out, when, and how we’ll measure + escalate.

TL;DR
- Email onboarding v2: continuing A/B and expanding send ramp to broader new-signup cohort starting Wed.
- Console nudges: first experiment goes live behind feature flag to a targeted cohort (with holdout) starting Thu.
- Dashboards: activation funnel + TTFT are the source of truth for decisioning; we’ll use the same cohort defs across email + console.

What’s launching
1) Onboarding email v2 (A/B)
- Audience: new Hosted API signups (self-serve) with standard suppression rules (unsubscribed, bounces, internal domains).
- Segments (initial):
  A) New signup → no API key within 6h
  B) API key created → no first request within 24h
  C) First successful request → nudge to “first 100k tokens” milestone
- Variants: per PM-659; Soojin-approved copy only (no new copy changes during ramp).
- Guardrails:
  - Complaint rate: stop/hold if >0.08% rolling 24h
  - Hard bounce rate: stop/hold if >1.0% rolling 24h
  - Unsubscribe rate: investigate if >1.5% on any send
  - Support spikes: watch SUP-24871 tags (“didn’t receive onboarding email”)

2) Console onboarding nudges (experiment 1)
- Placement: Console home + API Keys page (only when user is eligible for the next step).
- Targeting: feature-flag cohort assignment with holdout group.
- Initial nudges we’re shipping:
  - “Create an API key” (if none exists)
  - “Make your first request” (if key exists, no request)
  - “Enable streaming” (after first request; only if SDK supports it)
- Dismissal: should persist for 14d (or until completion).
- Guardrails:
  - Nudge must never block key creation or navigation (see SUP-24905 learnings).

Timing / rollout plan
- Wed 1/28
  - Email ramp expansion: increase from limited cohort → 50% of eligible new signups (with holdout per PM-659).
  - Confirm deliverability monitoring cadence (Ben to own).
- Thu 1/29
  - Console nudge experiment: enable to 25% of eligible console sessions (with 10% holdout), expand to 50% if no UX issues in first 6h.
  - QA sign-off: Marissa to confirm checklist + backout.
- Mon 2/2
  - Consolidate Week 2 readout and decide “keep/iterate/kill” for first console nudge set.
- Retargeting
  - Not launching this week. Audience + UTMs locked this week; launch window remains Week 3 pending final exclusions from Sales.

Measurement (source of truth)
- Primary activation: % of new signups completing
  1) First successful request within 7d
  2) First 100k tokens within 7d
- Secondary:
  - Time to first token (TTFT)
  - Step completion rates (API key creation, first request)
  - D7 active usage

Dashboards
- Activation funnel (canonical): https://looker.redwoodinference.com/dashboards/activation-funnel
- TTFT + latency breakdown: https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/hosted-api-onboarding/activation-and-ttft
- If anything looks off: please flag in-thread and tag Ben + Marissa; also open/attach to INT-3310 if it’s a data discrepancy.

Escalation paths
- Deliverability / sending config: Ben (first), then loop me + (per ENG-10518) for compliance hooks.
- Console UX regressions / nudge odd behavior: Marissa (first), then console eng on-call; file a SUP if it impacts customers.
- Eventing / funnel numbers not matching: open INT-3310 + tag Ben; we should assume instrumentation before interpretation.
- Sales exclusions / retargeting audience questions: Jordan (final say on “do not target” list).

Comms
- Please keep any “launch” announcements internal until we’ve validated metrics are stable for 24h.
- If you need to share externally (customers asking), use the approved phrasing: “We’re improving onboarding guidance in console and via optional lifecycle email; you can manage preferences any time.”

Attached: LifecycleGrowth_LaunchBrief_2026-01-27.pdf (same content, for forwarding to wider internal stakeholders if needed)

Camila
—
Camila Reyes
VP Growth (Exec Sponsor) | Redwood Inference

source.subject: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint
source.body:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

Camila — requesting approval for a Q1 retargeting test budget to support the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation/early retention).

Summary / ask
- Approve a Q1 spend cap of $25,000 total for retargeting (Jan–Mar), with a staged release:
  - Phase 1 (Sprint Week 3–4): $7,500 test budget
  - Phase 2 (Feb): up to $10,000 contingent on hitting measurement guardrails
  - Phase 3 (Mar): up to $7,500 contingent on continued performance + no sales conflicts

Why now / ROI rationale (brief)
- We have meaningful “signup → no activation” volume and a consistent drop-off between API key creation and first successful request.
- Retargeting is intended to recover stalled developers at low marginal engineering cost.
- We’ll optimize toward cost per activated developer (7-day activation) and early usage signals (first 100k tokens), not just CTR.
- Even modest lift (1–2pp) on activation in the “signup-no-activation” cohort should pay back quickly vs. the spend, given downstream conversion to usage-based revenue and sales-assisted handoffs.

Measurement expectations (what we will report)
- Primary: incremental lift in 7-day activation rate (first successful request) vs. holdout, attributed via UTMs + internal event joins.
- Secondary: time-to-first-token (TTFT), first 100k tokens within 7 days, D7 active usage.
- Efficiency: cost per activated developer (CPA-A), cost per 100k tokens milestone (CPA-100k), and spend pacing.

Guardrails / policies
- Hard exclusions to avoid targeting active enterprise opportunities:
  - Suppress any domain/account present in Sales “active opp” list (Jordan to confirm weekly refresh cadence)
  - Suppress known enterprise domains + high-intent inbound leads already in sales motion
  - Frequency caps to avoid overexposure
- Compliance: only first-party event-based audiences permitted per policy; no sensitive category targeting.

Attachments
- Q1_Retargeting_Budget_Request_RedwoodInference.pdf
- Retargeting_Audiences_v3.xlsx
- Measurement_Expectations_OnePager.pdf

If approved, Tyler (Marketing Ops) will set spend caps + UTMs and we’ll share a weekly readout in the sprint office hours.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Growth Marketing, Redwood Inference


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

+1 with conditions on exclusions.

We can provide a weekly “active opp + sensitive accounts” suppression file (Mon AM). Two notes:
1) Please also exclude any accounts in late-stage security/procurement (even if they’re not marked “closed won” yet). We’ve had mixed reactions when ads show up during redlines.
2) Make sure we’re excluding by BOTH email domain and company name match where possible (some use personal domains early).

If we’re good on that, I’m aligned on the phased budget approach.

Jordan Blake
Sales Leadership, Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 11:21 AM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

Ben — thanks. I’m supportive in principle, but I want to be explicit on the “continue/stop” criteria before we open up Feb/Mar.

A few questions:
1) What’s the target threshold for CPA-A (cost per activated dev) that would justify Phase 2? And what would trigger an immediate pause?
2) How are we handling view-through vs. click-through attribution? I don’t want us over-claiming.
3) Reporting cadence: can we commit to a weekly snapshot + a final Q1 summary (spend, cohorts, incremental lift)?

Also: please confirm the daily spend cap during Phase 1 and confirm we can pause within 24 hours if Sales flags an issue.

Camila Reyes
Executive Sponsor (GTM), Redwood Inference


---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 1:48 PM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

All fair. Proposed decision criteria + pause rules below.

1) CPA-A thresholds
- Phase 1 target (learning): CPA-A <= $120 for the “signup-no-activation (7d)” audience.
- Continue to Phase 2 if BOTH:
  a) CPA-A <= $100 on a 7-day trailing basis AND
  b) we observe a measurable lift vs. holdout of >= +0.8pp activation (or a credible trend if sample size is still small)
- Immediate pause triggers:
  - CPA-A > $180 for 3 consecutive days, OR
  - complaint volume spikes (support tickets referencing ads / negative feedback), OR
  - any Sales escalations re: targeting (we will pause same-day)

2) Attribution approach
- We will not use platform-reported conversion claims as the source of truth.
- We’ll use UTMs on landing pages + internal event joins to measure: ad click → signup → activation.
- For view-through, we will report it separately as directional only (clearly labeled), and we won’t use it for go/no-go decisions.

3) Reporting
- Weekly: spend, audience breakdown, CPA-A, activation lift vs holdout, and any Sales/compliance notes.
- End of Q1: consolidated readout with clear “keep/iterate/kill” decision and next steps.

Spend / pacing
- Phase 1 daily cap: $500/day across platforms; total cap $7,500.
- Pause within 24 hours: yes. We can pause immediately; worst case is end-of-day billing.

Sales exclusion handling
- We’ll use Jordan’s weekly suppression file + an always-on exclusion list for enterprise domains.
- We’ll also exclude “in sales motion” users by CRM flag where available (Jordan/Isabelle can advise; we’ll keep it conservative).

If you’re good with the above, I’ll ask Ops to proceed with setup aligned to PM-670 and our UTM conventions.

Ben


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:06 AM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

Approved with the structure you proposed:
- Q1 cap: $25,000 total
- Phase 1 release: up to $7,500, with $500/day cap
- Phase 2/3 contingent on the CPA-A thresholds + holdout lift reporting as described

Conditions:
1) Weekly report is mandatory (share in sprint office hours + email me the snapshot).
2) Maintain conservative exclusions (active opps + late-stage security/procurement) and honor any Sales pause request same-day.
3) Any material change to audiences or landing pages should be noted in the weekly report for measurement integrity.

Jordan — thanks for the suppression file offer; please set that cadence up with Ben starting Monday.

Camila
