- Phase 1 release: up to $7,500, with $500/day cap
- Phase 2/3 contingent on the CPA-A thresholds + holdout lift reporting as described

Conditions:
1) Weekly report is mandatory (share in sprint office hours + email me the snapshot).
2) Maintain conservative exclusions (active opps + late-stage security/procurement) and honor any Sales pause request same-day.
3) Any material change to audiences or landing pages should be noted in the weekly report for measurement integrity.

Jordan — thanks for the suppression file offer; please set that cadence up with Ben starting Monday.

Camila
text:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 9:12 AM
Subject: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

Camila — requesting approval for a Q1 retargeting test budget to support the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation/early retention).

Summary / ask
- Approve a Q1 spend cap of $25,000 total for retargeting (Jan–Mar), with a staged release:
  - Phase 1 (Sprint Week 3–4): $7,500 test budget
  - Phase 2 (Feb): up to $10,000 contingent on hitting measurement guardrails
  - Phase 3 (Mar): up to $7,500 contingent on continued performance + no sales conflicts

Why now / ROI rationale (brief)
- We have meaningful “signup → no activation” volume and a consistent drop-off between API key creation and first successful request.
- Retargeting is intended to recover stalled developers at low marginal engineering cost.
- We’ll optimize toward cost per activated developer (7-day activation) and early usage signals (first 100k tokens), not just CTR.
- Even modest lift (1–2pp) on activation in the “signup-no-activation” cohort should pay back quickly vs. the spend, given downstream conversion to usage-based revenue and sales-assisted handoffs.

Measurement expectations (what we will report)
- Primary: incremental lift in 7-day activation rate (first successful request) vs. holdout, attributed via UTMs + internal event joins.
- Secondary: time-to-first-token (TTFT), first 100k tokens within 7 days, D7 active usage.
- Efficiency: cost per activated developer (CPA-A), cost per 100k tokens milestone (CPA-100k), and spend pacing.

Guardrails / policies
- Hard exclusions to avoid targeting active enterprise opportunities:
  - Suppress any domain/account present in Sales “active opp” list (Jordan to confirm weekly refresh cadence)
  - Suppress known enterprise domains + high-intent inbound leads already in sales motion
  - Frequency caps to avoid overexposure
- Compliance: only first-party event-based audiences permitted per policy; no sensitive category targeting.

Attachments
- Q1_Retargeting_Budget_Request_RedwoodInference.pdf
- Retargeting_Audiences_v3.xlsx
- Measurement_Expectations_OnePager.pdf

If approved, Tyler (Marketing Ops) will set spend caps + UTMs and we’ll share a weekly readout in the sprint office hours.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Growth Marketing, Redwood Inference


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 10:03 AM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

+1 with conditions on exclusions.

We can provide a weekly “active opp + sensitive accounts” suppression file (Mon AM). Two notes:
1) Please also exclude any accounts in late-stage security/procurement (even if they’re not marked “closed won” yet). We’ve had mixed reactions when ads show up during redlines.
2) Make sure we’re excluding by BOTH email domain and company name match where possible (some use personal domains early).

If we’re good on that, I’m aligned on the phased budget approach.

Jordan Blake
Sales Leadership, Redwood Inference


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 11:21 AM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

Ben — thanks. I’m supportive in principle, but I want to be explicit on the “continue/stop” criteria before we open up Feb/Mar.

A few questions:
1) What’s the target threshold for CPA-A (cost per activated dev) that would justify Phase 2? And what would trigger an immediate pause?
2) How are we handling view-through vs. click-through attribution? I don’t want us over-claiming.
3) Reporting cadence: can we commit to a weekly snapshot + a final Q1 summary (spend, cohorts, incremental lift)?

Also: please confirm the daily spend cap during Phase 1 and confirm we can pause within 24 hours if Sales flags an issue.

Camila Reyes
Executive Sponsor (GTM), Redwood Inference


---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 16, 2026 at 1:48 PM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

All fair. Proposed decision criteria + pause rules below.

1) CPA-A thresholds
- Phase 1 target (learning): CPA-A <= $120 for the “signup-no-activation (7d)” audience.
- Continue to Phase 2 if BOTH:
  a) CPA-A <= $100 on a 7-day trailing basis AND
  b) we observe a measurable lift vs. holdout of >= +0.8pp activation (or a credible trend if sample size is still small)
- Immediate pause triggers:
  - CPA-A > $180 for 3 consecutive days, OR
  - complaint volume spikes (support tickets referencing ads / negative feedback), OR
  - any Sales escalations re: targeting (we will pause same-day)

2) Attribution approach
- We will not use platform-reported conversion claims as the source of truth.
- We’ll use UTMs on landing pages + internal event joins to measure: ad click → signup → activation.
- For view-through, we will report it separately as directional only (clearly labeled), and we won’t use it for go/no-go decisions.

3) Reporting
- Weekly: spend, audience breakdown, CPA-A, activation lift vs holdout, and any Sales/compliance notes.
- End of Q1: consolidated readout with clear “keep/iterate/kill” decision and next steps.

Spend / pacing
- Phase 1 daily cap: $500/day across platforms; total cap $7,500.
- Pause within 24 hours: yes. We can pause immediately; worst case is end-of-day billing.

Sales exclusion handling
- We’ll use Jordan’s weekly suppression file + an always-on exclusion list for enterprise domains.
- We’ll also exclude “in sales motion” users by CRM flag where available (Jordan/Isabelle can advise; we’ll keep it conservative).

If you’re good with the above, I’ll ask Ops to proceed with setup aligned to PM-670 and our UTM conventions.

Ben


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Jan 17, 2026 at 10:06 AM
Subject: Re: Retargeting spend cap approval (Q1) — lifecycle growth sprint

Approved with the structure you proposed:
- Q1 cap: $25,000 total
- Phase 1 release: up to $7,500, with $500/day cap
- Phase 2/3 contingent on the CPA-A thresholds + holdout lift reporting as described

Conditions:
1) Weekly report is mandatory (share in sprint office hours + email me the snapshot).
2) Maintain conservative exclusions (active opps + late-stage security/procurement) and honor any Sales pause request same-day.
3) Any material change to audiences or landing pages should be noted in the weekly report for measurement integrity.

Jordan — thanks for the suppression file offer; please set that cadence up with Ben starting Monday.

Camila

source.subject: ACME on-prem IaC: merge proposal + evidence handoff
messages:
From: Andre Souza <andre.souza@acme-corp.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: ACME Procurement <procurement@acme-corp.com>, Liam Carter <liam.carter@acme-corp.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Initial: ACME on-prem IaC - proposed merges and evidence bundle

Jordan / Kimberly —

Thanks again for the walkthrough last week. Per ACME's compliance kickoff we captured a small batch of Terraform/Helm/Ansible changes we'd like Redwood to accept into the private-deploy repo and also requested evidence for the SOC audit team. High level:

- A1: Terraform: add 'acme-vlan' and update provider pinning to 1.5.2 in modules/networking.
- H1: Helm: values override for edge-router chart to set MTU and egress annotations.
- A2: Ansible: inventory + playbook to bootstrap bastion with the customer KMS plugin (deployed as a K8s job).

I've attached a light IaC test report and a patch with the proposed merges. Can you confirm a security review window this week and what evidence artifacts Redwood can provide (apply logs, plan outputs, signed module checksums)?

Also looping in Procurement for timing/PO notes.

Thanks,
Andre Souza
Senior Platform Engineer — ACME Corp

Attachment(s): acme-iactest-report.pdf (stub), merge_proposal_patch.diff

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Andre Souza <andre.souza@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, ACME Procurement <procurement@acme-corp.com>, Liam Carter <liam.carter@acme-corp.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Jan 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: ACME on-prem IaC - proposed merges and evidence bundle

Andre — thanks for the clear summary and the artifacts. We can run a focused security review and produce the following evidence items: plan/apply logs (redacted keys), module checksums signed by our release key, and a short runbook describing the bootstrap sequence.

Proposed timeline:
- Tue 13 Jan: infra review (Kimberly + Rishi) — static checks and policy linting.
- Wed 14 Jan: small-scale staging apply in ACME sandbox (Grace + Jordan) — smoke tests + plan logs collected.
- Thu 15 Jan: evidence packaging and delivery to ACME Security.

Questions from our side:
1) Is the bastion KMS integration sensitive to network egress? If yes we need to capture egress rules and allowlist.
2) Does ACME need signed attestations (ISO timestamped) or are checksums sufficient?

I'll book 30 minutes on Wed for the staging play if that works. Kimberly / Rishi — can you confirm you can do the Tue review?

-Jordan
Jordan Blake
Manager, Customer Deployments — Redwood Inference
(attachment: merge_proposal_patch.diff referenced)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Andre Souza <andre.souza@acme-corp.com>, Liam Carter <liam.carter@acme-corp.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Jan 2026 08:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: ACME on-prem IaC - proposed merges and evidence bundle

Confirmed — we will do the policy and lint pass Tue afternoon (I've blocked 13:00-14:30 PT). Early notes after a quick skim of the patch:

- Terraform provider pin bump: acceptable, but we need a follow-up test matrix entry to validate provider behaviors on the backplane (we'll add this to INFRA-4821).
- Networking module: the proposed 'acme-vlan' resource uses dynamic allocation via a custom script; we'll require an explicit ID reservation step in the module to avoid drift during restores.
- Helm values: the MTU/egress annotation changes look fine; callout: update release notes to include egress annotation rationale for on-call.
- Ansible bastion playbook: security concern — the KMS plugin run uses a host-level secret temporarily mounted. We need a short-lived credential flow documented (one-time bootstrap token + immediate rotate).

I'll add inline comments to the merge_proposal_patch.diff and tag the specific lines. Grace/Rishi — please validate the Ansible secret handling and add a remediation plan if short-lived tokens aren't feasible.

-Kim
Kimberly Park
Principal Security Engineer — Redwood Inference

On Tue, Jan 12, 2026 at 11:02 AM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com> wrote:
> Andre — thanks for the clear summary...

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Andre Souza <andre.souza@acme-corp.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, ACME Procurement <procurement@acme-corp.com>, Liam Carter <liam.carter@acme-corp.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Jan 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Initial: ACME on-prem IaC - proposed merges and evidence bundle

Andre / team — quick update from staging and evidence packaging: we completed the small-scale apply on Wed in the ACME sandbox. Outcomes below (short):

- Terraform: plan matched expected delta. We captured plan output and apply logs; two minor drift candidates flagged (they are benign and documented).
- Helm: edge-router deployed with updated MTU; metrics look nominal.
- Ansible: bastion bootstrap completed but we changed the approach slightly — switched to ephemeral token exchange via ACME's internal token service (no host-mounted creds). Details in evidence_manifest.yaml attached.

Deliverables attached: evidence_manifest.yaml (includes signed module checksums and plan/apply logs pointers), acme-iactest-report.pdf (summary), and the final merge_proposal_patch.diff with inline comments resolved.

Next steps:
1) ACME Security (Liam) to confirm acceptance of evidence and sign-off.
2) Once accepted, Marissa will coordinate the merge window and release tagging.
3) Jordan to schedule a brief post-merge smoke run with ACME ops.

Thanks everyone — nice cross-team sprint.

-Grace
Grace O'Connor
Staff Infrastructure Engineer — Redwood Inference

Attachment(s): evidence_manifest.yaml, acme-iactest-report.pdf, merge_proposal_patch.diff

source.subject: AWS Marketplace listing refresh — copy + asset checklist review (EOD Thu)
source.body:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lukas Meyer <lukas_meyer@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jan 13, 2026 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: AWS Marketplace listing refresh — copy + asset checklist review (EOD Thu)

Team —

We’re aiming to send the updated AWS Marketplace listing package to Partnerships by Friday AM so they can start the partner portal submission workflow.

Can you please review:
1) Listing copy draft v3 (headline, long description, feature bullets, FAQs)
2) Asset checklist (screenshots + diagrams we need + owners)

Specific items I need eyes on:
- Copy: ensure Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private differentiation is crystal clear (and doesn’t over-claim on SLAs/security).
- Procurement path: confirm the “how it works” section aligns with the subscription → entitlement → Redwood org provisioning flow.
- Assets: confirm we have the minimum set for AWS review (console screenshots, onboarding flow diagram, security overview link).

Deadline: EOD Thu (Jan 15) for inline feedback; I’ll consolidate Friday 9am.

Attachments:
- Redwood_AWS_Marketplace_ListingCopy_Draft_v3.docx
- AWS_Marketplace_Asset_Checklist_v1.xlsx
- Console_Screenshot_Shotlist_v0.9.pdf

source.subject: Bucketed commit & geo hedging — APAC/EU/US usage mix
messages:
From: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Sun, 30 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the product walkthrough earlier. We ran some numbers and want to understand how Redwood's hosted API pricing would work for a mixed-geo deployment. A few specific asks: 

- We expect ~1.2B tokens/mo at launch, split roughly 60% US, 30% EU, 10% APAC. How do commit tiers and per-token discounts apply when usage is cross-region?
- Do credits apply uniformly or are APAC tokens charged a surcharge?
- We need clarity on billing currency (USD vs local), VAT handling for EU, and whether data residency options change pricing.

I've attached a CSV with our projected usage by endpoint (FinApps_usage_profile.csv). Can you send a short mapping of tier thresholds and an example invoice calc?

Goal: get a 1-page commercial summary + a sample SOW we can share with procurement.

Cheers,
Nora
Nora Lin
Head of Platform, FinApps

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
Cc: Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix

Hi Nora — thanks for the clear note and the usage CSV. Attaching a provisional pricing matrix (redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx) that shows examples; I'll summarize the key points inline so procurement has quick answers.

Key proposal (hosted API, usage-based with committed discounts):
- Base list rate: $0.0008 per token (baseline, US).
- Commit tiers (blended monthly effective price after commit credit):
  * 0 - 250M tokens: list price
  * 250M - 1B tokens: 12% discount off list
  * 1B - 3B tokens: 20% discount off list
  * 3B+ tokens: 30% discount off list (negotiated for multi-quarter commits)

Geo treatment and blended pricing suggestion:
- We support a single committed pool with region-weighted conversion for billing. Example: convert actual usage into "billing tokens" using region multipliers to account for local infra costs. Recommended multipliers (example): US x1.0, EU x1.03, APAC x1.10.
- Contract can lock in a blended effective price calculated as: (sum(region_multiplier * tokens_used) across regions) / committed_tokens. That gives you predictable unit economics without separate per-region commits.

Credits and surcharges:
- Committed credits are applied to the billing-token total. Any overage billed at the post-commit tiered list rate for that month.
- APAC surcharge is modeled via the multiplier above; we do not add a separate line-item surcharge if you accept the blended conversion model.

VAT and currency:
- Invoices issued in USD by default; we can issue EUR invoices for EU legal entities (exchange/FX may apply).
- VAT is applied per tax law; our billing team will provide EU-facing invoices with VAT lines where required.

Data residency and options:
- If you require EU-only data residency, we can route EU traffic to our EU clusters; that adds a 2% uplift on the blended price unless you commit to a Dedicated region (then we can discuss absorbing that uplift).

Next steps I recommend:
1) If that approach sounds ok, we'll produce a 1-page commercial term sheet and SOW outline.
2) I can loop in Evelyn (billing) for invoice templates and Jordan (sales lead) for any contractual guardrails.

Attaching the matrix and a draft SOW outline (SOW_outline_v1.pdf). Happy to jump on a 30-min call — how does Tue 11/2 or Wed 11/3 look on your side?

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx, SOW_outline_v1.pdf

On Sun, Nov 30, 2025 at 09:12 AM Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
>
> Thanks for the product walkthrough earlier. We ran some numbers and want to understand how Redwood's hosted API pricing would work for a mixed-geo deployment. A few specific asks: ...

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 15:55:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix (internal suggestion + clarifications)

Nora / Ben — quick clarifications from a product and ops POV. Adding here so procurement sees practical guardrails.

- The region multipliers Ben suggested are conservative; APAC at x1.10 covers current spot GPU and cross-region bandwidth. For larger sustained Commit (>=3B/mo), we can reduce APAC multiplier to x1.05 or remove it entirely if you take a Dedicated APAC pool.
- We recommend a quarterly geo-rebalancing clause: on a 90-day cadence we compute actual usage and allow a one-time reallocation of up to 15% of the committed pool between geographic buckets (conversion formula: billing_tokens = tokens * multiplier). This protects both parties against regional traffic shift.
- For EU VAT and data-residency: Redwood can provide EU-invoiceable contracts and guarantees that PII stays in-region when EU-only routing is selected. If that is required, note the small data residency uplift Ben mentioned (2%) unless Dedicated is chosen.
- Operational note: if you anticipate bursty APAC traffic (e.g., marketing spikes), suggest adding 25% burst headroom on the committed pool or enabling short-term burst credits tied to prepaid tokens — cheaper than constant overspend.

I'll attach the pricing matrix we just referenced. If FinApps is comfortable with the blended conversion model, legal can draft the geo-bucket rebalancing clause quickly.

Jordan Blake

title: Bucketed commit & geo hedging — APAC/EU/US usage mix
preview:
From: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Sun, 30 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the product walkthrough earlier. We ran some numbers and want to understand how Redwood's hosted API pricing would work for a mixed-geo deployment. A few specific asks: 

- We expect ~1.2B tokens/mo at launch, split roughly 60% US, 30% EU, 10% APAC. How do commit tiers and per-token discounts apply when usage is cross-region?
- Do credits apply uniformly or are APAC tokens charged a surcharge?
- We need clarity on billing currency (USD vs local), VAT handling for EU, and whether data residency options change pricing.

I've attached a CSV with our projected usage by endpoint (FinApps_usage_profile.csv). Can you send a short mapping of tier thresholds and an example invoice calc?

Goal: get a 1-page commercial summary + a sample SOW we can share with procurement.

Cheers,
Nora
Nora Lin
Head of Platform, FinApps


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
Cc: Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix

Hi Nora — thanks for the clear note and the usage CSV. Attaching a provisional pricing matrix (redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx) that shows examples; I'll summarize the key points inline so procurement has quick answers.

Key proposal (hosted API, usage-based with committed discounts):
- Base list rate: $0.0008 per token (baseline, US).
- Commit tiers (blended monthly effective price after commit credit):
  * 0 - 250M tokens: list price
  * 250M - 1B tokens: 12% discount off list
  * 1B - 3B tokens: 20% discount off list
  * 3B+ tokens: 30% discount off list (negotiated for multi-quarter commits)

Geo treatment and blended pricing suggestion:
- We support a single committed pool with region-weighted conversion for billing. Example: convert actual usage into "billing tokens" using region multipliers to account for local infra costs. Recommended multipliers (example): US x1.0, EU x1.03, APAC x1.10.
- Contract can lock in a blended effective price calculated as: (sum(region_multiplier * tokens_used) across regions) / committed_tokens. That gives you predictable unit economics without separate per-region commits.

Credits and surcharges:
- Committed credits are applied to the billing-token total. Any overage billed at the post-commit tiered list rate for that month.
- APAC surcharge is modeled via the multiplier above; we do not add a separate line-item surcharge if you accept the blended conversion model.

VAT and currency:
- Invoices issued in USD by default; we can issue EUR invoices for EU legal entities (exchange/FX may apply).
- VAT is applied per tax law; our billing team will provide EU-facing invoices with VAT lines where required.

Data residency and options:
- If you require EU-only data residency, we can route EU traffic to our EU clusters; that adds a 2% uplift on the blended price unless you commit to a Dedicated region (then we can discuss absorbing that uplift).

Next steps I recommend:
1) If that approach sounds ok, we'll produce a 1-page commercial term sheet and SOW outline.
2) I can loop in Evelyn (billing) for invoice templates and Jordan (sales lead) for any contractual guardrails.

Attaching the matrix and a draft SOW outline (SOW_outline_v1.pdf). Happy to jump on a 30-min call — how does Tue 11/2 or Wed 11/3 look on your side?

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx, SOW_outline_v1.pdf

On Sun, Nov 30, 2025 at 09:12 AM Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
>
> Thanks for the product walkthrough earlier. We ran some numbers and want to understand how Redwood's hosted API pricing would work for a mixed-geo deployment. A few specific asks: ...


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 15:55:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix (internal suggestion + clarifications)

Nora / Ben — quick clarifications from a product and ops POV. Adding here so procurement sees practical guardrails.

- The region multipliers Ben suggested are conservative; APAC at x1.10 covers current spot GPU and cross-region bandwidth. For larger sustained Commit (>=3B/mo), we can reduce APAC multiplier to x1.05 or remove it entirely if you take a Dedicated APAC pool.
- We recommend a quarterly geo-rebalancing clause: on a 90-day cadence we compute actual usage and allow a one-time reallocation of up to 15% of the committed pool between geographic buckets (conversion formula: billing_tokens = tokens * multiplier). This protects both parties against regional traffic shift.
- For EU VAT and data-residency: Redwood can provide EU-invoiceable contracts and guarantees that PII stays in-region when EU-only routing is selected. If that is required, note the small data residency uplift Ben mentioned (2%) unless Dedicated is chosen.
- Operational note: if you anticipate bursty APAC traffic (e.g., marketing spikes), suggest adding 25% burst headroom on the committed pool or enabling short-term burst credits tied to prepaid tokens — cheaper than constant overspend.

I'll attach the pricing matrix we just referenced. If FinApps is comfortable with the blended conversion model, legal can draft the geo-bucket rebalancing clause quickly.

Jordan Blake
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx

From: Alex Green <alex.green@medlytics.io>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Taylor Chu <taylor.chu@medlytics.io>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Intro to Redwood — initial demo request

Hey Evelyn,

Thanks again for the quick intro earlier. Wed like to schedule a 45-60 minute demo next week to see Redwoods hosted inference and Dedicated offering. Attendees would be Alex (platform lead), Taylor (SRE), and 1-2 PM stakeholders.

Proposed windows: Tue 11/18 10:00-11:00 PT or Wed 11/19 13:00-14:00 PT. Would you and an engineer be able to join either? Were likely to pivot to a technical deep-dive depending on how the execs react.

Cheers,
Alex
Alex Green
Head of Platform, Medlytics
alex.green@medlytics.io

--
Attachment: Medlytics-questions.xlsx (spreadsheet with initial compliance/dev questions)

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Green <alex.green@medlytics.io>
Cc: Taylor Chu <taylor.chu@medlytics.io>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Intro to Redwood — initial demo request

Hi Alex / Taylor,

Thanks for the calendar options. Were excited to show Redwood. Quick note from our side: Vivek (senior infra engineer) is traveling the week of 11/18 and wont be available for a hands-on scoping workshop on short notice. To make sure you get the right attendees and outcomes, can we split this into two short sessions instead of one long meeting?

Proposal:
- Exec overview (30 min): product highlights, pricing levers, and high-level rollout patterns — Tue 11/18 10:00 PT — Evelyn + Aditya.
- Engineering scoping (60-90 min): deep dive on PoC plan, data flows, perf targets, and infra requirements — Thu 11/20 09:00 PT — Vivek + Jordan (if that works).

If that split looks OK Ill send two invites and attach a one-page PoC outline so your team can prepare. If you prefer to keep one meeting, we can run a broad intro and then schedule the technical session the following week.

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Senior Sales Manager, Redwood Inference
Evelyn.Hart@redwood.ai

--
Attachment: Redwood-PoC-outline.pdf (one-page PoC suggestion)

From: Alex Green <alex.green@medlytics.io>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Cc: Taylor Chu <taylor.chu@medlytics.io>, partner-success@medlytics.io
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 07:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Intro to Redwood — initial demo request

Evelyn,

Thanks — the two-session approach makes sense to us. The execs want a concise view first and the engineers will appreciate a separate deep dive. Thu 11/20 09:00 PT for the engineering session works for Viveks timezone (hes remote but available then).

Could you include a short pre-read for the engs (expected data volumes, token estimates, retention/PII controls) so Jordan and our infra team can come prepared? Also please confirm who from Redwood will present the infra-level items.

Alex

On Thu, Nov 13, 2025 at 11:03 AM Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Alex / Taylor,
>
> Thanks for the calendar options. Were excited to show Redwood. Quick note from our side: Vivek (senior infra engineer) is traveling the week of 11/18 and wont be available for a hands-on scoping workshop on short notice. To make sure you get the right attendees and outcomes, can we split this into two short sessions instead of one long meeting?
>

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Green <alex.green@medlytics.io>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 10:22:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Intro to Redwood — initial demo request

Alex / Taylor,

Thanks — attaching a short pre-read and a more detailed PoC outline (linked and attached). For the engineering session well cover:
- Expected token throughput and cost levers (batching, quantization).
- Network/data considerations (VPC, private deployment options).
- KV cache strategy and latency SLOs for interactive UIs.
- A concrete PoC timeline and required artifacts for success.

Presenters: Vivek will lead the infra/scaling section; Jordan will run the demo of request routing and cache behavior. If Vivek becomes unavailable we can assign Jordan + an engineer from our Dedicated team.

Ill send separate calendar invites for 11/18 exec overview (30 min) and 11/20 eng scoping (90 min). Please confirm attendee list for each so we can tailor slides.

Best,
Evelyn

--
Attachments: Redwood-PoC-outline.pdf, Medlytics-questions.xlsx
Links: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2bPoCOutline

From: Taylor Chu <taylor.chu@medlytics.io>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Alex Green <alex.green@medlytics.io>
Date: Mon, 17 Nov 2025 08:11:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Intro to Redwood — initial demo request

Evelyn,

Confirmed attendees:
- Exec overview (11/18 10:00 PT): Alex Green, Rachel Lin (PM), Sofia Moreno (Head of AI) — 30 min.
- Eng scoping (11/20 09:00 PT): Taylor Chu (SRE), Jordan Vega (infra), Priya Sen (data engineering) — 90 min.

Quick ask: for the eng session can you put the expected sample payload sizes and a rough expected QPS in the pre-read? That will help our infra folks size network and egress.

Thanks,
Taylor

On Fri, Nov 14, 2025 at 10:22 AM Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Alex / Taylor,
>
> Thanks — attaching a short pre-read and a more detailed PoC outline (linked and attached). For the engineering session well cover: ...

Date: Wed, 19 Nov 2025 14:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Intro to Redwood — initial demo request (Calendar invites + pre-read)

All,

Invites sent:
- Exec overview: Tue 11/18 10:00-10:30 PT — Evelyn + Aditya presenting.
- Eng scoping: Thu 11/20 09:00-10:30 PT — Vivek + Jordan presenting.

Pre-read items included in the Drive link: sample payload examples (small/medium/large), a token-estimation worksheet, and network requirements. Alex/Taylor, if you want us to adapt the PoC timeline, let us know before EOD Monday so we can update slides.

Looking forward to both sessions.

source.subject: Request: activity provenance packet and export validators
messages:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: security@novamed.com
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro this morning. As part of our SIG+SOC2 evidence collection we need two things clarified before we schedule a hands-on review: a concise inventory of what "admin/activity" events you persist and an example export we can use to validate our ingestion pipeline. Our audit team is specifically asking: 

- Which event types are recorded (e.g., user login, API key use, model invocation, KV cache operations, policy/role changes, config updates, escalation handoffs)?
- For each event type: stored attributes (actor, actor_id, ip, user_agent, request_id, tokens_consumed, model_id, before/after snapshots, cluster_node_id, region) and whether payloads contain user-generated content or PII.
- Default retention for admin activity vs. operator events, and whether per-tenant retention overrides are possible.
- Export formats supported (JSONL, Parquet, compressed tarball), timezone normalization, and manifest/checksum strategy.
- Redaction options we can assert (automated PII scrubbing, field-level tokenization, and available redaction reports).

Could you send a small sample export (time window ~24h, obfuscated PII) and a short note about retention and on-demand extraction steps? Ideally we can validate ingest by Monday morning (Nov 17). We will treat the sample as controlled — no production access will be attempted.

Thanks,
Lena Ortiz
Senior Information Security Manager
Novamed Therapeutics

---
Attachment: request_template_for_exports.csv (csv)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Lena — thanks for the clear list. I'm pulling together a small obfuscated sample and a one-page inventory. Before I package, two quick clarifications so we give exactly what you need: 

1) When you say "operator events" do you want privileged session recordings (console commands) included, or only audit-row events (create/update/delete actions)?
2) For the ingestion test: do you want JSONL with a separate CSV manifest, or Parquet + manifest? JSONL + CSV manifest is easiest for us to render quickly.

Plan: Kimberly will add the event-to-field mapping and retention matrix; Jordan will generate a 24h JSONL slice with PII obfuscated and checksums. I'll deliver the package and a short playbook with the extraction steps and the Drive link for download by EOD today.

Best,
Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

---
[Ben's calendar link: https://cal.redwood.inference/ben/15m]
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 11:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Ben / Jordan — sending the field mapping and retention summary below (short form). I'll attach the formal matrix to the package.

Event classes we persist (high level):
- Auth events: user_login, api_token_create, api_token_revoke, mfa_change
- Access control: role_assignment, policy_change, group_membership_change
- Operator/admin actions: config_update, deployment_rollout, model_pin, model_unpin, canary_trigger
- Invocation-level metadata: model_invoke (per-request metadata only — not full user prompt unless customer opts into logging), embedding_create, batch_rerank
- System/infra events: node_reboot, gpu_preempt, kv_cache_eviction

Common stored attributes per-row: timestamp_utc, event_type, actor_name, actor_id, actor_role, tenant_id, request_id, src_ip, geo_country, user_agent, object_type, object_id, change_summary (diff if applicable), model_id (if invocation), token_count, sequence_length, cluster_zone, checksum_sha256.

Retention policy (defaults):
- Auth & Access control: 24 months
- Operator/Admin action rows: 36 months
- Invocation-level metadata (non prompt): 12 months
- Full prompt logs (if enabled by customer): configurable, default 30 days

Export formats we support for evidence handoff: JSONL (newline-delimited), Parquet (columnar), compressed tar.gz of either, plus a CSV manifest with checksums and a small attestation JSON (signed by our export service). Timezones are normalized to UTC in exports; timestamps include original offset where available. We compute SHA256 checksums for each file and a top-level manifest (manifest.csv) with file size and SHA256.

Redaction options: SHA-1 truncation for identifiers, deterministic tokenization for emails (hash+domain-preserve), and full-string redaction for free-text prompts. We generate a redaction_report.csv that lists which rows/fields were redacted and why.

Notes on PII: by default our admin activity rows do not include user-submitted content; invocation prompt bodies are stored only if customer toggles verbose logging. For Novamed's sample we'll obfuscate tenant IDs and replace emails with hashed placeholders.

I'll attach: redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx and the mapping CSV. Jordan, can you produce the JSONL slice with schema-compliant rows and place in Drive when ready?

Kimberly Park
Sr. Compliance Engineer — Redwood Inference

Attachment: redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (xlsx)
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 16:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Hi Lena — attaching the sample package and the short export playbook. Summary below and link to download: 

What we sent:
- sample_admin_slice.jsonl (24h obfuscated slice; 500 rows covering auth, admin_action, model_invoke, infra_event)
- export_manifest_template.csv (manifest with checksums and file sizes)
- redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (fields we redacted and why)
- small README: extraction_steps.txt (one-page list showing how to request an on-demand export via the Console API and our SFTP fallback)

Key points for your ingest test:
- Files are in UTC; timestamps are ISO-8601.
- Each JSON row has a request_id you can use to correlate across files; request_id is stable across related events.
- We included a manifest.csv with per-file SHA256 and a top-level attestation.json signed by our export process.
- Default retention limits are documented in the attached retention_matrix. If you need a longer retention carveout we can propose a contract amendment or isolated dedicated retention for the tenant.

Download: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake/Novamed-AdminProvenance-Sample (link expires in 7 days)

If you'd like to walk through the sample tomorrow morning we can do a 30m session — propose Nov 15 9:30am PST or Nov 16 10:00am PST.

Thanks — Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

---
On Thu, Nov 13, 2025 at 11:18 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com> wrote:
> Ben / Jordan — sending the field mapping and retention summary below (short form). I'll attach the formal matrix to the package.
>
> [redaction and export detail omitted in-line — see attached redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx]
>
> Kimberly
>
Attachments: sample_admin_slice.jsonl (jsonl), export_manifest_template.csv (csv), redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (xlsx)

Plan: Kimberly will add the event-to-field mapping and retention matrix; Jordan will generate a 24h JSONL slice with PII obfuscated and checksums. I'll deliver the package and a short playbook with the extraction steps and the Drive link for download by EOD today.

Best,
Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

---
[Ben's calendar link: https://cal.redwood.inference/ben/15m]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 11:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Ben / Jordan — sending the field mapping and retention summary below (short form). I'll attach the formal matrix to the package.

Event classes we persist (high level):
- Auth events: user_login, api_token_create, api_token_revoke, mfa_change
- Access control: role_assignment, policy_change, group_membership_change
- Operator/admin actions: config_update, deployment_rollout, model_pin, model_unpin, canary_trigger
- Invocation-level metadata: model_invoke (per-request metadata only — not full user prompt unless customer opts into logging), embedding_create, batch_rerank
- System/infra events: node_reboot, gpu_preempt, kv_cache_eviction

Common stored attributes per-row: timestamp_utc, event_type, actor_name, actor_id, actor_role, tenant_id, request_id, src_ip, geo_country, user_agent, object_type, object_id, change_summary (diff if applicable), model_id (if invocation), token_count, sequence_length, cluster_zone, checksum_sha256.

Retention policy (defaults):
- Auth & Access control: 24 months
- Operator/Admin action rows: 36 months
- Invocation-level metadata (non prompt): 12 months
- Full prompt logs (if enabled by customer): configurable, default 30 days

Export formats we support for evidence handoff: JSONL (newline-delimited), Parquet (columnar), compressed tar.gz of either, plus a CSV manifest with checksums and a small attestation JSON (signed by our export service). Timezones are normalized to UTC in exports; timestamps include original offset where available. We compute SHA256 checksums for each file and a top-level manifest (manifest.csv) with file size and SHA256.

Redaction options: SHA-1 truncation for identifiers, deterministic tokenization for emails (hash+domain-preserve), and full-string redaction for free-text prompts. We generate a redaction_report.csv that lists which rows/fields were redacted and why.

Notes on PII: by default our admin activity rows do not include user-submitted content; invocation prompt bodies are stored only if customer toggles verbose logging. For Novamed's sample we'll obfuscate tenant IDs and replace emails with hashed placeholders.

I'll attach: redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx and the mapping CSV. Jordan, can you produce the JSONL slice with schema-compliant rows and place in Drive when ready?

Kimberly Park
Sr. Compliance Engineer — Redwood Inference

Attachment: redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 16:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Hi Lena — attaching the sample package and the short export playbook. Summary below and link to download: 

What we sent:
- sample_admin_slice.jsonl (24h obfuscated slice; 500 rows covering auth, admin_action, model_invoke, infra_event)
- export_manifest_template.csv (manifest with checksums and file sizes)
- redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (fields we redacted and why)
- small README: extraction_steps.txt (one-page list showing how to request an on-demand export via the Console API and our SFTP fallback)

Key points for your ingest test:
- Files are in UTC; timestamps are ISO-8601.
- Each JSON row has a request_id you can use to correlate across files; request_id is stable across related events.
- We included a manifest.csv with per-file SHA256 and a top-level attestation.json signed by our export process.
- Default retention limits are documented in the attached retention_matrix. If you need a longer retention carveout we can propose a contract amendment or isolated dedicated retention for the tenant.

Download: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake/Novamed-AdminProvenance-Sample (link expires in 7 days)

If you'd like to walk through the sample tomorrow morning we can do a 30m session — propose Nov 15 9:30am PST or Nov 16 10:00am PST.

Thanks — Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

---

> Ben / Jordan — sending the field mapping and retention summary below (short form). I'll attach the formal matrix to the package.
>
> [redaction and export detail omitted in-line — see attached redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx]
>
> Kimberly
>
Attachments: sample_admin_slice.jsonl (jsonl), export_manifest_template.csv (csv), redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (xlsx)

Subject: Greenline: Console onboarding — proposed prep & time options

Hi Alex —

Thanks again for choosing Redwood. Per our discovery, we'd like to run a 90-minute console walkthrough + hands-on lab to cover: model catalog, routing policies, and a short API enablement for your integration engineers.

I proposed three windows next week in the calendar invite (attached) but wanted to flag the timezone: the times were listed in PT. If your team is primarily ET, we can move everything 3 hours later. Proposed slots (PT):
- Tue 9/16 09:00–10:30 PT
- Wed 9/17 13:00–14:30 PT
- Thu 9/18 16:00–17:30 PT

Please let me know which works or if you need later times (we can do an 18:00 PT start if that helps). I've looped Camila (SE) for the technical run and Naomi for logistics.

Could we move to 18:00 PT (21:00 ET) on Thursday instead? It avoids conflicts for our PMs. If Priya (copied) is available, she can co-host the session from our side.

Also, quick ask: can you send any lab creds we'll use ahead of time so our SREs can whitelist IPs?

—Alex

Subject: Console lab credentials and IP ranges (Greenline)

Alex / Priya —

Attached are one-time lab credentials and the IP ranges for our staging clusters. Please note the credentials will expire 72 hours after the session unless you ask for an extension.

Priya and I whitelisted the IPs and verified SSH to the lab-host (connection succeeded). 18:00 PT on Thu 9/18 is confirmed and I've accepted the calendar invite. Priya will join as our co-host. We'll also record the session on our end.

source.subject: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Malik <aisha@solutionhub.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — we liked the connector and think there’s concrete demand from two of our mutual customers. Before we lock in a pilot, two operational items we want to align on so we can protect both sides during the initial 90-day window:

1) Lead registration window: Redwood would like a 120-day registration window from the first qualified introduction (MQL -> SAL handoff) where SolutionHub is credited for top-of-funnel introductions. We propose a simple claim form and a 2-week validation turnaround.
2) Margin protection: For the pilot (first 6 months) we can offer a 14% reseller discount baseline but we need a carveout that protects E2E margin if SolutionHub drives bundled implementation services — specifically a cap on retroactive clawbacks unless there’s evidence of dual-sourced sales.

I’ve attached a brief MAP outline (SolutionHub-MAP-v1.pdf) and our onboarding checklist. Can your GTM/legal lead review and confirm these are acceptable or propose concrete redlines?

Next steps: if you’re good with the high-level mechanics we’ll route this through Marissa for final commercial sign-off and legal for a short SOW addendum.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake\Partner Sales\Redwood Inference\jordan.blake@redwood.ai

Attachment: SolutionHub-MAP-v1.pdf (summary of proposed margins and lead claim flow)
From: Aisha Malik <aisha@solutionhub.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: SolutionHub Commercial <partners@solutionhub.io>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Jordan —

Appreciate the clear ask. Quick reactions from our side:
• 120-day window is OK for now, but we ask for a mandatory snapshot of the opportunity (CRM URL + contact + date) when the claim is submitted so we can map attribution.
• 14% reseller baseline sounds workable for MVP customers, but for deals > $250k we’d expect a tiered uplift (up to 18%) if we provide sales-engineering support.
• For clawbacks, we’re comfortable with a no-retro clause unless there’s clear evidence the intro was not qualified (we’ll accept a short validation process).

I’m looping in Hannah (cc’d to partners@) from our commercial team to handle the formal claim form and timeline. We’ll review your MAP PDF and come back with a redline.

Best,
Aisha Malik\Head of Partnerships\SolutionHub\aisha@solutionhub.io

---
On Tue, Jul 22, 2025 at 9:15 AM Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Aisha,
>
> Thanks again for the demo yesterday — we liked the connector...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aisha Malik <aisha@solutionhub.io>
Date: Wed, 23 Jul 2025 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Jordan / team —

Thanks for sharing. A couple of operational clarifications from commercial/legal: 
1) Claim validation: we need a 10-business-day SLA for initial validation (the 2-week ask is fine). Please commit to providing the CRM snapshot + any supporting intel at submission. We’ll log claims in HubSpot (see link) and keep an audit trail.
2) Margin language: we can accept the 14% baseline for pilot customers, but I want an explicit schedule that ties uplift to named activities (e.g., SE-led POC, co-marketing credits). I’m attaching a lightweight onboarding checklist to capture responsibilities.
3) Clawbacks: propose a mutual ‘evidence-of-duplication’ clause — Redwood can only request retroactive adjustments if we can show customer-signed proof that SolutionHub acted outside the agreed registration process.

I will prepare a clean term sheet for signature if SolutionHub confirms the CRM snapshot requirement and the uplift triggers. Ben — please review the revenue routing for partner-sourced deals (I’ll tag you separately).

Thanks,
Marissa Cole\Head of Channels & Alliances\Redwood Inference\marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Attachment: redwood-partner-onboarding-checklist.xlsx
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 24 Jul 2025 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Marissa — quick note on revenue handling: for pilot deals we’ll flow partner credit through our reseller ledger as a contra-revenue entry and tag associated invoices so finance can reconcile credits quarterly. Two asks for SolutionHub:
1) Provide a single point-of-contact for invoicing and PO management.
2) Confirm that any uplift (e.g., >14%) will be identified at contract signature so we don’t have to do retroactive splits.

If we get those two items, finance is fine with the proposed 90/120-day operational cadence.

Ben Carter\Revenue Ops\Redwood Inference\ben.carter@redwood.ai
From: Aisha Malik <aisha@solutionhub.io>

title: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Malik <aisha@solutionhub.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — we liked the connector and think there’s concrete demand from two of our mutual customers. Before we lock in a pilot, two operational items we want to align on so we can protect both sides during the initial 90-day window:

1) Lead registration window: Redwood would like a 120-day registration window from the first qualified introduction (MQL -> SAL handoff) where SolutionHub is credited for top-of-funnel introductions. We propose a simple claim form and a 2-week validation turnaround.
2) Margin protection: For the pilot (first 6 months) we can offer a 14% reseller discount baseline but we need a carveout that protects E2E margin if SolutionHub drives bundled implementation services — specifically a cap on retroactive clawbacks unless there’s evidence of dual-sourced sales.

I’ve attached a brief MAP outline (SolutionHub-MAP-v1.pdf) and our onboarding checklist. Can your GTM/legal lead review and confirm these are acceptable or propose concrete redlines?

Next steps: if you’re good with the high-level mechanics we’ll route this through Marissa for final commercial sign-off and legal for a short SOW addendum.

Thanks,
Jordan Blake\Partner Sales\Redwood Inference\jordan.blake@redwood.ai

Attachment: SolutionHub-MAP-v1.pdf (summary of proposed margins and lead claim flow)

From: Aisha Malik <aisha@solutionhub.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: SolutionHub Commercial <partners@solutionhub.io>
Date: Tue, 22 Jul 2025 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Solution Hub: lead registration & margin protection (pilot terms)

Jordan —

Appreciate the clear ask. Quick reactions from our side:
• 120-day window is OK for now, but we ask for a mandatory snapshot of the opportunity (CRM URL + contact + date) when the claim is submitted so we can map attribution.
• 14% reseller baseline sounds workable for MVP customers, but for deals > $250k we’d expect a tiered uplift (up to 18%) if we provide sales-engineering support.
• For clawbacks, we’re comfortable with a no-retro clause unless there’s clear evidence the intro was not qualified (we’ll accept a short validation process).

I’m looping in Hannah (cc’d to partners@) from our commercial team to handle the formal claim form and timeline. We’ll review your MAP PDF and come back with a redline.

Best,
Aisha Malik\Head of Partnerships\SolutionHub\aisha@solutionhub.io

source.subject: SignalSuite vendor replacement: pilot ask & packaging options
messages:
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Hi Camila,

Quick note — we are evaluating a potential switch off ModelWave for our inference needs. We run a mix of long chat sessions (avg 700 tokens) and embedding-heavy batch jobs; current spend is roughly $110k/mo and ModelWave is offering a multi-year sticker that isn’t aligning with our burst/surge profile.

Two asks up front:
- Can you share packaging options that map to sustained commit vs bursty traffic?
- Would Redwood consider a constrained pilot (3 months) with migration credits to offset egress and implementation risk?

Attached is an excerpt of our current contract (ModelWave_contract_excerpt.pdf) — procurement wants a comparable commercial structure before we proceed.

Available for a quick call Wed afternoon.

Thanks,
Alex Turner
Head of ML Platforms
SignalSuite
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2025 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Alex — thanks for the note and the contract excerpt. Appreciate the usage breakdown in the file; that helps. A few quick points below and I can put together a short commercial two-pager.

High level packaging that typically maps to your profile:

1) Tiered Commit + Burst Pool
   - Monthly committed tokens with a small committed throughput SLA.
   - Shared burst pool for spikes billed at a higher token rate; can be capped per month.

2) Pilot (time-boxed) + Credits
   - 3-month pilot at a discount tier, with migration credits applied against first invoice or onboarding services.

3) Dedicated Reserved (if you want predictable latency)
   - Reserved GPU capacity with flat run-rate; suitable if you value tail-latency guarantees over unit cost.

Attached: redwood_pricing_snapshot_v1.xlsx — this is a starter view (estimates only).

If procurement wants to see an explicit pilot structure I can propose: 50% token-rate discount for 3 months + $25k migration credit, conditioned on a 12-month commit post-pilot. Does that sound like a starting point to you?

I can jump on Wed 2pm PT.

—Camila
Camila Reyes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2025 08:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Looping in to offer a concession framework and quick trade-off guidance.

Camila — recommend we put two structured options in the deck so procurement can sign off:
A) Pilot-first route (lowest friction): 3-month pilot, 50% off token rates, $25k migration credit, auto-convert to 12-month 40% commit discount if usage >= 80% of pilot avg.
B) Commit-first route (best unit economics): 12-month commit at tiered pricing with a 6-month ramp and a smaller one-time onboarding credit ($10k).

If SignalSuite is worried about egress/onboarding risk we can add a short SLA credit (service credit up to 5% of monthly invoice) for first 90 days post-conversion.

Camila — happy to draft a short concession table. Alex — is procurement comfortable with a conditional auto-convert?

Jordan Blake
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2025 09:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Thanks Camila/Jordan — that framework is helpful. Procurement reviewed and had two clarifications:

1) They want the pilot to include at least 250k embedding units per month and clear overage pricing.
2) Procurement is pushing for migration credits to cover egress and initial consulting (they suggested $40k total).

Timeline: procurement needs a signed pilot addendum by the end of the month to stay on track for a Q4 transition.

I attached a short procurement note (procurement_terms.docx) outlining our internal approvals. Can you confirm whether the auto-convert clause requires additional legal approval on Redwood’s side?

—Alex
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Jul 2025 17:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Alex — appreciate the clarity. Quick answers and next steps below.

- Pilot scope: we can commit to 250k embedding units/month and the chat workload described. Overage rates will be our standard burst rate (1.8x pilot token rate) and we’ll cap monthly overage at 30% of committed pilot consumption unless you opt out.

- Migration credit: we can increase the onboarding credit to $35k (we can make up the gap from professional services discounts); $40k is a little outside standard, but I’ll escalate if procurement needs the full amount.

- Auto-convert legal: the conditional auto-convert (pilot -> committed term) requires a short addendum but is standard; legal will approve template language (no unusual R/W clauses).

Next steps:
1) I’ll send a two-page pilot addendum + concession table (will include metrics and the overage cap).
2) If SignalSuite signs by 31 Jul we’ll kick off onboarding immediately and reserve extra burst pool capacity for initial ramp.
3) I’ll also open a HubSpot deal record and send a DocuSign pilot addendum link.

Links: https://app.hubspot.com/records/DEAL-5482 (deal), https://www.docusign.com/templates/SS-pilot-2025

Camila Reyes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments referenced: redwood_pricing_snapshot_v1.xlsx

Next steps:
1) I’ll send a two-page pilot addendum + concession table (will include metrics and the overage cap).
2) If SignalSuite signs by 31 Jul we’ll kick off onboarding immediately and reserve extra burst pool capacity for initial ramp.
3) I’ll also open a HubSpot deal record and send a DocuSign pilot addendum link.

Links: https://app.hubspot.com/records/DEAL-5482 (deal), https://www.docusign.com/templates/SS-pilot-2025

Camila Reyes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments referenced: redwood_pricing_snapshot_v1.xlsx
text:
From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Hi Camila,

Quick note — we are evaluating a potential switch off ModelWave for our inference needs. We run a mix of long chat sessions (avg 700 tokens) and embedding-heavy batch jobs; current spend is roughly $110k/mo and ModelWave is offering a multi-year sticker that isn’t aligning with our burst/surge profile.

Two asks up front:
- Can you share packaging options that map to sustained commit vs bursty traffic?
- Would Redwood consider a constrained pilot (3 months) with migration credits to offset egress and implementation risk?

Attached is an excerpt of our current contract (ModelWave_contract_excerpt.pdf) — procurement wants a comparable commercial structure before we proceed.

Available for a quick call Wed afternoon.

Thanks,
Alex Turner
Head of ML Platforms
SignalSuite


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2025 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Alex — thanks for the note and the contract excerpt. Appreciate the usage breakdown in the file; that helps. A few quick points below and I can put together a short commercial two-pager.

High level packaging that typically maps to your profile:

1) Tiered Commit + Burst Pool
   - Monthly committed tokens with a small committed throughput SLA.
   - Shared burst pool for spikes billed at a higher token rate; can be capped per month.

2) Pilot (time-boxed) + Credits
   - 3-month pilot at a discount tier, with migration credits applied against first invoice or onboarding services.

3) Dedicated Reserved (if you want predictable latency)
   - Reserved GPU capacity with flat run-rate; suitable if you value tail-latency guarantees over unit cost.

Attached: redwood_pricing_snapshot_v1.xlsx — this is a starter view (estimates only).

If procurement wants to see an explicit pilot structure I can propose: 50% token-rate discount for 3 months + $25k migration credit, conditioned on a 12-month commit post-pilot. Does that sound like a starting point to you?

I can jump on Wed 2pm PT.

—Camila
Camila Reyes
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2025 08:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exploring a possible replacement: ModelWave -> Redwood (pricing + pilot)

Looping in to offer a concession framework and quick trade-off guidance.

Camila — recommend we put two structured options in the deck so procurement can sign off:
A) Pilot-first route (lowest friction): 3-month pilot, 50% off token rates, $25k migration credit, auto-convert to 12-month 40% commit discount if usage >= 80% of pilot avg.
B) Commit-first route (best unit economics): 12-month commit at tiered pricing with a 6-month ramp and a smaller one-time onboarding credit ($10k).

If SignalSuite is worried about egress/onboarding risk we can add a short SLA credit (service credit up to 5% of monthly invoice) for first 90 days post-conversion.

Camila — happy to draft a short concession table. Alex — is procurement comfortable with a conditional auto-convert?

Jordan Blake
Head of Sales, Enterprise | Redwood Inference


From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@signalsuite.io>

2) Procurement is pushing for migration credits to cover egress and initial consulting (they suggested $40k total).

source.subject: Hosted API: minimum monthly spend and small-commit pathways
messages:
From: Claire Morris <claire@datasolve.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Minimum monthly spend for hosted API?

Hi Evelyn,

Thanks for the demo last week — the latency and streaming behavior looked great. We are a small team and are evaluating Redwood for a multi-feature rollout (summarization + semantic search) but we don’t have Dedicated capacity needs yet. Two quick questions on pricing before we take this to procurement: 

1) Is there a minimum monthly commit for Hosted API customers, or can we remain purely usage-based?
2) If a commit is required to get a commercial rate, what do your low-entry bands look like for companies at our scale (~500k tokens/mo initially)?

Procurement will ask for an example rate card and any available introductory credits or short-term pilots. Can you share the simplest path to get started with predictable pricing and a low friction commercial agreement?

Appreciate it —
Claire
Head of Product
DataSolve Inc.

P.S. We can loop procurement on a follow-up if more details are needed.

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
To: Claire Morris <claire@datasolve.com>
Cc: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Minimum monthly spend for hosted API?

Hi Claire — great to hear and glad the demo was useful. Short answers up front: no hard 6-figure minimums for Hosted API; we support pure pay-as-you-go but commercial rates (discounts) usually come with a small monthly commit. That said, we have a couple of starter options we commonly use for customers in your profile. I’ll outline them below and attach a one-page rate card.

Starter options (targeted at teams <1M tokens/mo):
- Pay-as-you-go: no commit, list rate per 1k tokens, no volume discount. Good for exploration.
- Starter Commit: $2,000/month commit (12-month term), rollover up to 2 months of unused commit, effective discount ~10% vs list at your projected volume.
- Pilot Credit + Short Commit: 3-month pilot with $5k in platform credits, then optional 6-month small commit (e.g., $3k/mo) to bridge procurement review and provide moderate savings.

I’m attaching the rate card and a sample mutual action plan. If procurement needs a formal procurement worksheet or PO flow, Sergio (cc’d) can share the procurement checklist and standard MSA language.

If you want, we can propose a tailored starter band after you confirm expected token mix (generation vs embeddings) and target SLA needs. Would you like me to draft a short proposal for procurement?

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: Redwood-Hosted-RateCard-2025.pdf, MutualActionPlan-DataSolve.xlsx

From: Claire Morris <claire@datasolve.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: DataSolve Procurement <procurement@datasolve.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 13:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Minimum monthly spend for hosted API?

Thanks Evelyn — that is helpful. Procurement is specifically asking: is the $2k/month a firm minimum for getting any discount, or are there other levers (annual prepay, committed token bundles) that could achieve similar economics with fewer contractual hoops? Also, are there timing-limited promotions for startups or early pilots?

I’ve included our procurement contact so they can call out any internal constraints. If you can flag any flexible paths we can evaluate (e.g., 3- or 6-month committable bands, trial credits tied to a pilot MOU), that would speed things up.

-Claire

From: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
To: Claire Morris <claire@datasolve.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Minimum monthly spend for hosted API?

Hi Claire — looping in a few clarifications from pricing ops.

1) The $2k/month starter commit is our broadly-offered low entry band and is the simplest to contract. It gives predictable unit economics and unlocks a modest discount on token pricing.

2) Alternative levers we offer for customers who can’t commit to $2k/mo: 
   - Annual prepay: e.g., prepays of $10k or $25k that sit on account for usage; effective discount becomes a function of prepay size and term. No monthly commit needed, but typically requires a single PO.
   - Committed token bundle: purchase a block of tokens (e.g., 5M tokens) upfront and draw down; useful if you can estimate consumption.
   - Pilot program: a limited-time credit ($3k–$7k) tied to a short MOU and a light success criteria doc. This avoids an immediate financial commit while giving you runway to validate.

3) Promotions: we occasionally run startup/partner promos. For DataSolve I can approve a one-time pilot credit up to $5k if you commit to a 3-month MOU with basic success criteria (call volume and quality checks). Procurement: this credit is separate from any commercial discount applied once you sign a commit.

If you want a bundled example (annual prepay vs monthly starter commit) I can prepare a two-row comparison showing net unit price at your estimated 500k tokens/mo. Evelyn, can you confirm if they expect a 70/30 generation/embedding split? That will materially affect per-token economics.

- Sergio
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

From: Claire Morris <claire@datasolve.com>
To: Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Cc: DataSolve Procurement <procurement@datasolve.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 10:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Minimum monthly spend for hosted API?

Thanks Sergio — we can sign a 3-month MOU for a pilot and would welcome the $5k pilot credit. Token mix for the pilot will likely be ~60% generation, 40% embeddings. Procurement confirms they prefer an annual prepay to avoid monthly invoices, but they need two examples: a $10k prepay (no monthly commitment) and the $2k/month starter commit for comparison.

Please send the two-line pricing comparison and a short MOU template we can use for the pilot. We’ll review and aim to move quickly.

Claire

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Claire Morris <claire@datasolve.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Jul 2025 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Minimum monthly spend for hosted API?

Claire — welcome. I work on pricing strategy and will put together the two-row comparison and a short MOU template this afternoon. Quick notes so we align:

- For a $10k annual prepay (applied immediately) we’ll show an effective ~8% reduction vs list pricing at your pilot mix; funds are available on the account without monthly billing. No required monthly minimum after prepay drawdown.
- For the $2k/month starter commit (12-month term) we’ll show a ~10% effective discount at your estimated volumes with 1-month rollover for unused commit, invoiced monthly.
- Pilot credit: $5k one-time credit applied to the first invoice after MOU execution; requires a short success criteria doc and a 3-month pilot MOU.

I’ll attach the comparison and the MOU draft to the HubSpot record and to the Drive folder referenced. Expect a note from me within 4 hours.

Regards,
Jordan Blake
Revenue Strategy, Redwood Inference

--
On behalf of Redwood Inference

source.subject: Evening walkthrough options for SOC 2 / pen test / DPA - GreenBank
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Andrea Soto <andrea.soto@greenbank.com>, security@greenbank.com
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security walkthrough availability (SOC 2 / pen test / DPA)

Hi Andrea —

Thanks for the quick intake form. We'd love to run a combined compliance walkthrough that covers SOC 2 scope, an outline of the upcoming pen test, and the DPA / data handling notes. To keep this efficient for your team, we typically run a 60–90 minute session where we:

- Review architecture and data flows (20–30 min)
- Controls and logging (20–30 min)
- Pen-test scope & timeline (10–15 min)
- DPA highlights and evidence handoff (10–15 min)

We know you have teams across timezones — below are two proposed slots. Please tell us which (if any) work and who from your side will join (infosec, app owners, procurement):

Option A — East / North America-friendly (evening):

source.subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)
source.body:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Wallace <ethan_wallace@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Noura El-Khoury <noura_el-khoury@redwoodinference.com>, Maya Chen <maya@northpeakpr.com>
Date: Wed, Jul 9, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)

Team — we’re in the last mile. Can we do a final pass today on:

1) Press release (quotes + any claims language)
2) Press kit ZIP contents + links (landing page, PDF, methodology)
3) Analyst/press email template (Maya)

Target: lock by 2:00pm PT so Northpeak can queue distribution for tomorrow 8:00am PT.

Current links (please confirm these are final):
- Landing page: https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar
- PDF report: https://redwoodinference.com/reports/latency-per-dollar-2025q3.pdf
- Methodology: https://redwoodinference.com/methodology/latency-per-dollar

Open items I’m tracking:
- Jordan quote: please confirm final wording.
- Legal: confirm whether we can say “best-in-class” anywhere (I believe no).
- Press kit: is the 1-pager methodology summary included (for folks who won’t read the full PDF)?

Attaching:
- RedwoodInference_BenchmarkReport_PressRelease_v7.docx
- RedwoodInference_PressKit_2025-07-10.zip
- Benchmark_Methodology_Summary_1pager.pdf

Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


---

From: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Wallace <ethan_wallace@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Mendes <sofia_mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodinference.com>, Maya Chen <maya@northpeakpr.com>
Date: Wed, Jul 9, 2025 at 9:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Benchmark report launch — press release + press kit final check (quotes/links)

All — I reviewed v7 and have two last-minute edits that I think we should make before Legal re-checks.

Edits requested (low-risk):
1) Replace “best-in-class latency-per-dollar” with “leading latency-per-dollar” (or “strong latency-per-dollar”) in the lead.
   - Current: “best-in-class latency-per-dollar across regions”
   - Proposed: “high latency-per-dollar across regions”

2) In the “How we measured” paragraph, add the explicit caveat that results vary by workload and configuration, with a pointer to methodology.
   - Proposed sentence: “Results vary by workload and configuration; full methodology and run artifacts are available at …”

Press kit ZIP contents check:
- ✅ Press release (doc)
- ✅ Report PDF
- ✅ PNG chart set (3)
- ✅ Company boilerplate
- ✅ Logos (approved)
- ✅ Methodology 1-pager (included)

Ben — can you confirm the PDF URL is stable (no redirects tomorrow morning)?

Soojin
Director of Product Marketing

From: Michael Grant <michael_grant@redwoodinference.com>
