source.subject: Invoice intake: webhook/SFTP vs email fallback — Lumos vendor onboarding
messages:
From: Susan Lee <susan.lee@lumos-inc.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@lumos-inc.com
Date: Fri, 4 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding + invoice submission options

Hi Dev,

Thanks for the quick intro earlier. We need to get Lumos set up in your system so our AP team can submit invoices cleanly. A couple of constraints on our side:

- We currently push invoices from our ERP to external partners via SFTP (nightly batch) or via an EDI gateway; we generally do not use vendor portals unless there's a straightforward API.
- If portal integration is required, it needs an SSO/jumpstart and an onboarding form so AP can map PO numbers to invoices.

Can you share the recommended ingestion paths at Redwood (portal API, webhook, SFTP, email with subject token) and what the expected CSV/PDF layout should be? Also: how do you handle PO stamping/PO affinity for multi-PO invoices?

Attached: lumos_vendor_onboarding_form_v1.pdf (pdf) — basic fields we can provide.

Happy to hop on a short call next week — Susan and our AP lead (accounts-payable@lumos-inc.com) are copied.

Thanks,
Susan Lee\Vendor Operations\Lumos Inc.
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Susan Lee <susan.lee@lumos-inc.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Jul 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding + invoice submission options

Hi Susan — thanks for the context and the form.

Quick summary of recommended paths (pros/cons):
- Portal API (recommended if you can call out from your stack): low friction for our ops, supports PO affinity, real-time ACKs, tokenized submission. Requires mapping during onboarding but gives best visibility.
- SFTP (batch): good if you already generate nightly files; we can pickup CSVs. We'll provide a sample CSV schema and an idempotency key. Lower visibility on immediate ACKs but reliable for high-volume vendors.
- Webhook (push to our endpoint): only for real-time integrations — needs engineering support.
- Email fallback: support email-based submissions with subject token (e.g., SUBMIT-REDWOOD:vendor_id). Works for small volume or when other channels aren't available; more manual ops and higher failure rate for malformed PDFs.

PO stamping: we support three modes — single-PO enforcement, line-item PO matching, and free-form (AP tags PO numbers in invoice). For multi-PO invoices we prefer a line-item CSV with explicit PO columns to avoid manual reconciliation.

I've attached a short draft playbook that includes a sample CSV (example-po-stamped-invoice.csv) and the minimal required headers. Can you confirm Lumos can produce a CSV with: invoice_id, invoice_date, line_number, po_number, sku, qty, unit_price, tax, total? If not, let us know which fields you can supply.

Proposed next step: brief call next Tue 7/8 to pick channel and agree on a pilot (one month) — happy to run a small validation batch.

Cheers,
Dev
Dev Patel\Onboarding & AP Integrations\Redwood Inference
From: Susan Lee <susan.lee@lumos-inc.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@lumos-inc.com
Date: Mon, 7 Jul 2025 08:23:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding + invoice submission options

On Fri, Jul 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Susan — thanks for the context and the form.
> 
> Quick summary of recommended paths (pros/cons):
> - Portal API (recommended if you can call out from your stack): low friction for our ops, supports PO affinity, real-time ACKs, tokenized submission. Requires mapping during onboarding but gives best visibility.

Thanks for the clear options. A few clarifications from AP team:
- We can export a CSV nightly. The CSV header you proposed is fine but we will not include SKU (we only have PO#, description, qty, unit price, tax, total).
- SFTP is our preference for now — less change. We'll need to know file naming convention and whether you require GPG encryption or a TLS-only upload.

title: Invoice intake: webhook/SFTP vs email fallback — Lumos vendor onboarding
preview:
From: Susan Lee <susan.lee@lumos-inc.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@lumos-inc.com
Date: Fri, 4 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding + invoice submission options

Hi Dev,

Thanks for the quick intro earlier. We need to get Lumos set up in your system so our AP team can submit invoices cleanly. A couple of constraints on our side:

- We currently push invoices from our ERP to external partners via SFTP (nightly batch) or via an EDI gateway; we generally do not use vendor portals unless there's a straightforward API.
- If portal integration is required, it needs an SSO/jumpstart and an onboarding form so AP can map PO numbers to invoices.

Can you share the recommended ingestion paths at Redwood (portal API, webhook, SFTP, email with subject token) and what the expected CSV/PDF layout should be? Also: how do you handle PO stamping/PO affinity for multi-PO invoices?

Attached: lumos_vendor_onboarding_form_v1.pdf (pdf) — basic fields we can provide.

Happy to hop on a short call next week — Susan and our AP lead (accounts-payable@lumos-inc.com) are copied.

Thanks,
Susan Lee\Vendor Operations\Lumos Inc.


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Susan Lee <susan.lee@lumos-inc.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Jul 2025 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding + invoice submission options

Hi Susan — thanks for the context and the form.

Quick summary of recommended paths (pros/cons):
- Portal API (recommended if you can call out from your stack): low friction for our ops, supports PO affinity, real-time ACKs, tokenized submission. Requires mapping during onboarding but gives best visibility.
- SFTP (batch): good if you already generate nightly files; we can pickup CSVs. We'll provide a sample CSV schema and an idempotency key. Lower visibility on immediate ACKs but reliable for high-volume vendors.
- Webhook (push to our endpoint): only for real-time integrations — needs engineering support.
- Email fallback: support email-based submissions with subject token (e.g., SUBMIT-REDWOOD:vendor_id). Works for small volume or when other channels aren't available; more manual ops and higher failure rate for malformed PDFs.

PO stamping: we support three modes — single-PO enforcement, line-item PO matching, and free-form (AP tags PO numbers in invoice). For multi-PO invoices we prefer a line-item CSV with explicit PO columns to avoid manual reconciliation.

I've attached a short draft playbook that includes a sample CSV (example-po-stamped-invoice.csv) and the minimal required headers. Can you confirm Lumos can produce a CSV with: invoice_id, invoice_date, line_number, po_number, sku, qty, unit_price, tax, total? If not, let us know which fields you can supply.

Proposed next step: brief call next Tue 7/8 to pick channel and agree on a pilot (one month) — happy to run a small validation batch.

Cheers,
Dev
Dev Patel\Onboarding & AP Integrations\Redwood Inference

source.subject: Private deployment observability: network & SIEM handoff
messages:
From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 15:12:09 -0400
Subject: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Hi Aditya / Kim,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We kicked off a security review and the team needs a concrete list of network egress paths and an approach for shipping logs/traces from the private deployment into our Splunk tenant. Specific asks from our side:

- Minimum outbound ports/IPs required (hec/syslog/s3/kafka endpoints) and whether connections are client-initiated or need persistent tunnels.
- If mutual TLS is required for HEC; cert rotation expectations and supported CA options.
- A short-runbook for incident evidence transfer (how we request packetized evidence, transport, retention).
- Any on-host agents vs. forwarder preference for private on-prem installs.

We attached our internal compliance checklist. Can you confirm what you need from our infra team to run the onramp? Also please include sample firewall rules and a sample allowlist.

Pat
Ops Lead, Acme Logistics

Attachment: acme-compliance-checklist.docx (docx)
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 17:05:44 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — thanks for the clear list. I pulled together a concise network + observability handoff that should map to the checklist. High level answers first, then a proposed runbook and attachments.

High level:
- Outbound connections are client-initiated from the private control plane to our ingest endpoints. No inbound firewall holes are required into customer networks.
- Supported transports: HTTPS HEC (Splunk), TLS-wrapped syslog over TCP, S3-compatible object push for batch evidence, and Kafka (TLS) where customers already host a broker.
- HEC: mutual TLS is supported and recommended for higher assurance; we also support token-based HEC over TLS if MTLS is operationally difficult. Cert rotation: we recommend 90-day rotation with automated renewal via your internal CA or HashiVault — we document the API hooks in the attached checklist.
- Forwarder pattern: preferred model is a lightweight forwarder (constrained container) that performs local buffering, checksum bundling and signs evidence packets before pushing to Redwood-controlled ingest endpoints. This minimizes required changes to customer SIEM.

Proposed firewall allowlist (egress only) — minimal set (sample):
- 55.116.210.10/32 TCP 443 (redwood-hec-prod-1.redwood.com) — Splunk HEC endpoint for tenant ingest
- 52.18.34.0/24 TCP 443 (redwood-metrics-prod.redwood.com) — metrics push endpoint (Prometheus remote write over TLS)
- 34.201.12.87/32 TCP 514 (syslog-tls.redwood.com) — TCP/TLS syslog aggregator (optional)
- 13.59.23.128/32 TCP 443 (s3-redwood-evidence.us-west-2.amazonaws.com) — S3-compatible evidence bucket interface
- NTP outbound UDP 123 to your internal NTP or allowed pool (time drift impacts signed evidence)

Notes: we provide static egress IPs for Dedicated/private contracts; if you're using ephemeral NAT IPs we recommend setting up a small NAT pool and letting us know the range so we can update ACLs.

Runbook (short):
1) Pre-onramp: exchange tenant IDs, public keys for MTLS, and preferred ingest mode (HEC vs S3).
2) Networking: customer provisions egress ACLs for the allowlist (sample attached).
3) Deployment: we supply a forwarder container image and Helm chart (on-prem) or systemd unit (bare metal).
4) Acceptance: push a signed evidence packet and verify checksums; complete basic trace correlation sample (span id preserved).
5) Production: enable continuous metrics remote-write, set retention windows, and onboard into your Splunk index with provided field mappings.

Attachments: redwood-network-nexus-diagram-v2.pdf — includes sample topology with peering/VPN variants; siem-handshake-checklist.xlsx — mapping of checklist items to delivery owners; egress-allowlist.txt — copy-pasteable ACLs.

I'll loop in Jordan for the trace hook specifics and Marissa for the acceptance criteria. If you want, we can schedule a 30-min run-through with your infra team tomorrow afternoon.

— Aditya
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 09:48:22 -0400
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Aditya — this looks great. A few clarifications from our security team below:

- For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)? We will not share private keys; we can publish an intermediate CA bundle.
- Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json that includes UTC timestamps, sha256 checksums for each log/trace file, and the forwarder signature. Do you provide tooling to validate signatures client-side?
- Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs and preservation of trace/span metadata when exported to Splunk; our APM team will validate with a small trace run.

Also, Splunk onboarding asked whether you prefer to map logs into a neutral JSON schema prior to HEC ingest or to rely on field extraction rules inside Splunk. Their concern is consistent parsing across environments.

Can we also get the brief runbook inserted into our internal ticket system (I've attached the ticket) so our network team can pick it up.

Pat

Attachment: ACME-REQ-3987-ticket.pdf (pdf)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 09:03:11 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — quick follow-ups and commitments from our side: 

1) MTLS: yes — we can accept a customer-supplied intermediate CA bundle. Our forwarder validates server certs and presents a client cert signed by the customer CA. We will never request private keys. Rotation: we provide hooks for automated replacement; we can also accept short-lived certs issued from your vault (HashiCorp/Enterprise PKI).

2) Evidence packet: we will provide a reference validator (small Go binary + scripts) that checks manifest.json, verifies sha256 checksums and validates the forwarder signature (ED25519). I attached a sample manifest in the design PDF and included a minimal packet example in the archive on the Drive link.

3) Tracing: our tracing hooks preserve 128-bit trace IDs and inject a redwood.trace_id field when forwarding spans to Splunk. Splunk team — we recommend pre-normalizing into a canonical JSON envelope at the forwarder; that reduces field-extraction variance. If you prefer to handle extraction in Splunk, we can ship field mapping docs instead.

4) Runbook insertion: Ack — Aditya will push the runbook into your ticketing system; we can do a quick handoff call to walk the network engineers through the ACLs.

Next steps:
- Aditya: share the allowlist file and the Helm chart link (I pushed the chart to the Drive folder).
- Kimberly: start a compliance evidence packet template that matches your retention requirements and legal hold controls.
- Marissa: I'll coordinate an integration test window (we propose Jul 10 22:00-23:30 UTC for an acceptance run).

Thanks — Jordan
Staff Software Engineer, Observability

Quoted: 
> On Tue, 01 Jul 2025 at 09:48, Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com> wrote:
> > For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)?
> > Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json...
> > Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs...

Attachment referenced: redwood-network-nexus-diagram-v2.pdf, siem-handshake-checklist.xlsx, egress-allowlist.txt

3) Tracing: our tracing hooks preserve 128-bit trace IDs and inject a redwood.trace_id field when forwarding spans to Splunk. Splunk team — we recommend pre-normalizing into a canonical JSON envelope at the forwarder; that reduces field-extraction variance. If you prefer to handle extraction in Splunk, we can ship field mapping docs instead.

4) Runbook insertion: Ack — Aditya will push the runbook into your ticketing system; we can do a quick handoff call to walk the network engineers through the ACLs.

Next steps:
- Aditya: share the allowlist file and the Helm chart link (I pushed the chart to the Drive folder).
- Kimberly: start a compliance evidence packet template that matches your retention requirements and legal hold controls.
- Marissa: I'll coordinate an integration test window (we propose Jul 10 22:00-23:30 UTC for an acceptance run).

Thanks — Jordan
Staff Software Engineer, Observability

Quoted: 
> On Tue, 01 Jul 2025 at 09:48, Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com> wrote:
> > For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)?
> > Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json...
> > Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs...

Attachment referenced: redwood-network-nexus-diagram-v2.pdf, siem-handshake-checklist.xlsx, egress-allowlist.txt
text:
From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 15:12:09 -0400
Subject: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Hi Aditya / Kim,

Thanks for the walkthrough last week. We kicked off a security review and the team needs a concrete list of network egress paths and an approach for shipping logs/traces from the private deployment into our Splunk tenant. Specific asks from our side:

- Minimum outbound ports/IPs required (hec/syslog/s3/kafka endpoints) and whether connections are client-initiated or need persistent tunnels.
- If mutual TLS is required for HEC; cert rotation expectations and supported CA options.
- A short-runbook for incident evidence transfer (how we request packetized evidence, transport, retention).
- Any on-host agents vs. forwarder preference for private on-prem installs.

We attached our internal compliance checklist. Can you confirm what you need from our infra team to run the onramp? Also please include sample firewall rules and a sample allowlist.

Pat
Ops Lead, Acme Logistics

Attachment: acme-compliance-checklist.docx (docx)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Jun 2025 17:05:44 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — thanks for the clear list. I pulled together a concise network + observability handoff that should map to the checklist. High level answers first, then a proposed runbook and attachments.

High level:
- Outbound connections are client-initiated from the private control plane to our ingest endpoints. No inbound firewall holes are required into customer networks.
- Supported transports: HTTPS HEC (Splunk), TLS-wrapped syslog over TCP, S3-compatible object push for batch evidence, and Kafka (TLS) where customers already host a broker.
- HEC: mutual TLS is supported and recommended for higher assurance; we also support token-based HEC over TLS if MTLS is operationally difficult. Cert rotation: we recommend 90-day rotation with automated renewal via your internal CA or HashiVault — we document the API hooks in the attached checklist.
- Forwarder pattern: preferred model is a lightweight forwarder (constrained container) that performs local buffering, checksum bundling and signs evidence packets before pushing to Redwood-controlled ingest endpoints. This minimizes required changes to customer SIEM.

Proposed firewall allowlist (egress only) — minimal set (sample):
- 55.116.210.10/32 TCP 443 (redwood-hec-prod-1.redwood.com) — Splunk HEC endpoint for tenant ingest
- 52.18.34.0/24 TCP 443 (redwood-metrics-prod.redwood.com) — metrics push endpoint (Prometheus remote write over TLS)
- 34.201.12.87/32 TCP 514 (syslog-tls.redwood.com) — TCP/TLS syslog aggregator (optional)
- 13.59.23.128/32 TCP 443 (s3-redwood-evidence.us-west-2.amazonaws.com) — S3-compatible evidence bucket interface
- NTP outbound UDP 123 to your internal NTP or allowed pool (time drift impacts signed evidence)

Notes: we provide static egress IPs for Dedicated/private contracts; if you're using ephemeral NAT IPs we recommend setting up a small NAT pool and letting us know the range so we can update ACLs.

Runbook (short):
1) Pre-onramp: exchange tenant IDs, public keys for MTLS, and preferred ingest mode (HEC vs S3).
2) Networking: customer provisions egress ACLs for the allowlist (sample attached).
3) Deployment: we supply a forwarder container image and Helm chart (on-prem) or systemd unit (bare metal).
4) Acceptance: push a signed evidence packet and verify checksums; complete basic trace correlation sample (span id preserved).
5) Production: enable continuous metrics remote-write, set retention windows, and onboard into your Splunk index with provided field mappings.

Attachments: redwood-network-nexus-diagram-v2.pdf — includes sample topology with peering/VPN variants; siem-handshake-checklist.xlsx — mapping of checklist items to delivery owners; egress-allowlist.txt — copy-pasteable ACLs.

I'll loop in Jordan for the trace hook specifics and Marissa for the acceptance criteria. If you want, we can schedule a 30-min run-through with your infra team tomorrow afternoon.

— Aditya
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Acme Cloud Security <ops@acmecloud.com>
Date: Tue, 01 Jul 2025 09:48:22 -0400
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Aditya — this looks great. A few clarifications from our security team below:

- For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)? We will not share private keys; we can publish an intermediate CA bundle.
- Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json that includes UTC timestamps, sha256 checksums for each log/trace file, and the forwarder signature. Do you provide tooling to validate signatures client-side?
- Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs and preservation of trace/span metadata when exported to Splunk; our APM team will validate with a small trace run.

Also, Splunk onboarding asked whether you prefer to map logs into a neutral JSON schema prior to HEC ingest or to rely on field extraction rules inside Splunk. Their concern is consistent parsing across environments.

Can we also get the brief runbook inserted into our internal ticket system (I've attached the ticket) so our network team can pick it up.

Pat

Attachment: ACME-REQ-3987-ticket.pdf (pdf)


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Splunk Onboarding <onboarding@splunk.com>
Date: Wed, 02 Jul 2025 09:03:11 -04:00
Subject: Re: Integrating Redwood private deploy with Acme SIEM (network details)

Pat — quick follow-ups and commitments from our side: 

1) MTLS: yes — we can accept a customer-supplied intermediate CA bundle. Our forwarder validates server certs and presents a client cert signed by the customer CA. We will never request private keys. Rotation: we provide hooks for automated replacement; we can also accept short-lived certs issued from your vault (HashiCorp/Enterprise PKI).

2) Evidence packet: we will provide a reference validator (small Go binary + scripts) that checks manifest.json, verifies sha256 checksums and validates the forwarder signature (ED25519). I attached a sample manifest in the design PDF and included a minimal packet example in the archive on the Drive link.

3) Tracing: our tracing hooks preserve 128-bit trace IDs and inject a redwood.trace_id field when forwarding spans to Splunk. Splunk team — we recommend pre-normalizing into a canonical JSON envelope at the forwarder; that reduces field-extraction variance. If you prefer to handle extraction in Splunk, we can ship field mapping docs instead.

4) Runbook insertion: Ack — Aditya will push the runbook into your ticketing system; we can do a quick handoff call to walk the network engineers through the ACLs.

Next steps:
- Aditya: share the allowlist file and the Helm chart link (I pushed the chart to the Drive folder).
- Kimberly: start a compliance evidence packet template that matches your retention requirements and legal hold controls.
- Marissa: I'll coordinate an integration test window (we propose Jul 10 22:00-23:30 UTC for an acceptance run).

Thanks — Jordan
Staff Software Engineer, Observability

> On Tue, 01 Jul 2025 at 09:48, Pat Taylor <ptaylor@acmeops.com> wrote:
> > For the HEC MTLS option: can Redwood accept our internally-issued certs (private CA)?
> > Evidence packet format: we need an example ZIP with a manifest.json...
> > Tracing: we require 128-bit trace IDs...

source.subject: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 21 Jun 2025 14:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Hi Alex —

Great to meet earlier and thanks for walking us through BrightCart's ingestion flow and expected throughput. Quick recap + proposed next steps from our side: 

- Fit: Hosted API looks like the fastest onramp for your use case (no infra lift), with Dedicated as an option once you hit sustained peak traffic or need guaranteed capacity.
- Key asks we flagged: expected peak concurrent requests (~40 qps), burst patterns (short spikes up to ~150 qps during promotions), and data residency for EU shoppers (you mentioned EU region requirement for parts of the service).

Next steps we propose: 
1) We’ll provision a sandbox Hosted endpoint for you — endpoint details and token below. This will be rate-limited by default; we’ll propose a rate plan and a temporary higher burst slice for the pilot.
2) Share a one-page quickstart checklist and a small sample endpoint capacity CSV for you to validate against your traffic.
3) If you want predictable latency/SLA later, we can sketch a Dedicated capacity baseline and estimated monthly commit.

I’ll loop Ben for the concrete ratebands and Marissa for the onboarding runbook. Expect the sandbox token + checklists within 24 hours.

Thanks again — looking forward to a short pilot.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/jordan-blake

From: Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Jun 2025 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Jordan —

Thanks for the clear recap. Confirming a few things from our side: 

- Peak and burst numbers are correct (40 qps steady, promo spikes up to 150 qps over a 10–15 minute window).
- We need EU residency for any PII-derived embeddings, but non-PII inference can run in us-west for the pilot.
- Timeline: team can start integration next Tuesday (June 24).

Please send the sandbox token and the quickstart checklist. Also can you include a suggested rateband (tokens/sec and allowlist for burst) so we can validate locally? If Hosted proves noisy in terms of unit cost we may want to test a small Dedicated baseline — happy to get a ballpark.

Cheers,
Alex Turner
Head of Platform Engineering, BrightCart
alex@brightcart.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Jun 2025 08:02:00 -0700
Subject: FW: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Looping in suggested numbers for the pilot and an internal checklist: 

Suggested Hosted pilot ratebands (initial):
- Default: 30 qps sustained, per-endpoint token throttle at 15 tokens/sec (keeps latency predictable)
- Pilot burst slice: 120 qps for 30 minutes/day window for two weeks
- Backpressure: 429 + Retry-After header for sustained exceed; circuit-breaker at 200 qps sustained per endpoint

Notes: these are conservative so BrightCart can validate behavior without overrun. If they confirm traffic profile we can increase sustained limits or propose Dedicated estimates (rough calc below).

Dedicated baseline (rough, for planning only):
- GPU pool sized for 200 qps steady (mixed short-context gen) ≈ 2 x A10-equivalent nodes reserved; estimated commit ~$18k/month (subject to model/quantization).

Attachments I’m dropping here: redwood-quickstart-checklist.pdf and sample-endpoint-capacity-plan.csv — includes a sample mapping of endpoints to expected qps and a checklist for keys, CORS, and prefetched KV cache entries.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Jun 2025 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Quickstart endpoints, capacity & rate plan (next steps)

Alex —

Thanks — confirmed on the timeline. Attached are the two files Ben mentioned. Quick pointers and the sandbox token below: 

- Sandbox endpoint: https://api.redwood.ai/v1/hosted/brightcart-sandbox (token: sk_test_brightcart_20250623)
- Rate plan: we provisioned the initial default and the pilot burst slice Ben suggested. You’ll see 429 responses with Retry-After when the burst window is exhausted.
- EU residency: we’ve enabled geo-routing for embeddings so PII-derived embeddings can be routed to eu-central-1. Engineers will need to set region=eu in the request header for those calls.

Requested next actions: 
1) BrightCart: validate the attached sample-endpoint-capacity-plan.csv against your traffic (highlight any endpoints >120 qps).
2) BrightCart: attempt a small integration on June 24 and share any 429 patterns + example requests.
3) Redwood: we’ll prepare a short Dedicated sketch if you ask for cost-comparison after the pilot.

I’ll also drop the HubSpot link for the deal and the DocuSign for a mutual NDA if you prefer it signed before sending sample data.

HubSpot: https://hubspot.redwood.ai/deal/BRIGHT-1294
DocuSign NDA (optional): https://www.docusign.com/envelopes/E-123456789

Look forward to Tuesday — ping me on Slack if you want the sandbox rotated or a wider burst window.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0134
Redwood Inference — Production-first inference for builders

On Sun, 22 Jun 2025 at 09:18, Alex Turner <alex@brightcart.com> wrote:
> Jordan —
>
> Thanks for the clear recap. Confirming a few things from our side: 
>
> - Peak and burst numbers are correct (40 qps steady, promo spikes up to 150 qps over a 10–15 minute window).
> - We need EU residency for any PII-derived embeddings, but non-PII inference can run in us-west for the pilot.
> - Timeline: team can start integration next Tuesday (June 24).
>
> Please send the sandbox token and the quickstart checklist. Also can you include a suggested rateband (tokens/sec and allowlist for burst) so we can validate locally? If Hosted proves noisy in terms of unit cost we may want to test a small Dedicated baseline — happy to get a ballpark.
>
> Cheers,
> Alex Turner
> Head of Platform Engineering, BrightCart
> alex@brightcart.com

> Please send the sandbox token and the quickstart checklist. Also can you include a suggested rateband (tokens/sec and allowlist for burst) so we can validate locally? If Hosted proves noisy in terms of unit cost we may want to test a small Dedicated baseline — happy to get a ballpark.
>
> Cheers,
> Alex Turner
> Head of Platform Engineering, BrightCart
> alex@brightcart.com

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir.k@integral.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Economic delta worksheet & implementation risk checklist

Hi Samir —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation, I pulled together a compact delta worksheet that maps expected monthly spend, latency/SLO buckets, and an initial staged-credit proposal so your procurement team can see a concrete replacement path. The attached worksheet models three adoption scenarios (Pilot / Ramp / Steady-state) and shows our recommended credit + technical backfill for the migration window.

A couple quick notes:
- We think a tiered temporary credit (higher % in Pilot, step down into Ramp) keeps your unit economics neutral while removing immediate cash outlay.
- We include an ops backfill line for early-stage SRE work (hand-holding + runbook integration) — customers find this reduces cutover risk.

Attachment: [Attachment: 2025_06_18_delta_worksheet.xlsx (spreadsheet)]

Would you be open to a short follow-up with procurement this week to walk through the numbers? If you can share rough 3-month usage (monthly token counts / peak qps), I can re-run the workbook with your exact profile.

Thanks,
Connor O'Brien
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
connor.obrien@redwood.com | (415) 555-0198

--

Connor —

1) Can you map the staged credits to explicit dollar amounts by month for our Pilot and Ramp buckets? We need the month-by-month cash impact for our CFO.
2) What are the success criteria to move from Pilot -> Ramp (latency/accuracy thresholds, onboarding milestones)? We want clear exit gates.
3) On parity: your notes mention "runtime compatibility" — what guarantees do we have that models/latency won't degrade vs our current vendor during the first 30 days?

Cc'ing Lisa from procurement — she will own the contract ask. If possible, please include an example credit term (how it appears in the MSA or SOW) and any standard rollback language.

Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 09:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Economic delta worksheet & implementation risk checklist

Marissa / Jordan —

Looping you in: IntegralAI is asking for month-by-month cash impact, explicit success criteria for Pilot -> Ramp, and assurances around parity during the first 30 days. They cc'd procurement (Lisa).

Can you advise on two things quickly:
- Credit structure: pilot % and ramp phase-down cadence that you'd be comfortable approving for a deal of this scale (likely $400–700k ARR first year if we get steady-state).
- Operational commitment: what level of SRE hours / runbook work do we commit to vs recommend as professional services? Should we map an ops backfill line item rather than unlimited SRE support?

Attachment: [Attachment: redwood_credit_playbook.pdf (pdf)]

If you want me to put numbers into the worksheet before we send to Samir, say the word and I will.

Connor

> >

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Economic delta worksheet & implementation risk checklist

Connor / Jordan —

Recommended concession structure (compact):
- Pilot (month 0–2): 60% credit on incremental consumption capped at $45k per month OR a fixed $80k pilot credit bucket (whichever is greater).
- Ramp (month 3–5): 30% credit on incremental consumption, step-down to 15% in month 6.
- Conversion: remaining credit amortized over first 6 months of steady-state billing.

Success criteria (sample gates):
- Latency: p95 within 1.2x of incumbent for top 3 endpoints for two consecutive weeks.
- Accuracy: scoring on your eval set (we can import) within +/- 2% for primary NLU intents.
- Operational: integration checklist completed (API keys, VPC controls, tracing hooks), and successful live run of a 12-hour traffic replay.

Ops commitment: carve out a 40-hour "backfill" bucket (covered by credits) to be used for runbook integration, initial triage, and onboarding Q&A. Anything beyond that is billed as professional services at standard PS rates. This limits open-ended SRE commitments while giving them the hand-holding they ask for.

I attached a quick concession matrix and a sample SOW clause we use for pilot credits. Include HubSpot 835211 in the DocuSign envelope so legal links to the right deal.

Attachment: [Attachment: concession_matrix_20250618.csv (spreadsheet)]
Related: https://hubspot.com/deal/835211, https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c_concession_docs

Marissa Cole
Director, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

> > Marissa / Jordan —
> > 
> > Looping you in: IntegralAI is asking for month-by-month cash impact...

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir.k@integral.ai>, Lisa Park <lisa.park@novasol.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Jun 2025 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Economic delta worksheet & implementation risk checklist

Samir / Lisa —

Thanks for the quick questions. Based on the internal playbook (attached) and a conservative migration plan, here's a formalized version of what we can offer to match the near-term economics while protecting both sides from open-ended risk:

Proposal (summary):
- Pilot (months 0–2): $80,000 pilot credit bucket (applied to usage). This is equivalent to a ~60% credit on typical pilot volumes we modeled.
- Ramp (months 3–5): 30% usage credit applied to incremental consumption vs baseline; month 6 step-down to 15%.
- Conversion amortization: any unused credit converts into a dollar offset applied over months 7–12 at no greater than 10% of monthly invoice.
- Ops backfill: 40 hours of SRE/onboarding support covered inside the pilot credit. Additional SRE hours are available via PS engagement (we can include a separate SOW).

Exit gates (Pilot -> Ramp):
- p95 latency for primary endpoints <= 1.2x incumbent for two consecutive weeks
- Accuracy on agreed eval set within +/- 2%
- Integration checklist completed and validated (we'll run a 12-hour traffic replay jointly)

Contract language: we'll include a short SOW appendix that lists credit buckets, amortization rules, and a rollback clause that enables either party to pause the credits with 30 days notice if the integration fails to meet agreed gates. I'll draft the SOW and share a DocuSign link for execution.

Attachments: [Attachment: 2025_06_18_delta_worksheet.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Attachment: concession_matrix_20250618.csv (spreadsheet)]
Related links: https://hubspot.com/deal/835211, https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c_concession_docs

If that looks reasonable, would you like the SOW and DocuSign envelope today? Happy to hop on a 30-minute call with procurement to walk through the month-by-month cash impact.

Best,
Connor O'Brien
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
connor.obrien@redwood.com | (415) 555-0198

messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@meditech.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Jun 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Draft: evaluation checkpoints & acceptance timeline (GDoc seed)

Hi Samira —

Thank you again for the demo earlier this week. As promised, I put together a lightweight seed for the evaluation checkpoints and a proposed acceptance timeline so your tech and procurement teams can validate next steps.

What I shared:
- A short GDoc with checkpoints by week (tech validation, performance targets, privacy/security checklist, success criteria).
- A one-page acceptance window (milestones + sign-off owners).

Ask:
1) Please review the GDoc and add any internal names/constraints (procurement timing, legal hold windows).
2) Do you have hard dates for the pilot start and the final acceptance review? We used 2025-07-07 as a placeholder in the draft.

I’ve CC’d Marissa (solutions lead) and Jordan (supporting SE) — they’ll help flesh the tech validation steps and align on the KV caching / perf targets we demoed.

Quick attachment stubs: eval-checkpoints-gdoc-seed.gdoc (link inside), MediTech-Security-Reqs.pdf (your security questionnaire from the demo).

If helpful, we can hop on a 30-min alignment call Friday to finalize owners and dates.

Thanks,
Evelyn
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0192

---
Note: GDoc link (editable for Samira): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1a2b3c-eval-checkpoints-seed

From: Samira Khan <samira@meditech.io>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@meditech.io
Date: Thu, 12 Jun 2025 10:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Draft: evaluation checkpoints & acceptance timeline (GDoc seed)

Hi Evelyn —

Appreciate the quick turn. I skimmed the doc and it looks sensible. A few immediate notes from procurement and infra:
- Procurement: we need an estimated TCO ballpark for the pilot (monthly), and a shortlist of any non-standard contract clauses. I’ve asked procurement@meditech.io to add constraints in the doc.
- Infra/Security: please confirm whether audit logs and token-level retention are available for the pilot (we flagged data residency during the demo).

Can you have Marissa add a crisp perf acceptance row (p95 target at X tokens/sec) and a short runbook for reproducing latency spikes? That will speed internal sign-off.

I’m copying procurement so they can flag timing — we currently have a budget window that starts 2025-07-01 but final legal review can push to late July if we need to.

Samira
Director, Platform Engineering
MediTech

>

source.subject: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints
source.body:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Jordan + Evelyn —

We’re kicking off the public benchmark report focused on production inference economics (latency/$) across a curated model + region set. Before we get deep into runs + content, I want to align on (1) goals, (2) timeline, and (3) executive review checkpoints so we don’t drift and so Legal is looped at the right moments.

Proposed goals (what we’re optimizing for):
- Credible, technically defensible report + interactive page that engineers can trust and share.
- Clear story for Dedicated + Private pipeline (regional consistency + predictable throughput + operational controls), without over-claiming.
- Reproducible methodology artifacts retained internally (manifests/logs) and a publishable “how we measured” section.

What we will NOT do:
- No “best overall” / absolute claims. No cherry-picked prompts. No “X% faster than <competitor>” unless Legal signs off on substantiation + caveats.

Timeline (high level):
- Week 1–2 (Jun 3–Jun 14): methodology lock + instrumentation, model/region list locked, review checklist.
- Week 3–5 (Jun 15–Jul 5): region sweeps + validation, draft narrative + charts.
- Week 6–7 (Jul 6–Jul 19): internal reviews (Eng/PM/Sales/Legal/Security), finalize PDF + web.
- Week 8 (Jul 20–Jul 26): launch + webinar + post-launch monitoring.

Exec checkpoints I’m proposing:
1) Methodology sign-off (what counts as “fair”) — Jun 12/13
2) “So what?” / key findings review (high-level chart review) — week of Jun 30
3) Final claims + messaging sign-off (post-Legal) — week of Jul 7
4) Go/no-go readiness (launch checklist: web, PDF, enablement, support) — week of Jul 14

Asks:
- Jordan: can you confirm you’re comfortable with the scope and the above checkpoints? Anything you need specifically for Sales leadership enablement timing?
- Evelyn: would appreciate your view on how aggressive we should be on any competitor context (we’ll keep caveats prominent), and what an “executive-level” substantiation threshold should look like.

Attachments:
- Redwood_Benchmark_Report_Exec_OnePager_v0.3.pdf
- Benchmark_Report_Timeline_v0.2.xlsx

Thanks —
Camila

—
Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Works for me. I like the explicit “what we will not do” section — we should keep that tight in enablement too.

Two notes/asks from the revenue side:
1) Dedicated/Private angle: I want the takeaways to map cleanly to a sales motion (Hosted = get started fast; Dedicated = predictable throughput + isolation; Private = regulated + data residency). If the report is purely “hosted API latency/$”, we need a bridge in the narrative.
2) Competitive context: I’m fine with “contextual baselines” but only if we can explain it in one breath and point to methodology. Otherwise, we should lead with Redwood-only numbers + reproducibility.

Enablement timing: ideally we have a one-slide summary + top 5 FAQs by end of week of Jul 7 so Sales can start using it in outbound immediately.

If you can get the methodology sign-off on the calendar by next week, I’ll join.

Jordan

—
Jordan Blake
CRO, Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 2:21 PM PT, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 2:21 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Agree on the bridge. We’ll make sure the report includes a short section on “How to interpret the benchmark for your deployment mode” (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private) and we’ll also ensure the landing page CTAs route accordingly.

Re: competitor context — aligned. Default is Redwood-only. If we include any competitor baselines, it’ll be:
- clearly labeled as “context,”
- method + configs disclosed at a high level,
- strict caveats (variance, region differences, workload mismatch),
- and cleared by Legal.

Soojin — can you draft the Sales/SE asset timeline (one-slide + FAQ + talk track) to match Jordan’s Jul 7 target? We’ll keep it tied to the same “disallowed claims” list.

Evelyn — would still love your guidance on substantiation threshold / anything you’ve seen go sideways with benchmark launches.

I’ll have my assistant send holds for:
- Jun 13: methodology sign-off
- Jul 1: findings preview
- Jul 9: final claims/messaging sign-off
- Jul 15: go/no-go

Camila


On Thu, Jun 5, 2025 at 7:26 AM PT, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com> wrote:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jun 5, 2025 at 7:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

This is the right structure.

Substantiation threshold (what I’d treat as “exec-safe”):
- You can trace every headline number back to a run manifest and a retained raw log set.
- You can explain the workload in plain English (prefill vs decode, concurrency, cache policy) and why it maps to production.
- You have an explicit statement of what the benchmark does NOT represent (e.g., “this is not a universal ranking across all workloads”).
- For any comparisons: assume scrutiny. If Legal is not comfortable, don’t do it. It’s better to win on transparency.

Also: plan for external critique on “fairness.” Have a short prepared response that points to methodology + caveats, and be ready to share enough detail for a third party to reproduce (even if they won’t).

One last thought: if the interactive page is the main entry point, ensure the disclaimers are unmissable (not only in the PDF footnotes) and that export includes the same caveats.

Happy to join the methodology and final claims checkpoints.

Evelyn

—
Evelyn Hart
Operating Partner, Northstar VC

Happy to join the methodology and final claims checkpoints.

Evelyn

—
Evelyn Hart
Operating Partner, Northstar VC
text:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 8:12 AM PT
Subject: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Jordan + Evelyn —

We’re kicking off the public benchmark report focused on production inference economics (latency/$) across a curated model + region set. Before we get deep into runs + content, I want to align on (1) goals, (2) timeline, and (3) executive review checkpoints so we don’t drift and so Legal is looped at the right moments.

Proposed goals (what we’re optimizing for):
- Credible, technically defensible report + interactive page that engineers can trust and share.
- Clear story for Dedicated + Private pipeline (regional consistency + predictable throughput + operational controls), without over-claiming.
- Reproducible methodology artifacts retained internally (manifests/logs) and a publishable “how we measured” section.

What we will NOT do:
- No “best overall” / absolute claims. No cherry-picked prompts. No “X% faster than <competitor>” unless Legal signs off on substantiation + caveats.

Timeline (high level):
- Week 1–2 (Jun 3–Jun 14): methodology lock + instrumentation, model/region list locked, review checklist.
- Week 3–5 (Jun 15–Jul 5): region sweeps + validation, draft narrative + charts.
- Week 6–7 (Jul 6–Jul 19): internal reviews (Eng/PM/Sales/Legal/Security), finalize PDF + web.
- Week 8 (Jul 20–Jul 26): launch + webinar + post-launch monitoring.

Exec checkpoints I’m proposing:
1) Methodology sign-off (what counts as “fair”) — Jun 12/13
2) “So what?” / key findings review (high-level chart review) — week of Jun 30
3) Final claims + messaging sign-off (post-Legal) — week of Jul 7
4) Go/no-go readiness (launch checklist: web, PDF, enablement, support) — week of Jul 14

Asks:
- Jordan: can you confirm you’re comfortable with the scope and the above checkpoints? Anything you need specifically for Sales leadership enablement timing?
- Evelyn: would appreciate your view on how aggressive we should be on any competitor context (we’ll keep caveats prominent), and what an “executive-level” substantiation threshold should look like.

Attachments:
- Redwood_Benchmark_Report_Exec_OnePager_v0.3.pdf
- Benchmark_Report_Timeline_v0.2.xlsx

Thanks —
Camila

—
Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Works for me. I like the explicit “what we will not do” section — we should keep that tight in enablement too.

Two notes/asks from the revenue side:
1) Dedicated/Private angle: I want the takeaways to map cleanly to a sales motion (Hosted = get started fast; Dedicated = predictable throughput + isolation; Private = regulated + data residency). If the report is purely “hosted API latency/$”, we need a bridge in the narrative.
2) Competitive context: I’m fine with “contextual baselines” but only if we can explain it in one breath and point to methodology. Otherwise, we should lead with Redwood-only numbers + reproducibility.

Enablement timing: ideally we have a one-slide summary + top 5 FAQs by end of week of Jul 7 so Sales can start using it in outbound immediately.

If you can get the methodology sign-off on the calendar by next week, I’ll join.

Jordan

—
Jordan Blake
CRO, Redwood Inference


On Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 2:21 PM PT, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 4, 2025 at 2:21 PM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

Agree on the bridge. We’ll make sure the report includes a short section on “How to interpret the benchmark for your deployment mode” (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private) and we’ll also ensure the landing page CTAs route accordingly.

Re: competitor context — aligned. Default is Redwood-only. If we include any competitor baselines, it’ll be:
- clearly labeled as “context,”
- method + configs disclosed at a high level,
- strict caveats (variance, region differences, workload mismatch),
- and cleared by Legal.

Soojin — can you draft the Sales/SE asset timeline (one-slide + FAQ + talk track) to match Jordan’s Jul 7 target? We’ll keep it tied to the same “disallowed claims” list.

Evelyn — would still love your guidance on substantiation threshold / anything you’ve seen go sideways with benchmark launches.

I’ll have my assistant send holds for:
- Jun 13: methodology sign-off
- Jul 1: findings preview
- Jul 9: final claims/messaging sign-off
- Jul 15: go/no-go

Camila


On Thu, Jun 5, 2025 at 7:26 AM PT, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com> wrote:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@northstarvc.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, Jun 5, 2025 at 7:26 AM PT
Subject: Re: Exec alignment: Benchmark report (latency/$) — goals, timeline, and review checkpoints

This is the right structure.

Substantiation threshold (what I’d treat as “exec-safe”):
- You can trace every headline number back to a run manifest and a retained raw log set.
- You can explain the workload in plain English (prefill vs decode, concurrency, cache policy) and why it maps to production.
- You have an explicit statement of what the benchmark does NOT represent (e.g., “this is not a universal ranking across all workloads”).
- For any comparisons: assume scrutiny. If Legal is not comfortable, don’t do it. It’s better to win on transparency.

Also: plan for external critique on “fairness.” Have a short prepared response that points to methodology + caveats, and be ready to share enough detail for a third party to reproduce (even if they won’t).

One last thought: if the interactive page is the main entry point, ensure the disclaimers are unmissable (not only in the PDF footnotes) and that export includes the same caveats.

Happy to join the methodology and final claims checkpoints.

Evelyn

—
Evelyn Hart
Operating Partner, Northstar VC

source.subject: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing
messages:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Team —

Attaching an updated Q3 sizing workbook and Cobalt's usage assumptions (see attachments). They just signaled a POC + 6 month pilot with expected ramp to 250 concurrent inference slots at peak if the pilot goes well. Ben, this is the opportunity we discussed on last week's call.

Agenda for our quick huddle:
- Validate the conservative/likely/aggressive GPU slot numbers (tab: scenarios)
- Confirm whether we can cover a canary of 20 slots in Phoenix for June and 50 slots for July (region note below)
- Finance constraints: what monthly committed spend / minimum reserve would be required to keep latency SLOs intact?
- Infra: what lead time, and which SKU (A100x2 vs H100x1) would you recommend for the canary vs ramp?

Quick asks before the call: please review the "infra_notes" tab and add a one-line comment in the workbook if a number looks off.

Attachments: 2025Q3_pipeline_sizing_v2.xlsx (workbook), Cobalt_MVP_usage_estimates.pdf (customer assumptions)

—Camila
Director, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Thanks Camila — reviewed the workbook. Short notes:  

- My conservative/likely/aggressive splits line up with the tab but I think the "likely" curve underestimates pilot conversion. Suggested: pilot -> 20 slots (canary), month 2 -> 80, month 3 -> 180 for the 6 month ramp.
- Cobalt has asked for an SLA of 99.5 p95 latency. That pushes us to reserved capacity vs pure burst.
- Proposed commercial: 3-month reserved commit for canary (20 slots) to secure capacity + monthly auto-scale burst credits. Will need finance sign-off but this is our working ask when we present pricing.

I'll add a new column in the sheet with my proposed commit profile. Can we aim to finalize optics by EOD tomorrow so I can fold into the HT lead's commercial?

-Ben
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 09:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
>
> Attaching an updated Q3 sizing workbook and Cobalt's usage assumptions...
>

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Following up on Ben's notes. Finance perspective: 

- We can support a 3-month reservation for the 20-slot canary if the ARR uplift projections in the workbook are met. Need explicit milestone-based credit terms (PO, signed SOW, payment within 30 days) to justify the short reservation.
- For a ramp to 180-250 slots we need a minimum 6- to 12-month commitment tier; otherwise cost of burst will exceed margin thresholds in month 3.
- Please add a cashflow projection (month-by-month) and I will run sensitivity for revenue recognition / deferred revenue impact.

I flagged a couple of assumptions in the sheet (row comments) where the customer forecast looks overly front-loaded. If they truly ramp slow, we should recommend a staged reserved schedule: 20 -> 80 -> 180 tied to signed milestones.

Laura
Director, Revenue Operations & Finance
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 10:04 AM Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Camila — reviewed the workbook. Short notes: ...
>

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 May 2025 14:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Infra constraints and recommended plan: 

- Short answer: we can stand up a 20-slot canary in PHX within 10 business days if we route to dedicated A100x2 pools. H100 provisioning lead times currently sit at ~6 weeks in our supply queue — so if the customer insists on H100 for latency/Perf, plan accordingly.
- For the July ramp to 50-80 slots, we can use a mixed SKU approach: canary on A100x2 reserved, burst to H100x1 where latency-critical. This preserves cost while honoring 99.5 p95 target.
- Key risk: simultaneous close of two other enterprise deals in July (see capacity reservations sheet). If both convert, we hit 85% pool utilization in PHX and will require cross-region spilling to us-west-2 (extra egress and latency impact).

Action items for infra:
1) Reserve 20 A100x2 slots in PHX (can set a 30-day hold)
2) Pre-book 50 H100x1 burst units for July (soft allocation)
3) Run a canary SLA test (5–10 synthetic clients) and capture p95 under load; send results to Camila and the Cobalt team.

-Jordan
Head of Infra Operations
Redwood Inference

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 11:03 AM Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com> wrote:
> Following up on Ben's notes. Finance perspective: ...
>

From: Ana Soto <ana.soto@cobalta.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 30 May 2025 16:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Weekly fleet scenario huddle: Cobalt Analytics inbound deal + Q3 sizing

Hi Camila / team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and for walking us through the workbook on the call today. A few confirmations from Cobalt:

- We agree to a 3-month reserved pilot for the 20-slot canary (PO will go to procurement next week).
- Cobalt requests the canary to be in PHX (lower RTT for our East Coast clients) and would like an option to convert to a 12-month commitment if KPIs (latency, throughput) are met by month 2.
- Procurement will need an expected pricing table + DocuSign SOW so we can calendar the PO. Can you share the DocuSign link and final pricing by EOD Monday?

Attachments: None (we will send PO pdf once procurement completes internal review)

Best,
Ana Soto
Head of ML Ops
Cobalt Analytics

On Thu, May 29, 2025 at 09:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
>
> Attaching an updated Q3 sizing workbook and Cobalt's usage assumptions...
>

source.subject: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks
related_links: HubSpot view: PLG Signals - NA Pilot Queue
source.body:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 8:14 AM PT
Subject: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks
Attachments: PLG_Signals_Pilot_Week1_Summary_2025-05-27.pdf (application/pdf)

Team — quick Week 1 readout for the NA PLG→sales signal pilot (Mon–Sun).

Cohort + volume
- Cohort: 212 NA self-serve companies (paid + high-usage trials) in the pilot list
- Signals generated (raw): 96
- Signals routed to HubSpot tasks (qualified after suppression/filters): 41
- Unique companies with ≥1 routed signal: 28
- Median time from signal to task creation: 19 min (p95: 2h 06m)

Early funnel outcomes (Week 1)
- First-touch completed (email or call logged): 17 / 28 (61%)
- Meetings booked: 6 / 28 (21%)
- “Interested / evaluating” (no meeting yet): 5 / 28 (18%)
- Disqualified as “too small / not a fit / wrong contact”: 7 / 28 (25%)

What worked
- Reliability pain signals are converting: repeated 429s + sustained token growth produced the highest meeting rate (4/9)
- Dedicated/Private intent (docs/security page sequences) led to good discovery calls when combined with usage scale (not by itself)

Noise / false positives (qualitative)
- AEs flagged ~8 tasks as “low signal” where intent was purely docs browsing (pricing + dedicated docs) with no corresponding usage or 429s.
- 2 instances where marketing nurture overlapped with AE outreach within 24h (tone felt “pile-on” to the customer). Camila/tyler team already tracking.
- One customer complaint came in re: outreach timing/assumptions (see SUP-3392) — appears tied to a docs-view-only signal that got through.

Proposed threshold + suppression adjustments (for Week 2)
1) Intent-only gating: require intent signals to be paired with at least one “hard” indicator (spend velocity OR sustained tokens OR repeated 429s) to route as a task.
   - Exception: security/compliance intent (SOC2/security FAQ/VPC docs) can route without usage if the account is paid.
2) Increase “sustained usage” cut line for NA pilot from 3-day avg ≥ 2.5M tokens/day → 4.0M tokens/day (keeps volume manageable).
3) 429s: require ≥ 3 distinct hours with 429s in a 48h window (vs current ≥2) to reduce one-off rate limit spikes.
4) Add a 7-day re-notification suppression per company per signal_type unless severity increases (to avoid multiple tasks for the same underlying issue).

Asks
- Stephanie: does the meeting rate look directionally right vs your team capacity? If we ship the above changes, we project ~20–25 routed tasks/week (down from 41).
- Camila: OK if we add a 72h marketing suppression window for accounts that get an AE task created?
- Nikhil/David: can we implement the gating + new cut lines by EOD Thu so it affects next Monday’s routing?

Details in the attached 1-pager + the pilot cohort sheet is updated.

Ben
Ben Carter
Growth, Redwood Inference


---

From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 9:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

This is helpful — thanks.

On capacity: 41 tasks/week was a little hot given NA team load. The 20–25/week target feels right for a pilot (esp if we’re prioritizing higher-quality).

AE feedback (from Avery + 2 others):
- The best ones were “growth + pain” (spend velocity + 429s / latency complaints). Those should keep going.
- The weakest ones were “they read docs” with no context — it reads like we’re watching them too closely. So I strongly support your intent-only gating.

One request: can we make the task titles more explicit about why it routed? Some were just “PLG signal: intent” and the AE had to click around.

Stephanie


---

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 9:28 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

+1 to gating intent-only. Marketing has the same concern — we should avoid “vanity intent” driving human outreach.

Re: suppression, yes we can do a 72h pause on lifecycle emails for accounts with an AE task created, but we need the signal to flow into HubSpot in a way the workflow can check reliably.

David — can you confirm we have a single property we can key off of (e.g., last_plg_signal_task_created_at) and that it’s consistently populated?

Also re SUP-3392: if we’re changing routing rules, I’d like a short customer-facing line we can use if it comes up again (“we reached out based on service usage patterns; happy to pause and we can disable proactive outreach”). I can draft, but want Legal/CS aligned.

Camila


---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 10:11 AM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

Good early signal.

Two things I want us to be crisp on:
1) Quality bar: we should optimize for customer trust first, pipeline second. I’m comfortable trading volume for fewer false positives.
2) Measurement: can we add a weekly breakdown of meeting-booked rate by signal type (reliability vs scale vs security/compliance intent vs team expansion)? That will make the iteration loop faster.

Also, for the 6 meetings booked — any early read on whether these are trending Dedicated vs Private? Even directional is fine.

Jordan


---

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, May 27, 2025 at 12:03 PM PT
Subject: Re: Week 1 pilot results (PLG→Sales signals) + proposed threshold tweaks

Agree on quality > volume.

Answers + next steps:

Meeting-booked by signal type (Week 1)
- Reliability (429s / elevated error rate + growth): 4 meetings booked (highest)
- Scale-only (sustained tokens/spend velocity without errors): 1 meeting booked
- Security/compliance intent: 1 meeting booked (paid account; asked for SOC2 + audit logs)
- Team expansion (new workspaces/invite spikes): 0 meetings booked (AEs said it’s better suited to CS/PLG nurture unless paired with spend growth)

Dedicated vs Private (directional)
- Dedicated-leaning: 4 (performance/throughput, predictable capacity)
- Private-leaning: 1 (data residency + retention controls)
- TBD: 1 (early discovery)

Task title / clarity (Stephanie’s point)
- We can update the task template to include the top 2 contributing signals + the window, e.g. “PLG: 429s (3 hrs/48h) + spend +62% WoW”

Marketing suppression (Camila)
- David/Nikhil: if we can populate one canonical property (e.g., plg_last_task_created_at) on company, Camila can key the 72h pause workflow off that. Otherwise we can use task creation itself, but it’s less deterministic.

Implementation plan (proposed)
- Ship intent-only gating + increased thresholds by Thu EOD
- Add 7-day re-notification suppression by next Monday (unless severity increases)
- Add weekly “signal→meeting by type” table to the pilot update starting next Tuesday

CS alignment
- Jasmine: for the team-expansion signal type, we should likely route as a CS heads-up (existing customers) or keep as a non-task note (new self-serve) until we have better qualification.

I’ll update the working notes + ping Nikhil with the concrete thresholds.

Ben
