- Security/compliance intent: 1 meeting booked (paid account; asked for SOC2 + audit logs)
- Team expansion (new workspaces/invite spikes): 0 meetings booked (AEs said it’s better suited to CS/PLG nurture unless paired with spend growth)

Dedicated vs Private (directional)
- Dedicated-leaning: 4 (performance/throughput, predictable capacity)
- Private-leaning: 1 (data residency + retention controls)
- TBD: 1 (early discovery)

Task title / clarity (Stephanie’s point)
- We can update the task template to include the top 2 contributing signals + the window, e.g. “PLG: 429s (3 hrs/48h) + spend +62% WoW”

Marketing suppression (Camila)
- David/Nikhil: if we can populate one canonical property (e.g., plg_last_task_created_at) on company, Camila can key the 72h pause workflow off that. Otherwise we can use task creation itself, but it’s less deterministic.

Implementation plan (proposed)
- Ship intent-only gating + increased thresholds by Thu EOD
- Add 7-day re-notification suppression by next Monday (unless severity increases)
- Add weekly “signal→meeting by type” table to the pilot update starting next Tuesday

CS alignment
- Jasmine: for the team-expansion signal type, we should likely route as a CS heads-up (existing customers) or keep as a non-task note (new self-serve) until we have better qualification.

I’ll update the working notes + ping Nikhil with the concrete thresholds.

Ben
links: HubSpot view: PLG Signals - NA Pilot Queue

source.subject: Third-party claim escalation, SLA exceptions & MSA redline coordination
messages:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@datapulse.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@datapulse.com
Date: 2025-05-18T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (attachments)

Ben —

We received a notice from a third party alleging patent infringement tied to outputs generated during a customer proof-of-concept. Our legal team would like to tighten the indemnity and notice language in the MSA/order form before we sign the final PO. Key asks from our side:

- Defense control: we prefer vendor (you) to defend, but only for claims that clearly relate to Redwood's model outputs rather than our data; otherwise we want a carve-out.
- Notice and cure windows: 15 days isn't enough; proposing 30 days for us to respond and supply evidence.
- Evidence preservation: we need a defined evidence request list and contact for triage.
- Cap/insurance: can you confirm insurance offsets and whether a cap will be relative to the order form value?

I attached a redline of your MSA (DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf) with the customer's proposed language. We need a response this week so procurement can finalize the PO.

Can you confirm who on your side will own the technical evidence collection and the escalation path?

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Counsel, DataPulse
elena.ruiz@datapulse.com

Attachments: DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf, evidence_packet_summary.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-18T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (help needed)

Team — forwarding DataPulse counsel's note. Summary of their asks below and files attached. We need legal + sales aligned before sending a formal redline back. Procurement is threatening to hold PO if we don't respond by Friday.

Customer asks:
- Limit defense control to claims "clearly related" to Redwood outputs; otherwise carve out
- Extend notice/cure to 30 days
- Confirm insurance offsets and whether cap tied to order form value
- Provide evidence request list and contact for triage

Immediate asks for Kimberly: can you propose a compromise that minimizes long-term liability but keeps the deal moving?
For Jordan: what's the deal economics — would we accept a cap equal to one year of committed revenue, or do we need a higher threshold?
Grace: can you own ops/engineer evidence liaison if we agree to any defense-control language that requires technical handover?

Attachments: DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf, evidence_packet_summary.pdf

-Ben

----- Forwarded message -----
From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@datapulse.com>
Date: Sun, May 18, 2025 at 9:03 AM
Subject: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (attachments)
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@datapulse.com

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-19T11:40:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (legal proposal)

Ben / Jordan / Grace —

Thanks for the heads up. I reviewed DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf and the customer's comments. Below is a proposed position that balances legal risk and commercial momentum.

High-level compromise (legal):
1) Defense control: Offer a hybrid approach — Redwood retains initial defense control only for third-party claims arising directly from model outputs that are not traceable to the customer's proprietary prompt/data stream. For claims where customer data or customer-provided models are the likely cause, we propose a pass-through requiring customer to assume defense. This avoids us taking on open-ended coverage for customer data issues.

2) Notice/cure window: Accept 30 days for non-emergency evidence collection, but retain a 5-business-day "notification of material escalation" requirement so we can trigger retention holds. Put explicit steps for evidence request (list of artifacts) and a named ops contact for technical handover.

3) Insurance / offsets & cap: Propose cap = greater of (a) total fees paid in prior 12 months under the order form, or (b) $250k, with insurance offset language preserved but limited so that we don't get double-reduced below a reasonable threshold. If DataPulse needs a lower cap, escalate to me for exec review.

4) Cooperation: Add mutual cooperation clause with specific timelines for document production (e.g., 7 business days for logs, 10 business days for models / checkpoints where feasible), and privilege carve-out language for privileged counsel communications.

5) Evidence custody: Grace — we will need an on-call evidence custodian (ops engineer) to accept secure artifact transfer if this proceeds. Can you confirm availability and an intake process?

I attached a tracked redline with these changes (orderform-amendment-draft.docx). If Sales accepts the cap proposal, legal is comfortable sending this back as a counter to the customer's redline. If Jordan says the cap kills the economics, tell me the threshold and I will prepare an exec memo for a limited exception.

-Kimberly

Attachments: orderform-amendment-draft.docx

On Sun, May 18, 2025 at 10:15 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — forwarding DataPulse counsel's note. Summary of their asks below and files attached. We need legal + sales aligned before sending a formal redline back. Procurement is threatening to hold PO if we don't respond by Friday.
>
> -Ben

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@datapulse.com>, contracts@datapulse.com
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-05-20T16:32:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (proposed compromise)

Elena —

Thanks for raising this and for the redline. Kimberly and I discussed and we've prepared a compromise we think addresses both parties' concerns while keeping the PO on track. Summary of our position (see attached counter-redline):

- Defense control: Redwood will accept defense for claims that independent technical analysis shows arise from Redwood model outputs (not originating from DataPulse proprietary inputs). For claims plausibly attributable to DataPulse inputs or configurations, DataPulse will retain responsibility. We will include an escalation step to neutral technical review in the event of a dispute.
- Notice & cure: We can accept a 30-day notice/cure window for the customer's remediation, but require an immediate material escalation notice (5 business days) to trigger retention of evidence.
- Cap and insurance offsets: Proposed cap = greater of prior 12 months' fees or $250k. Insurance offsets will apply but not below a minimum residual recovery of $50k. If DataPulse wants a lower cap tied strictly to the single order value, we'll need executive approval (Jordan — are you able to sign off or should I route?).
- Evidence handover: Grace will be the ops evidence custodian. Technical intake will follow the checklist in the attached doc.

Next steps:
1) Review attached counter-redline (orderform-amendment-draft.docx with tracked changes).
2) If acceptable, sign the amendment via DocuSign: https://docusign.net/link/orderform-amend-DP-0412
3) If DataPulse requests a lower cap, ask for business justification and Jordan please confirm whether revenue impact allows escalation without delaying the PO.

We tried to be constructive here — want to keep procurement moving and avoid an overbroad defense obligation on Redwood.

-Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: orderform-amendment-draft.docx, DP_redline_MSA_v3.pdf

To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: contracts@datapulse.com
Subject: Urgent: Third-party patent assertion & MSA redline (attachments)

source.subject: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)
related_links: PM-496 (pilot plan NA self-serve to sales handoff)
source.body:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, May 10, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)

Team —

As we turn on the PLG signal routing pilot, we need to avoid the “double tap” where Marketing sends a nurture email the same day an AE is reaching out (or vice versa). We already saw this in smaller tests and it reads spammy.

Proposal for pilot (NA cohort):
1) If a company has a qualified PLG signal routed + a HubSpot sales task created, suppress lifecycle/nurture sends for that company for X days.
2) Keep messaging consistent: Marketing copy should point to the same next step as Sales (Dedicated eval / security review) vs generic “learn more.”
3) Add a lightweight “recent touches” property so AEs can see if we already emailed in the last 72 hours.

Can we align on:
- Suppression window duration (7? 10? 14 days)
- Which programs to suppress (all lifecycle vs just “scale/reliability/compliance” tracks)
- How to handle the inverse case: marketing email already went out, then signal fires.

Tyler/Isabelle — assuming this is mostly HubSpot workflow logic + lists, but want your read before we bake it into the spec.

Camila
—
Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, May 10, 2025 at 9:46 AM PT
Subject: Re: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)

+1. AEs are already complaining about mixed messaging when a customer gets:
- “Congrats on scaling!” from Marketing
- “Do you want to talk to sales?” from us
within the same 24 hours.

My preference:
- Suppress marketing sends for 10 days once an AE task is created from a PLG signal (company-level).
- But DO NOT suppress product/system comms (billing receipts, incident comms, etc.).

Inverse case: if marketing already sent within last 3 days and then signal fires, we still want the AE task but it should say “marketing touched on <date>” so the AE can reference it instead of starting cold.

Stephanie


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, May 10, 2025 at 10:21 AM PT
Subject: Re: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)

Agree with 10 days for the pilot. Suggest we do it by “touch type” so we can tune without breaking everything:

Suppression rules (draft):
A) When PLG signal is “qualified” AND HubSpot task created => suppress marketing lifecycle emails tagged:
- plg_scale
- plg_reliability
- plg_security
for 10 days.

title: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tyler Benson <tyler.benson@redwoodinference.com>, Isabelle Laurent <isabelle.laurent@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, May 10, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)

Team —

As we turn on the PLG signal routing pilot, we need to avoid the “double tap” where Marketing sends a nurture email the same day an AE is reaching out (or vice versa). We already saw this in smaller tests and it reads spammy.

Proposal for pilot (NA cohort):
1) If a company has a qualified PLG signal routed + a HubSpot sales task created, suppress lifecycle/nurture sends for that company for X days.
2) Keep messaging consistent: Marketing copy should point to the same next step as Sales (Dedicated eval / security review) vs generic “learn more.”
3) Add a lightweight “recent touches” property so AEs can see if we already emailed in the last 72 hours.

Can we align on:
- Suppression window duration (7? 10? 14 days)
- Which programs to suppress (all lifecycle vs just “scale/reliability/compliance” tracks)
- How to handle the inverse case: marketing email already went out, then signal fires.

Tyler/Isabelle — assuming this is mostly HubSpot workflow logic + lists, but want your read before we bake it into the spec.

Camila
—
Camila Reyes
VP Marketing, Redwood Inference


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>

Date: Sat, May 10, 2025 at 9:46 AM PT
Subject: Re: Signal-based lifecycle campaign alignment (avoid duplicate touches w/ AE outreach)

+1. AEs are already complaining about mixed messaging when a customer gets:
- “Congrats on scaling!” from Marketing
- “Do you want to talk to sales?” from us
within the same 24 hours.

My preference:
- Suppress marketing sends for 10 days once an AE task is created from a PLG signal (company-level).
- But DO NOT suppress product/system comms (billing receipts, incident comms, etc.).

Inverse case: if marketing already sent within last 3 days and then signal fires, we still want the AE task but it should say “marketing touched on <date>” so the AE can reference it instead of starting cold.

Stephanie

“Recent marketing touch: <campaign name> on <date>. Consider reply/forward to continue thread.”

C) If AE logs outcome = “Not a fit / asked to stop outreach” => suppress those tags for 30 days.

Open question: do we want suppression at contact-level or company-level? I’d vote company-level to keep it simple + avoid edge cases where multiple contacts get hit.

Ben

source.subject: Temporary admin access: post-access review & SDLC evidence bundle
messages:
From: Alice Long <alice.long@acmebank.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: FinTrust Audit <fintrust-audit@fintrust.com>
Date: 2024-10-18 13:02:00 -0700
Subject: Request: evidence for temporary admin access (Sep 30)

Hi Evelyn,

Thanks again for taking this on. As part of Acme Bank's SIG/SOC review the assessor has one outstanding item: a complete package documenting the temporary admin access granted to engineer j.singh@acmebank on 2024-09-30 for the hotfix. They need:
- approval trail for the access (change request + approvers)
- list of CI/CD jobs and logs that used the elevated credentials
- code review PRs tied to the hotfix and merge timestamps
- secrets/access retirement evidence (key rotation / vault audit)
- a short attestation that access was time-limited and solely for the hotfix

Can you confirm you can provide those artifacts and a short timeline by EOD? I've attached the assessor's request PDF.

Attachment: temp_access_request.pdf (pdf)

Best,
Alice
Alice Long
Senior Auditor, Acme Bank
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Alice Long <alice.long@acmebank.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Date: 2024-10-18 14:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: evidence for temporary admin access (Sep 30)

Alice — thanks for the clear checklist. Jordan and Irene, looping you in for artifacts.

Action plan (quick):
- We will assemble a single evidence bundle with change approval, PR chronology, CI logs, and vault rotation entries.
- Jordan: collect PR + CI job logs and annotate which jobs used the elevated credential.
- Irene: pull vault audit entries and HSM rotation records; draft the attestation text.
- I will package and deliver a signed bundle to the Drive link and email Alice a zip + index.

Target: initial bundle to Alice by 2024-10-18 17:30 PT. If anything blocks, flag immediately.

Attaching: temp_admin_evidence_bundle_v1.zip (attachment stub - index inside).

Thanks —
Evelyn Hart
Head of Compliance Programs
Redwood Inference
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2024-10-18 15:05:12 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: evidence for temporary admin access (Sep 30)

Adding the technical artifacts I collected. Summary first: the hotfix PR was created 2024-09-29 22:18 UTC, merged 2024-09-30 01:07 UTC; deployment pipeline ran 2024-09-30 01:12 UTC. The temporary access request was approved via our change system (CR-4987) and the access window opened 2024-09-30 00:45 UTC and closed 2024-09-30 04:00 UTC (enforced by ephemeral session tokens).

Attached files:
- pr-1234-chronology.csv (CSV) — PR diffs, reviewers, timestamps
- ci-job-logs-20240930.log (text) — annotated with which step used the privileged credential (line markers)

Quick notes:
- CI job names: deploy-hotfix-prod; deploy-hotfix-prod used integration credential redwood-infra-temp-20240930 (ephemeral)
- We toggled the job to only run with one approver because of emergency policy; approvals are in CR-4987.

If the assessor wants additional annotations (e.g., exact SHA links to artifacts), say the word and I will add them to the index.

Jordan
Senior Site Reliability Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2024-10-18 16:00:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: evidence for temporary admin access (Sep 30)

Evelyn/Jordan — attaching an attestation draft and listing the vault/HSM evidence we can provide.

Vault/HSM artifacts available:
- K/V audit lines for redwood-infra-temp-20240930 (shows create/consume/delete events)
- Key rotation event: HSM rotation job HSM-rot-20241001 (rotation occurred 2024-10-01 03:05 UTC)
- Ephemeral-token issuance logs (stamped with session IDs)

I've drafted a short attestation text you can sign. It covers the basis of the emergency approval, the limited scope, and confirmation of key rotation within 24 hours. Please review the wording in the attached doc: attestation-draft.docx.

Attachment: attestation-draft.docx (docx)

I also copied Kimberly since billing/finance wants to confirm there were no billable infra surprises.

Irene Choi
Security & Compliance Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Alice Long <alice.long@acmebank.com>, FinTrust Audit <fintrust-audit@fintrust.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2024-10-18 17:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: evidence for temporary admin access (Sep 30)

Alice — delivering the requested evidence bundle and a short attestation. We included an index in the root of the zip with file checksums and a short readme that ties each artifact to the assessor's checklist items.

Delivered items (in Drive and attached zip):
- CR-4987_change_request.pdf (approval trail, approvers: Evelyn Hart, Marcus Lin)
- pr-1234-chronology.csv (PR & merge times)
- ci-job-logs-20240930.log (annotated; deploy-hotfix-prod clearly marked)
- vault-audit-redwood-infra-temp-20240930.csv (K/V create/read/delete)
- hsm-rotation-hist-20241001.csv (key rotation job entry)
- attestation-signed.pdf (signed by Evelyn Hart)
- index.md (mapping to assessor checklist)

Attachment: temp_admin_evidence_bundle_v1.zip (zip)

Evelyn — attestation-signed.pdf is in the bundle; I signed on Evelyn's behalf per the delegated sign-off matrix (Evelyn approved in thread at 14:10 PT). If Acme requires Evelyn's original signature, let me know and we will provide the scanned doc with audit header.

Alice, FinTrust: please confirm receipt and whether you need any additional granular logs (e.g., exact session IDs with IPs). We can redact IPs if required for privacy.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Director, Finance Operations (on record for evidence delivery)
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Temporary hosting subprocessor notice — DPA action & next steps
messages:
From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: EdgeBridge Ops <ops@edgebridge.com>; Elena Varga <elena@edgebridge.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Jun 2024 08:17:00 -0700
Subject: Temporary hosting subprocessor notice — DPA action & next steps

Hello Elena / EdgeBridge Ops,

Quick heads up: we plan to add EdgeBridge as a temporary hosting subprocessor for telemetry relay in the eu-west-1 region to support a short-term pilot for a European customer. The window would be approximately 6 weeks starting late June.

Required from your side for our DPA/compliance packet:
- Signed DPA addendum (we have a template; we can push via DocuSign)
- Updated dataflow diagram showing EU-only routing and egress points (PNG/PDF)
- Confirmation of encryption-in-transit and at-rest keys used for this job; KMS jurisdiction details
- Any SOC2 / security attestations you can share (or an expected delivery date)

Attachments: edgebridge_dpa_addendum_v1.pdf (template), dataflow_diagram_v2.png (current draft)

Can you confirm feasibility and an estimated date when you can provide the items above? We need the artifacts by EOD Wed Jun 19 to align with customer comms and procurement gating.

Thanks — Olga
Olga Petrov
Privacy & Compliance Manager, Redwood Inference
olga.petrov@redwood.com
---
Note: This is a notification to downstream teams; internal ops/procurement will be looped in once we have their attestation.

From: Elena Varga <elena@edgebridge.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>; EdgeBridge Ops <ops@edgebridge.com>
Date: Wed, 12 Jun 2024 11:09:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Temporary hosting subprocessor notice — DPA action & next steps

Hi Olga,

Thanks for the heads up — we can support a 6-week pilot for eu-west-1. Quick answers up front:
- DPA addendum: we can sign the addendum; happy to receive DocuSign.
- Dataflow diagram: attaching an updated sketch and we will refine per your comments.
- KMS: we use KeyVault EU region for this job; will provide the key policy excerpt.
- SOC2: snapshot available; full report needs an NDA but we can provide a redacted summary this week.

I attached a quick Ops note (EdgeBridge-PSA.pdf) with throughput and required IP allowlists. We need to confirm expected peak RPS and retention TTL for telemetry to size the routing.

Proposed timeline:
- Jun 14: send refined dataflow + KMS excerpt
- Jun 17: return signed DPA
- Jun 18–19: final operational checklist and access allowlist

If Nathan from FinRidge wants to join the call, happy to schedule next week.

Best,
Elena
Head of Customer Integrations, EdgeBridge
elena@edgebridge.com

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>; Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: EdgeBridge Ops <ops@edgebridge.com>; Elena Varga <elena@edgebridge.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Jun 2024 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Temporary hosting subprocessor notice — DPA action & next steps

On Wed, Jun 12, 2024 at 8:17 AM Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick heads up: we plan to add EdgeBridge as a temporary hosting subprocessor for telemetry relay in the eu-west-1 region to support a short-term pilot for a European customer.

Thanks Olga — looping procurement and operations to confirm gating. A few follow-ups from security/legal:
- We need a documented onshore fallback plan for EU customer traffic if EdgeBridge becomes unavailable (explicit steps + contact).
- KMS jurisdiction confirmation is required; if KeyVault is EU-only and keys never leave region, flag as EU-resident. If any key escrow is outside EU, we need to escalate.
- Please push the DPA addendum via DocuSign and include the redlined clause for temporary subcontracting (duration-limited).

Marissa/Jordan: can you confirm if procurement needs a PO change or if this can be processed under the existing master services agreement? Also confirm if CS should pre-notify the customer of the temporary routing.

-Kim
Kimberly Park
Sr. Security Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.com>
To: EdgeBridge Ops <ops@edgebridge.com>; Elena Varga <elena@edgebridge.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>; Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Jun 2024 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Temporary hosting subprocessor notice — DPA action & next steps

Elena / EdgeBridge — thank you for the quick responses and the sketch.

Summary / agreed next steps:
1) EdgeBridge to provide updated dataflow + KMS excerpt by Jun 14 (received) — thank you.
2) EdgeBridge to return signed DPA addendum via DocuSign by Jun 17. DocuSign link: https://app.docusign.com/prepare/ABCDEF123456
3) Security ask: onshore fallback doc due Jun 18 — Kim will review and sign off.
4) Ops: Marissa will confirm IP allowlist window and peak RPS expected; Jordan will confirm procurement path (PO vs MSA amendment).

We will keep FinRidge / customer-facing comms conservative until the addendum is signed and KMS residency is confirmed. If anything slips, notify privacy@redwood.com immediately.

Olga
Privacy & Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: edgebridge_dpa_addendum_v1.pdf, dataflow_diagram_v2.png
